text.NABRE: The cherubim lifted their wings and rose up from the earth before my eyes as they departed with the wheels beside them. They stopped at the entrance of the eastern gate of the LORD’s house, and the glory of the God of Israel was up above them.
text.DRB: And the Cherubs lifting up their wings, were exalted from the earth before me: and they going forth, the wheels also followed: & it stood in the entry of the east gate of the house of our Lord: and the glory of the God of Israel was over them.

text.NRSV: These were the living creatures that I saw underneath the God of Israel by the river Chebar; and I knew that they were cherubim.
text.Rotherham: The same, was the living one which I saw under the God of Israel by the river Chebar,- then knew I that cherubim, they were.
text.KJV: This is the living creature that I saw under the God of Israel by the river of Chebar; and I knew that they were the cherubims.
text.NABRE: These were the living creatures I had seen beneath the God of Israel by the river Chebar. Now I knew they were cherubim.
text.DRB: The same is the living creature, which I saw under the God of Israel by the river Chobar: an I understood that they were Cherubs.

text.NRSV: Each had four faces, each four wings, and underneath their wings something like human hands.
text.Rotherham: Four faces severally, had each one, and four wings, had each one,—and the likeness of the hands of a man, was under their wings.
text.KJV: Every one had four faces apiece, and every one four wings; and the likeness of the hands of a man was under their wings.
text.NABRE: Each of them had four faces and four wings, and something like human hands under their wings.
text.DRB: Four faces to one, and four wings to one: and the similitude of a man's hand under their wings.

text.NRSV: As for what their faces were like, they were the same faces whose appearance I had seen by the river Chebar. Each one moved straight ahead.
text.Rotherham: And as for the likeness of their faces, They were the faces which I saw by the river Chebar, their appearances and themselves— every one straight before him, did they go.
text.KJV: And the likeness of their faces was the same faces which I saw by the river of Chebar, their appearances and themselves: they went every one straight forward.
text.NABRE: Their faces looked just like the faces I had seen by the river Chebar; and each one went straight ahead.
text.DRB: And the similitude of their faces, the same faces, which I had seen by the river Chobar, and the looks of them, and the sway of every one to go before his face.

text.NRSV: The spirit lifted me up and brought me to the east gate of the house of the Lord, which faces east. There, at the entrance of the gateway, were twenty-five men; among them I saw Jaazaniah son of Azzur, and Pelatiah son of Benaiah, officials of the people.
text.Rotherham: And the Spirit lifted me up, and brought me into the east gate of the house of Yahweh which looketh eastward, and lo! in the opening of the gate, twenty-five men,—and I saw in their midst Jaazaniah son of Azzur and Pelatiah son of Benaiah princes of the people.
text.KJV: Moreover the spirit lifted me up, and brought me unto the east gate of the Lord’s house, which looketh eastward: and behold at the door of the gate five and twenty men; among whom I saw Jaazaniah the son of Azur, and Pelatiah the son of Benaiah, princes of the people.
text.NABRE: The spirit lifted me up and brought me to the east gate of the house of the Lord facing east. There at the entrance of the gate were twenty-five men; among them I saw the public officials Jaazaniah, son of Azzur, and Pelatiah, son of Benaiah.
text.DRB: And the spirit lifted me up, and brought me into the east gate of the house of our Lord, which looketh to the rising of the sun: and behold in the entry of the gate five and twenty men: and I saw in the midst of them Jezonias the son of Azur, & Pheltias the son of Banaias, the Princes of the people.

text.NRSV: He said to me, “Mortal, these are the men who devise iniquity and who give wicked counsel in this city;
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me, Son of man, These, are the men who are devising iniquity, and who are counselling wicked counsel in this city;
text.KJV: Then said he unto me, Son of man, these are the men that devise mischief, and give wicked counsel in this city:
text.NABRE: The Lord said to me: Son of man, these are the men who are planning evil and giving wicked counsel in this city.
text.DRB: And he said to me: Son of man, these are the men that conceive iniquity, and devise most wicked counsel in this city,

text.NRSV: they say, ‘The time is not near to build houses; this city is the pot, and we are the meat.’
text.Rotherham: who are saying, Not near! let us build houses,— It, is the caldron, and, we, are the flesh!
text.KJV: Which say, It is not near; let us build houses: this city is the caldron, and we be the flesh.
text.NABRE: They are saying, “No need to build houses! The city is the pot, and we are the meat.”
text.DRB: saying: Were not houses builded of late? This is the cauldron, & we the flesh.

text.NRSV: Therefore prophesy against them; prophesy, O mortal.”
text.Rotherham: Therefore, prophesy against them, —prophesy. Son of man.
text.KJV: Therefore prophesy against them, prophesy, O son of man.
text.NABRE: Therefore prophesy against them, son of man, prophesy!
text.DRB: Therefore prophesy of them, prophesy thou son of man.

text.NRSV: Then the spirit of the Lord fell upon me, and he said to me, “Say, Thus says the Lord: This is what you think, O house of Israel; I know the things that come into your mind.
text.Rotherham: Then fell upon me the Spirit of Yahweh, and he said unto me Say—Thus, saith Yahweh, Thus have ye said O house of Israel, Yea, the things that come up on your spirit, I, know every one.
text.KJV: And the Spirit of the Lord fell upon me, and said unto me, Speak; Thus saith the Lord; Thus have ye said, O house of Israel: for I know the things that come into your mind, every one of them.
text.NABRE: Then the spirit of the Lord fell upon me and told me to say: Thus says the Lord: This is how you talk, house of Israel. I know the things that come into your mind!
text.DRB: And the spirit of our Lord fell upon me, & said to me: Speak: Thus saith our Lord: So have you spoken, o house of Israel, & the cogitations of your heart I know.

text.NRSV: You have killed many in this city, and have filled its streets with the slain.
text.Rotherham: Ye have multiplied your slain in this city,- And have filled her streets with slain.
text.KJV: Ye have multiplied your slain in this city, and ye have filled the streets thereof with the slain.
text.NABRE: You have slain many in this city, filled its streets with the slain.
text.DRB: Very many have you killed in this city, and you have filled the ways thereof with the slain.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God: The slain whom you have placed within it are the meat, and this city is the pot; but you shall be taken out of it.
text.Rotherham: Therefore Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Your slain. whom ye have laid in her midst, They are the flesh, and She is the caldron,—when ye are taken out of her midst.
text.KJV: Therefore thus saith the Lord God; Your slain whom ye have laid in the midst of it, they are the flesh, and this city is the caldron: but I will bring you forth out of the midst of it.
text.NABRE: Therefore thus says the Lord God: The slain whom you piled up in it, that is the meat, the pot is the city. But you I will bring out of it.
text.DRB: Therefore thus saith our Lord God: Your slain, whom you have laid in the midst thereof, these are the flesh, and this is the cauldron: and I will bring you out of the midst thereof.

text.NRSV: You have feared the sword; and I will bring the sword upon you, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: A sword, have ye feared,— And a sword, will I bring in upon you, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: Ye have feared the sword; and I will bring a sword upon you, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: You fear the sword—that sword I will bring upon you—oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: The sword you have feared, and the sword I will bring upon you, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: I will take you out of it and give you over to the hands of foreigners, and execute judgments upon you.
text.Rotherham: So will I take you forth out of her midst, and will deliver you into the hand of foreigners, —and will execute upon you judgments.
text.KJV: And I will bring you out of the midst thereof, and deliver you into the hands of strangers, and will execute judgments among you.
text.NABRE: I will bring you out of the city, hand you over to foreigners, and execute judgments against you.
text.DRB: And I will cast you out of the midst thereof, & I will give you into the hand of the enemies, & will do judgements in you.

text.NRSV: You shall fall by the sword; I will judge you at the border of Israel. And you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: By the sword, shall ye fall, Upon the boundary of Israel, will I judge you, —So shall ye know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: Ye shall fall by the sword; I will judge you in the border of Israel; and ye shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: By the sword you shall fall. At the borders of Israel I will judge you so that you will know that I am the Lord.
text.DRB: You shall fall by the sword: in the borders of Israel will I judge you, and you shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: This city shall not be your pot, and you shall not be the meat inside it; I will judge you at the border of Israel.
text.Rotherham: This city shall not serve you for a caldron, Neither shall, ye, serve in the midst thereof for flesh,—Near the boundary of Israel, will I judge you,
text.KJV: This city shall not be your caldron, neither shall ye be the flesh in the midst thereof; but I will judge you in the border of Israel:
text.NABRE: The city shall not be a pot for you, nor shall you be meat within it. At the borders of Israel I will judge you,
text.DRB: This shall not be as a cauldron to you, and you shall not be as flesh in the midst thereof, in the borders of Israel I will judge you.

text.NRSV: Then you shall know that I am the Lord, whose statutes you have not followed, and whose ordinances you have not kept, but you have acted according to the ordinances of the nations that are around you.”
text.Rotherham: So shall ye know that, I, am Yahweh. In whose statutes, ye have not walked And whose appointments, ye have not executed,— But according to the appointments of the nations that are round about you, have ye done.
text.KJV: And ye shall know that I am the Lord: for ye have not walked in my statutes, neither executed my judgments, but have done after the manners of the heathen that are round about you.
text.NABRE: so you shall know that I am the Lord, whose statutes you did not follow, whose ordinances you did not keep. Instead, you acted according to the ordinances of the nations around you.
text.DRB: And you shall know that I am the Lord: because you have not walked in my precepts, and have not done my judgements, but you have done according to the judgements of nations, that are round about you.

text.NRSV: Now, while I was prophesying, Pelatiah son of Benaiah died. Then I fell down on my face, cried with a loud voice, and said, “Ah Lord God! will you make a full end of the remnant of Israel?”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass as I prophesied, that Pelatiah son of Benaiah died,—so then I fell down upon my face and made outcry with a loud voice and said— Alas! My Lord Yahweh! A full end, art thou making of the remnant of Israel?
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when I prophesied, that Pelatiah the son of Benaiah died. Then fell I down upon my face, and cried with a loud voice, and said, Ah Lord God! wilt thou make a full end of the remnant of Israel?
text.NABRE: While I was prophesying, Pelatiah, the son of Benaiah, dropped dead. I fell down on my face and cried out in a loud voice: “Alas, Lord God! You are finishing off what remains of Israel!”
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when I prophesied, Pheltias the son of Banaias died: and I fell upon my face, crying with a loud voice: and said: Alas, alas, alas, o Lord God: makest thou a consummation of the remnant of Israel?

text.NRSV: Then the word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me saying:
text.KJV: Again the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: Restoration for the Exiles. The word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, your kinsfolk, your own kin, your fellow exiles, the whole house of Israel, all of them, are those of whom the inhabitants of Jerusalem have said, “They have gone far from the Lord; to us this land is given for a possession.”
text.Rotherham: Son of man Thine own brethren, thine own brethren the men of thy kindred, even all the house of Israel all of it are they to whom the inhabitants of Jerusalem have said, Get you far away from Yahweh, To us, it is that the land, hath been given, for a possession!
text.KJV: Son of man, thy brethren, even thy brethren, the men of thy kindred, and all the house of Israel wholly, are they unto whom the inhabitants of Jerusalem have said, Get you far from the Lord: unto us is this land given in possession.
text.NABRE: Son of man, the inhabitants of Jerusalem are saying about all your relatives, the other exiles, and all the house of Israel, “They are far away from the Lord. The land is given to us as a possession.”
text.DRB: Son of man thy brethren, thy brethren, thy kinsmen, and all the house of Israel, all to whom the inhabitants of Jerusalem have said: Depart ye far from our Lord, the land is given to us in possession.

text.NRSV: Therefore say: Thus says the Lord God: Though I removed them far away among the nations, and though I scattered them among the countries, yet I have been a sanctuary to them for a little while in the countries where they have gone.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, say, Thus saith My Lord. Yahweh, Although I have removed them far away among the nations, And although I have dispersed them throughout the lands Yet have I become to them a sanctuary for a little while, in the lands which they have entered
text.KJV: Therefore say, Thus saith the Lord God; Although I have cast them far off among the heathen, and although I have scattered them among the countries, yet will I be to them as a little sanctuary in the countries where they shall come.
text.NABRE: Therefore say: Thus says the Lord God: I have indeed sent them far away among the nations, scattered them over the lands, and have been but little sanctuary for them in the lands to which they have gone.
text.DRB: Therefore thus saith our Lord God: Because I have made them far off in the Gentiles, and because I have dispersed them in the lands: I will be a little sanctification to them in the lands, to which they are come.

text.NRSV: Therefore say: Thus says the Lord God: I will gather you from the peoples, and assemble you out of the countries where you have been scattered, and I will give you the land of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Therefore say, Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, Therefore will I assemble you from among the peoples, And gather you out of the lands wherein ye have been dispersed,- And will give you the soil of Israel.
text.KJV: Therefore say, Thus saith the Lord God; I will even gather you from the people, and assemble you out of the countries where ye have been scattered, and I will give you the land of Israel.
text.NABRE: Therefore, thus says the Lord God, I will gather you from the nations and collect you from the lands through which you were scattered, so I can give you the land of Israel.
text.DRB: Therefore speak: Thus saith our Lord God: I will gather you out of the peoples, and unite you out of the lands, wherein you are dispersed, and I will give you the ground of Israel.

text.NRSV: When they come there, they will remove from it all its detestable things and all its abominations.
text.Rotherham: So shall they come in thither,— and take away all her detestable things, and all her abominations, out of her;
text.KJV: And they shall come thither, and they shall take away all the detestable things thereof and all the abominations thereof from thence.
text.NABRE: They will enter it and remove all its atrocities and abominations.
text.DRB: And they shall go in thither, and shall take away all the offences, and all the abominations thereof out of it.

text.NRSV: I will give them one heart, and put a new spirit within them; I will remove the heart of stone from their flesh and give them a heart of flesh,
text.Rotherham: And I will give them another heart, And a new spirit, will I put within you,—And will take away the heart of stone out of their flesh, and give them a heart of flesh:
text.KJV: And I will give them one heart, and I will put a new spirit within you; and I will take the stony heart out of their flesh, and will give them an heart of flesh:
text.NABRE: And I will give them another heart and a new spirit I will put within them. From their bodies I will remove the hearts of stone, and give them hearts of flesh,
text.DRB: And I will give them one heart, and will give a new spirit in their bowels: and I will take away the stony heart out of their flesh, and will give them a fleshy heart:

text.NRSV: so that they may follow my statutes and keep my ordinances and obey them. Then they shall be my people, and I will be their God.
text.Rotherham: To the end that in my statutes, they may walk, And mine appointments, they may keep, and do them,— So shall they become my people, And I will become their God.
text.KJV: That they may walk in my statutes, and keep mine ordinances, and do them: and they shall be my people, and I will be their God.
text.NABRE: so that they walk according to my statutes, taking care to keep my ordinances. Thus they will be my people, and I will be their God.
text.DRB: that they may walk in my precepts, and keep my judgements, and do them: and they may be my people, and I may be their God.

text.NRSV: But as for those whose heart goes after their detestable things and their abominations, I will bring their deeds upon their own heads, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: But as for them who having a heart for their detestable things and for their abominations after their own heart, do walk, Their way upon their own heads, will I render, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: But as for them whose heart walketh after the heart of their detestable things and their abominations, I will recompense their way upon their own heads, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: But as for those whose hearts are devoted to their atrocities and abominations, I will bring their conduct down upon their heads—oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: Whose heart walketh after their offences and abominations, their way will I lay on their head, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: Then the cherubim lifted up their wings, with the wheels beside them; and the glory of the God of Israel was above them.
text.Rotherham: Then did the cherubim lift up their wings, the wheels also going in unison with them,— the glory of the God of Israel, being over them above.
text.KJV: Then did the cherubims lift up their wings, and the wheels beside them; and the glory of the God of Israel was over them above.
text.NABRE: Then the cherubim lifted their wings and the wheels alongside them, with the glory of the God of Israel above them.
text.DRB: And the Cherubs lifted up their wings, and the wheels with them: and the glory of the God of Israel was over them.

text.NRSV: And the glory of the Lord ascended from the middle of the city, and stopped on the mountain east of the city.
text.Rotherham: Thus went up the glory of Yahweh, out from the midst of the city,—and stood on the mountain, which is on the east of the city.
text.KJV: And the glory of the Lord went up from the midst of the city, and stood upon the mountain which is on the east side of the city.
text.NABRE: The glory of the Lord rose up from the middle of the city and came to rest on the mountain east of the city.
text.DRB: And the glory of our Lord ascended from the midst of the city, and stood over the mount, that is on the east side of the city.

text.NRSV: The spirit lifted me up and brought me in a vision by the spirit of God into Chaldea, to the exiles. Then the vision that I had seen left me.
text.Rotherham: And, the spirit lifted me up, and brought me to Chaldea unto them of the captivity, in the vision by the Spirit of God,—thus went up from me, the vision which I had seen.
text.KJV: Afterwards the spirit took me up, and brought me in a vision by the Spirit of God into Chaldea, to them of the captivity. So the vision that I had seen went up from me.
text.NABRE: In a vision, the spirit lifted me up and brought me back to the exiles in Chaldea, by the spirit of God. The vision I had seen left me,
text.DRB: And the spirit lifted me up, and brought me into Chaldee to the transmigration, in a vision in the spirit of God: and the vision which I had seen was taken up from me.

text.NRSV: And I told the exiles all the things that the Lord had shown me.
text.Rotherham: So I spake unto them of the captivity,—all the words of Yahweh which he had shewed me.
text.KJV: Then I spake unto them of the captivity all the things that the Lord had shewed me.
text.NABRE: and I told the exiles everything the Lord had shown me.
text.DRB: And I spake to the transmigration all the words of our Lord, which he had shewed me.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me saying:
text.KJV: The word of the Lord also came unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, you are living in the midst of a rebellious house, who have eyes to see but do not see, who have ears to hear but do not hear;
text.Rotherham: Son of man. In the midst of a perverse house, dost thou dwell,— Who have eyes to see—and have not seen. Ears have they to hear—and have no heard, For a perverse house, they are.
text.KJV: Son of man, thou dwellest in the midst of a rebellious house, which have eyes to see, and see not; they have ears to hear, and hear not: for they are a rebellious house.
text.NABRE: Son of man, you live in the midst of a rebellious house; they have eyes to see, but do not see, and ears to hear but do not hear. They are such a rebellious house!
text.DRB: Son of man, thou dwellest in the midst of an exasperating house: which have eyes to see, and see not: and ears to hear, and hear not: because it is an exasperating house.

text.NRSV: for they are a rebellious house. Therefore, mortal, prepare for yourself an exile’s baggage, and go into exile by day in their sight; you shall go like an exile from your place to another place in their sight. Perhaps they will understand, though they are a rebellious house.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore Son of man, Prepare thee baggage for exile, and exile thyself, by day, before their eyes,—so shalt thou exile thyself out of thy place unto another place before their eyes, peradventure they will consider— though a perverse house, they are.
text.KJV: Therefore, thou son of man, prepare thee stuff for removing, and remove by day in their sight; and thou shalt remove from thy place to another place in their sight: it may be they will consider, though they be a rebellious house.
text.NABRE: Now, son of man, during the day while they watch, pack a bag for exile, and again while they watch, go into exile from your place to another place; perhaps they will see that they are a rebellious house.
text.DRB: Thou therefore, o son of man, make thee vessels of transmigration, and thou shalt flit by day before them: and thou shalt flit out of thy place to another place in their sight; if perhaps they will behold: because it is an exasperating house.

text.NRSV: You shall bring out your baggage by day in their sight, as baggage for exile; and you shall go out yourself at evening in their sight, as those do who go into exile.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shalt thou take forth thy baggage as baggage for exile, by day before their eyes,—and thou thyself, shalt go forth in the evening, before their eyes, like them who go forth to exile.
text.KJV: Then shalt thou bring forth thy stuff by day in their sight, as stuff for removing: and thou shalt go forth at even in their sight, as they that go forth into captivity.
text.NABRE: During the day, while they watch, bring out your bag, an exile’s bag. In the evening, again while they watch, go out as if into exile.
text.DRB: And thou shalt carry forth thy vessels as the vessels of one flitting by day in the sight of them: and thou shalt go forth at even before them, as one goeth forth that flitteth.

text.NRSV: Dig through the wall in their sight, and carry the baggage through it.
text.Rotherham: Before their eyes, break thou forth by thyself through the wall,—and carry forth through it.
text.KJV: Dig thou through the wall in their sight, and carry out thereby.
text.NABRE: While they watch, dig a hole through the wall and go out through it.
text.DRB: Before their eyes dig to thee through the wall: and thou shalt go forth through it.

text.NRSV: In their sight you shall lift the baggage on your shoulder, and carry it out in the dark; you shall cover your face, so that you may not see the land; for I have made you a sign for the house of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Before their eyes, Upon the shoulder, shalt thou lift it. In thick darkness, take it forth, Thy face, shalt thou cover so that thou see not the land; For a sign, have I appointed thee to the house of Israel.
text.KJV: In their sight shalt thou bear it upon thy shoulders, and carry it forth in the twilight: thou shalt cover thy face, that thou see not the ground: for I have set thee for a sign unto the house of Israel.
text.NABRE: While they watch, shoulder your load and go out in darkness. Cover your face so you cannot see the land, for I am making you a sign for the house of Israel!
text.DRB: In their sight on shoulders thou shalt be carried, in the dark thou shalt be carried out: thou shalt cover thy face, and shalt not see the earth: because I have given thee a portending sign to the house of Israel.

text.NRSV: I did just as I was commanded. I brought out my baggage by day, as baggage for exile, and in the evening I dug through the wall with my own hands; I brought it out in the dark, carrying it on my shoulder in their sight.
text.Rotherham: And I did so, just as I was commanded, My baggage, took I forth as baggage for exile by day, and in the evening, I brake forth by myself through the wall, by force: in the twilight, I took it forth—on to my shoulder, I lifted it, before their eyes.
text.KJV: And I did so as I was commanded: I brought forth my stuff by day, as stuff for captivity, and in the even I digged through the wall with mine hand; I brought it forth in the twilight, and I bare it upon my shoulder in their sight.
text.NABRE: I did just as I was commanded. During the day I brought out my bag, an exile’s bag. In the evening while they watched, I dug a hole through the wall with my hands and set out in darkness, shouldering my load.
text.DRB: I did therefore as he had commanded me: I carried my vessels as the vessels of one flitting by day: and at even I digged to me through the wall with my hand: and I went forth in the dark, and was carried on shoulders in their sight.

text.NRSV: In the morning the word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me in the morning, saying:
text.KJV: And in the morning came the word of the Lord unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: In the morning, the word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me in the morning, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, has not the house of Israel, the rebellious house, said to you, “What are you doing?”
text.Rotherham: Son of man, Have not the house of Israel the perverse house, said unto thee,— What art thou doing?
text.KJV: Son of man, hath not the house of Israel, the rebellious house, said unto thee, What doest thou?
text.NABRE: Son of man, did not the house of Israel, that house of rebels, say, “What are you doing?”
text.DRB: Son of man, hath not the house of Israel the exasperating house said to thee: What doest thou?

text.NRSV: Say to them, “Thus says the Lord God: This oracle concerns the prince in Jerusalem and all the house of Israel in it.”
text.Rotherham: Say unto them, Thus, saith My Lord, Yahweh,— [For] the Bearer, is this burden, in Jerusalem, and [for] all the house of Israel such as are in their midst.
text.KJV: Say thou unto them, Thus saith the Lord God; This burden concerneth the prince in Jerusalem, and all the house of Israel that are among them.
text.NABRE: Tell them: Thus says the Lord God: This load is the prince in Jerusalem and the whole house of Israel within it.
text.DRB: Say to them: Thus saith our Lord God: This burden upon the Prince, that is in Jerusalem, and upon all the house of Israel, which is in the midst of them.

text.NRSV: Say, “I am a sign for you: as I have done, so shall it be done to them; they shall go into exile, into captivity.”
text.Rotherham: Say, I, am your sign: As I have done, so. shall it be done to them, Into exile—into captivity, shall they go.
text.KJV: Say, I am your sign: like as I have done, so shall it be done unto them: they shall remove and go into captivity.
text.NABRE: Say, I am a sign for you: just as I have done, so it shall be done to them; into exile, as captives they shall go.
text.DRB: Say: I am your portending sign, as I have done, so shall it be done to them, into transmigration, and into captivity shall they go.

text.NRSV: And the prince who is among them shall lift his baggage on his shoulder in the dark, and shall go out; he shall dig through the wall and carry it through; he shall cover his face, so that he may not see the land with his eyes.
text.Rotherham: Yea, the Bearer who is in their midst, Upon his shoulder, shall lift it. In thick darkness, shall he go forth, Through the wall, shall they break to bear forth through it,— His face, shall he cover, to the end that, his own eye, may not see the land.
text.KJV: And the prince that is among them shall bear upon his shoulder in the twilight, and shall go forth: they shall dig through the wall to carry out thereby: he shall cover his face, that he see not the ground with his eyes.
text.NABRE: The prince among them shall shoulder his load in darkness and go out through the hole they dug in the wall to bring him out. His face shall be covered so that he cannot even see the ground.
text.DRB: And the Prince, that is in the midst of them, shall be carried on shoulders, he shall go forth in the dark: they shall dig through the wall to bring him out: his face shall be covered, that he may not see the earth with his eye.

text.NRSV: I will spread my net over him, and he shall be caught in my snare; and I will bring him to Babylon, the land of the Chaldeans, yet he shall not see it; and he shall die there.
text.Rotherham: Then will I spread my net over him, and he shall be taken in my snare; And I will take him to Babylon. in the land of the Chaldeans, The which, indeed, he shall not see. And yet there, shall he die!
text.KJV: My net also will I spread upon him, and he shall be taken in my snare: and I will bring him to Babylon to the land of the Chaldeans; yet shall he not see it, though he shall die there.
text.NABRE: I will spread my net over him and he shall be caught in my snare. I will bring him into Babylon, to the land of the Chaldeans, though he shall not see it, and there he shall die.
text.DRB: And I will stretch forth my net upon him, and he shall be taken in my net: and I will bring him into Babylon into the land of the Chaldees, and he shall not see it, and there he shall die.

text.NRSV: I will scatter to every wind all who are around him, his helpers and all his troops; and I will unsheathe the sword behind them.
text.Rotherham: And all who are round about him to help him and all his troops, will I scatter to every wind,—and, a sword, will I make bare after them.
text.KJV: And I will scatter toward every wind all that are about him to help him, and all his bands; and I will draw out the sword after them.
text.NABRE: All his retinue, his aides and all his troops, I will scatter to the winds and pursue them with the sword.
text.DRB: And all that are about him, his guard, and his troops I will disperse into every wind: and I will draw the sword after them.

text.NRSV: And they shall know that I am the Lord, when I disperse them among the nations and scatter them through the countries.
text.Rotherham: So shall they know that, I, am Yahweh,— By my dispersing them among the nations, And scattering them throughout the lands.
text.KJV: And they shall know that I am the Lord, when I shall scatter them among the nations, and disperse them in the countries.
text.NABRE: Then they shall know that I am the Lord, when I disperse them among the nations and scatter them throughout the lands.
text.DRB: And they shall know that I am the Lord, when I shall have dispersed them in the Gentiles, and scattered them in the lands.

text.NRSV: But I will let a few of them escape from the sword, from famine and pestilence, so that they may tell of all their abominations among the nations where they go; then they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Yet will I leave remaining of them men easily counted, from the sword from the famine and from the pestilence,—that they may recount all their abominations among the nations whither they have come, So shall they know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: But I will leave a few men of them from the sword, from the famine, and from the pestilence; that they may declare all their abominations among the heathen whither they come; and they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: But I will let a few of them escape the sword, starvation, and plague, so that they may recount all their abominations among the nations to which they go. Thus they may know that I am the Lord.
text.DRB: And I will leave of them a few men from the sword, and the famine, and the pestilence: that they may declare all their wicked deeds in the Gentiles, to which they shall enter: and they shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me, saying:
text.KJV: Moreover the word of the Lord came to me, saying,
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made unto me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, eat your bread with quaking, and drink your water with trembling and with fearfulness;
text.Rotherham: Son of man, Thy bread with trembling, shalt thou eat,— And, thy water in agitation and in fear, shalt thou drink.
text.KJV: Son of man, eat thy bread with quaking, and drink thy water with trembling and with carefulness;
text.NABRE: Son of man, eat your bread trembling and drink your water shaking with fear.
text.DRB: Son of man, eat thy bread in trouble: yea & drink thy water in haste and sorrowfulness.

text.NRSV: and say to the people of the land, Thus says the Lord God concerning the inhabitants of Jerusalem in the land of Israel: They shall eat their bread with fearfulness, and drink their water in dismay, because their land shall be stripped of all it contains, on account of the violence of all those who live in it.
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou say unto the people of the land, Thus saith My Lord Yahweh concerning the inhabitants of Jerusalem upon the soil of Israel, Their bread with anxious care, shall they eat, And, their water in astonishment, shall they drink,— That her land, may be deserted, of her fulness, because of the violence of all them who dwell therein:
text.KJV: And say unto the people of the land, Thus saith the Lord God of the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and of the land of Israel; They shall eat their bread with carefulness, and drink their water with astonishment, that her land may be desolate from all that is therein, because of the violence of all them that dwell therein.
text.NABRE: And say to the people of the land: Thus says the Lord God about the inhabitants of Jerusalem in the land of Israel: they shall eat their bread in fear and drink their water in horror, because the land will be emptied of what fills it—the lawlessness of all its inhabitants.
text.DRB: And thou shalt say to the people of the land: Thus saith our Lord God to them that dwell in Jerusalem in the land of Israel: Their bread they shall eat in carefulness, and their water in desolation they shall drink: that the land may be made desolate from the multitude thereof, for the iniquity of all that dwell therein.

text.NRSV: The inhabited cities shall be laid waste, and the land shall become a desolation; and you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Yea, the cities that are inhabited, shall be laid waste, And, the land, shall become, an astonishment,— So shall ye know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: And the cities that are inhabited shall be laid waste, and the land shall be desolate; and ye shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: Inhabited cities shall be in ruins, the land a desolate place. Then you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.DRB: And the cities, that are now inhabited, shall be desolate, and the land desert: and you shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me saying:
text.KJV: And the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: Prophecy Ridiculed. The word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, what is this proverb of yours about the land of Israel, which says, “The days are prolonged, and every vision comes to nothing”?
text.Rotherham: Son of man What is this proverb ye have, concerning the soil of Israel, saying,— The days, are prolonged, Therefore shall every vision come to nought?
text.KJV: Son of man, what is that proverb that ye have in the land of Israel, saying, The days are prolonged, and every vision faileth?
text.NABRE: Son of man, what is this proverb you have in the land of Israel: “The days drag on, and every vision fails”?
text.DRB: Son of man, what is this proverb with you in the land of Israel saying: The days shall be deferred to a long time, and all vision shall perish.

text.NRSV: Tell them therefore, “Thus says the Lord God: I will put an end to this proverb, and they shall use it no more as a proverb in Israel.” But say to them, The days are near, and the fulfillment of every vision.
text.Rotherham: Therefore say unto them, Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, I will cause this proverb to cease, And they shall use it as a proverb no more in Israel,— But speak unto them, The days, are drawn near, And the substance of every vision.
text.KJV: Tell them therefore, Thus saith the Lord God; I will make this proverb to cease, and they shall no more use it as a proverb in Israel; but say unto them, The days are at hand, and the effect of every vision.
text.NABRE: Say to them therefore: Thus says the Lord God: I will put an end to that proverb; they shall never use it again in Israel. Say to them instead: “The days are at hand and every vision fulfilled.”
text.DRB: Therefore say to them: Thus saith our Lord God: I will make this proverb cease, neither shall it any more be commonly said in Israel: and speak to them that the days are at hand, and the word of every vision.

text.NRSV: For there shall no longer be any false vision or flattering divination within the house of Israel.
text.Rotherham: For there shall no more be— Any vision of falsehood Or divination of deceit, In the midst of the house of Israel.
text.KJV: For there shall be no more any vain vision nor flattering divination within the house of Israel.
text.NABRE: No longer shall there be any false visions or deceitful divinations within the house of Israel,
text.DRB: for all vision shall no more be in vain, nor divination ambiguous in the midst of the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: But I the Lord will speak the word that I speak, and it will be fulfilled. It will no longer be delayed; but in your days, O rebellious house, I will speak the word and fulfill it, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: For, I, Yahweh will speak whatsoever word I please to speak, And it shall be done, It shall not be delayed any more,— For in your own days, O perverse house, will I speak a word and perform it, Declareth My Lord. Yahweh.
text.KJV: For I am the Lord: I will speak, and the word that I shall speak shall come to pass; it shall be no more prolonged: for in your days, O rebellious house, will I say the word, and will perform it, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: for whatever word I speak shall happen without delay. In your days, rebellious house, whatever I speak I will bring about—oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: Because I the Lord will speak: and what word soever I shall speak, shall also be done, it shall not be prolonged any more: but in your days ye exasperating house, will I speak the word, and will do it, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying:
text.KJV: Again the word of the Lord came to me, saying,
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, the house of Israel is saying, “The vision that he sees is for many years ahead; he prophesies for distant times.”
text.Rotherham: Son of man. Lo! the house of Israel are saying, the vision which he seeth is for many days, Yea for times far away, hath he prophesied.
text.KJV: Son of man, behold, they of the house of Israel say, The vision that he seeth is for many days to come, and he prophesieth of the times that are far off.
text.NABRE: Son of man, listen! The house of Israel is saying, “The vision he sees is a long time off; he prophesies for distant times!”
text.DRB: Son of man, behold the house of Israel, they that say: The vision, that this man seeth, is for many days: and this man Prophesieth long times.

text.NRSV: Therefore say to them, Thus says the Lord God: None of my words will be delayed any longer, but the word that I speak will be fulfilled, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: Therefore say unto them. Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, None of my words shall be delayed any more,— Whatsoever word I speak, then shall it be performed, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: Therefore say unto them, Thus saith the Lord God; There shall none of my words be prolonged any more, but the word which I have spoken shall be done, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: Say to them therefore: Thus says the Lord God: None of my words shall be delayed any longer. Whatever I say is final; it shall be done—oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: Therefore say to them: Thus saith our Lord God: Every word of mine shall be prolonged no further: the word that I shall speak, shall be accomplished, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying:
text.KJV: And the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, prophesy against the prophets of Israel who are prophesying; say to those who prophesy out of their own imagination: “Hear the word of the Lord!”
text.Rotherham: Son of man. prophesy against the prophets of Israel, who are prophesying,—and say unto them who prophesy out of their own heart, Hear ye the word of Yahweh,
text.KJV: Son of man, prophesy against the prophets of Israel that prophesy, and say thou unto them that prophesy out of their own hearts, Hear ye the word of the Lord;
text.NABRE: Son of man, prophesy against the prophets of Israel, prophesy! Say to those who prophesy their own thoughts: Hear the word of the Lord!
text.DRB: Son of man prophesy to the Prophets of Israel, that prophesy: and thou shalt say to them, that prophesy of their own heart: Hear ye the word of our Lord:

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God, Alas for the senseless prophets who follow their own spirit, and have seen nothing!
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith My Lord, Yahweh, Alas for the base prophets,—who follow their own spirit and have seen, nothing.
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord God; Woe unto the foolish prophets, that follow their own spirit, and have seen nothing!
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord God: Woe to those prophets, the fools who follow their own spirit and see nothing.
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord God: Woe to the foolish Prophets, which follow their own spirit, and see nothing.

text.NRSV: Your prophets have been like jackals among ruins, O Israel.
text.Rotherham: As jackals, among ruins, thy prophets O Israel have been.
text.KJV: O Israel, thy prophets are like the foxes in the deserts.
text.NABRE: Like foxes among ruins are your prophets, Israel!
text.DRB: As foxes in the deserts, were thy Prophets o Israel.

text.NRSV: You have not gone up into the breaches, or repaired a wall for the house of Israel, so that it might stand in battle on the day of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Ye have not gone up into the breaches, Nor built a wall about the house of Israel,- to stand in the battle in the day of Yahweh.
text.KJV: Ye have not gone up into the gaps, neither made up the hedge for the house of Israel to stand in the battle in the day of the Lord.
text.NABRE: You did not step into the breach, nor repair the wall around the house of Israel so it would stand firm against attack on the day of the Lord.
text.DRB: You have not ascended against, nor opposed a wall for the house of Israel, that you might stand in battle in the day of our Lord.

text.NRSV: They have envisioned falsehood and lying divination; they say, “Says the Lord,” when the Lord has not sent them, and yet they wait for the fulfillment of their word!
text.Rotherham: They have had Visions of falsehood, and Divinations of lies, Who say. Declareth Yahweh, when, Yahweh, hath not sent them,— Yet have they waited for a fulfilling of the word.
text.KJV: They have seen vanity and lying divination, saying, The Lord saith: and the Lord hath not sent them: and they have made others to hope that they would confirm the word.
text.NABRE: False visions! Lying divinations! They say, “The oracle of the Lord,” even though the Lord did not send them. Then they expect their word to be confirmed!
text.DRB: They see vain things, and they divine lies, saying: Our Lord saith: whereas our Lord sent them not: and they persevered to confirm the word.

text.NRSV: Have you not seen a false vision or uttered a lying divination, when you have said, “Says the Lord,” even though I did not speak?
text.Rotherham: Is it not a Vision of falsehood, ye have seen, and a Divination of lies, ye have spoken, Since ye are saying. Declareth Yahweh! when I have not spoken?
text.KJV: Have ye not seen a vain vision, and have ye not spoken a lying divination, whereas ye say, The Lord saith it; albeit I have not spoken?
text.NABRE: Was not the vision you saw false? Did you not report a lying divination when you said, “Oracle of the Lord,” even though I never spoke?
text.DRB: Why have you not seen vain vision, and spoken lying divination? And you say: Our Lord saith; whereas I have not spoken.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God: Because you have uttered falsehood and envisioned lies, I am against you, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: Therefore Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, Because ye have spoken falsehood, And have had visions of lies, Therefore behold me! against you, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: Therefore thus saith the Lord God; Because ye have spoken vanity, and seen lies, therefore, behold, I am against you, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: Therefore thus says the Lord God: Because you have spoken falsehood and seen lying visions, therefore, for certain I am coming at you—oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: Therefore thus saith our Lord God: Because you have spoken vain things, and have seen lies: therefore behold I to you, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: My hand will be against the prophets who see false visions and utter lying divinations; they shall not be in the council of my people, nor be enrolled in the register of the house of Israel, nor shall they enter the land of Israel; and you shall know that I am the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: So then my hand shall be against the prophets who have Visions of falsehood and who divine Divinations of lies— In the council of my people, shall they not be found. And in the register of the house of Israel, shall they not be written, And Upon the so of Israel, shall they not enter,— So shall ye know that, I, am The Lord Yahweh,
text.KJV: And mine hand shall be upon the prophets that see vanity, and that divine lies: they shall not be in the assembly of my people, neither shall they be written in the writing of the house of Israel, neither shall they enter into the land of Israel; and ye shall know that I am the Lord God.
text.NABRE: My hand is against the prophets who see false visions and who make lying divinations. They shall not belong to the community of my people. They shall not be written in the register of the house of Israel, nor shall they enter the land of Israel. Thus you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.DRB: And my hand shall be upon the Prophets, that see vain things, and divine a lie: in the counsel of my people they shall not be, & in the scripture of the house of Israel they shall not be written, neither shall they enter into the land of Israel: and you shall know that I am the Lord God.

text.NRSV: Because, in truth, because they have misled my people, saying, “Peace,” when there is no peace; and because, when the people build a wall, these prophets smear whitewash on it.
text.Rotherham: Because, yea, even because, they have led astray my people saying. Prosperity! when there was no prosperity,—and one man, was building a partition wall, when there they were! eating it with whitewash
text.KJV: Because, even because they have seduced my people, saying, Peace; and there was no peace; and one built up a wall, and, lo, others daubed it with untempered morter:
text.NABRE: Because they led my people astray, saying, “Peace!” when there is no peace, and when a wall is built, they cover it with whitewash,
text.DRB: For that they have deceived my people, saying: Peace, and there is not peace: and he builded the wall, and they daubed it with mortar without straw.

text.NRSV: Say to those who smear whitewash on it that it shall fall. There will be a deluge of rain, great hailstones will fall, and a stormy wind will break out.
text.Rotherham: Say thou unto them who are coating with whitewash It shall fall,- There hath come an overflowing rain And I will make hail-stones fall, And a tempestuous wind shall break it down.
text.KJV: Say unto them which daub it with untempered morter, that it shall fall: there shall be an overflowing shower; and ye, O great hailstones, shall fall; and a stormy wind shall rend it.
text.NABRE: say then to the whitewashers: I will bring down a flooding rain; hailstones shall fall, and a stormwind shall break forth.
text.DRB: Say to them that daub without tempering, that it shall fall: for there shall be a shower overflowing, and I will give very great stones falling violently from above, and the wind of a storm dissipating.

text.NRSV: When the wall falls, will it not be said to you, “Where is the whitewash you smeared on it?”
text.Rotherham: Lo! when the wall hath fallen, shall it not be said unto you, Where is the coating, wherewith ye coated it?
text.KJV: Lo, when the wall is fallen, shall it not be said unto you, Where is the daubing wherewith ye have daubed it?
text.NABRE: When the wall has fallen, will you not be asked: “Where is the whitewash you spread on it?”
text.DRB: For indeed behold the wall is fallen: shall it not be said to you: Where is the daubing, that you daubed?

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God: In my wrath I will make a stormy wind break out, and in my anger there shall be a deluge of rain, and hailstones in wrath to destroy it.
text.Rotherham: Therefore Thus saith my Lord, Yahweh, So will I break down with a tempestuous wind. in mine indignation,— And an overflowing rain in mine anger, shall there be, With hailstones in wrath to make an end;
text.KJV: Therefore thus saith the Lord God; I will even rend it with a stormy wind in my fury; and there shall be an overflowing shower in mine anger, and great hailstones in my fury to consume it.
text.NABRE: Therefore thus says the Lord God: In my fury I will let loose stormwinds; because of my anger there will be flooding rain, and hailstones will fall with destructive wrath.
text.DRB: Therefore thus saith our Lord God: And I will make the spirit of tempests to break forth in mine indignation, and there shall be an overflowing showering in my fury: & great stones in my wrath unto consumption.

text.NRSV: I will break down the wall that you have smeared with whitewash, and bring it to the ground, so that its foundation will be laid bare; when it falls, you shall perish within it; and you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And I will pull down the wall which ye have coated with whitewash And will bring it unto the ground So shall be discovered its foundation, Yea it shall fall And ye shall come to an end in the midst thereof, So shall ye know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: So will I break down the wall that ye have daubed with untempered morter, and bring it down to the ground, so that the foundation thereof shall be discovered, and it shall fall, and ye shall be consumed in the midst thereof: and ye shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: I will tear down the wall you whitewashed and level it to the ground, laying bare its foundations. When it falls, you shall be crushed beneath it. Thus you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.DRB: And I will destroy the wall, that you have daubed without tempering: & I will make it even with the ground, and the foundation thereof shall be revealed: and it shall fall, and shall be consumed in the midst thereof: and you shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: Thus I will spend my wrath upon the wall, and upon those who have smeared it with whitewash; and I will say to you, The wall is no more, nor those who smeared it—
text.Rotherham: Thus will I bring to an end mine indignation against the wall, and against them who were coating it with whitewash, And will say to you No more is the wall, And no more are they who were coating it:
text.KJV: Thus will I accomplish my wrath upon the wall, and upon them that have daubed it with untempered morter, and will say unto you, The wall is no more, neither they that daubed it;
text.NABRE: When I have poured out my fury on the wall and its whitewashers, it will fall. Then I will say to you: No wall! No whitewashers—
text.DRB: And I will accomplish mine indignation in the wall, and in them, that daub it without tempering, and I will say to you: The wall is not, and they are not that daub it.

text.NRSV: the prophets of Israel who prophesied concerning Jerusalem and saw visions of peace for it, when there was no peace, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: to wit the prophets of Israel who are prophesying unto Jerusalem, and are seeing, on her behalf, visions of prosperity,— when there is no prosperity, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: To wit, the prophets of Israel which prophesy concerning Jerusalem, and which see visions of peace for her, and there is no peace, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: the prophets of Israel who prophesy to Jerusalem and see visions of peace for it when there is no peace—oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: The Prophets of Israel, which prophesy to Jerusalem, & see vision of peace for it: and there is not peace, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: As for you, mortal, set your face against the daughters of your people, who prophesy out of their own imagination; prophesy against them
text.Rotherham: And thou, Son of man Set thy face against the daughters of thy people, Those who are prophesying out of their own heart,— And prophesy thou against them;
text.KJV: Likewise, thou son of man, set thy face against the daughters of thy people, which prophesy out of their own heart; and prophesy thou against them,
text.NABRE: Against Witches. As for you, son of man, now set your face against the daughters of your people who play the prophet from their own thoughts, and prophesy against them.
text.DRB: And thou son of man, put thy face against the daughters of thy people, which prophesy of their own heart: and do thou prophesy upon them,

text.NRSV: and say, Thus says the Lord God: Woe to the women who sew bands on all wrists, and make veils for the heads of persons of every height, in the hunt for human lives! Will you hunt down lives among my people, and maintain your own lives?
text.Rotherham: and say, Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Alas! for the women Who sew oracle, cases on all elbows And prepare wraps upon the head of every stature to hunt souls,— the souls ye hunt, are those of my people, Whereas your own souls:, ye keep alive.
text.KJV: And say, Thus saith the Lord God; Woe to the women that sew pillows to all armholes, and make kerchiefs upon the head of every stature to hunt souls! Will ye hunt the souls of my people, and will ye save the souls alive that come unto you?
text.NABRE: You shall say, Thus says the Lord God: Woe to those who sew amulets for the wrists of every arm and make veils for every head size to snare lives! You ensnare the lives of my people, even as you preserve your own lives!
text.DRB: and say: Thus saith our Lord God: woe to them that sow cushions under every cubit of the hand: and make pillows under the head of every age to catch souls: and when they caught the souls of my people, they did vivificate their souls.

text.NRSV: You have profaned me among my people for handfuls of barley and for pieces of bread, putting to death persons who should not die and keeping alive persons who should not live, by your lies to my people, who listen to lies.
text.Rotherham: Thus have ye profaned me unto my people for handfuls of barley and for pieces of bread, Putting to death the souls that should not die, and Keeping alive the souls that should not live,— By your lying to my people, who hearken to lies
text.KJV: And will ye pollute me among my people for handfuls of barley and for pieces of bread, to slay the souls that should not die, and to save the souls alive that should not live, by your lying to my people that hear your lies?
text.NABRE: You have profaned me among my people for handfuls of barley and crumbs of bread, slaying those who should not be slain, and keeping alive those who should not live, lying to my people, who listen to lies.
text.DRB: And they violated me to my people, for a handful of barley, and a cantel of a bread, that they might kill the souls, which die not, and vivificate the souls which live not, lying to my people, that believeth lies.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God: I am against your bands with which you hunt lives; I will tear them from your arms, and let the lives go free, the lives that you hunt down like birds.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus saith my Lord Yahweh, Behold me! against your oracle-cases wherewith ye are hunting the souls to make them fly, And I will tear them off your arms, And let go the souls of them whose souls ye are hunting as birds;
text.KJV: Wherefore thus saith the Lord God; Behold, I am against your pillows, wherewith ye there hunt the souls to make them fly, and I will tear them from your arms, and will let the souls go, even the souls that ye hunt to make them fly.
text.NABRE: Therefore thus says the Lord God: See! I am coming after your amulets by which you ensnare lives like prey. I will tear them from your arms and set free the lives of those you have ensnared like prey.
text.DRB: For this cause thus saith our Lord God: Behold I to your cushions, wherewith you catch flying souls: and I will break them from your arms: and I will let go the souls that you take, the souls to fly

text.NRSV: I will tear off your veils, and save my people from your hands; they shall no longer be prey in your hands; and you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And will tear off your wraps, And deliver my people out of your hand, And they shall be no longer in your hand to be hunted;- So shall ye know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: Your kerchiefs also will I tear, and deliver my people out of your hand, and they shall be no more in your hand to be hunted; and ye shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: I will tear off your veils and deliver my people from your power, so that they shall never again be ensnared by your hands. Thus you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.DRB: And I will break your pillows, & will deliver my people out of your hand, neither shall they be any more in your hands to be a prey: and you shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: Because you have disheartened the righteous falsely, although I have not disheartened them, and you have encouraged the wicked not to turn from their wicked way and save their lives;
text.Rotherham: Because of the paining of the heart of the righteous man with falsehood, whom, I, have not pained,— And strengthening the hands of the lawless man, that he should not turn from his wicked way. by letting him live
text.KJV: Because with lies ye have made the heart of the righteous sad, whom I have not made sad; and strengthened the hands of the wicked, that he should not return from his wicked way, by promising him life:
text.NABRE: Because you discourage the righteous with lies when I did not want them to be distressed, and encourage the wicked so they do not turn from their evil ways and save their lives,
text.DRB: For that you have made the heart of the just to mourn lyingly, whom I made not sorrowful: and have strengthened the hands of the impious, that he might not return from his evil way, and live.

text.NRSV: therefore you shall no longer see false visions or practice divination; I will save my people from your hand. Then you will know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Therefore—of falsehood:, shall ye have no vision, And a divination:, shall ye not divine any more,— So will I deliver my people out of your hand, And ye shall know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: Therefore ye shall see no more vanity, nor divine divinations: for I will deliver my people out of your hand: and ye shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: therefore you shall no longer see false visions or practice divination again. I will deliver my people from your hand. Thus you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.DRB: Therefore you shall not see vain things, and divinations you shall divine no more, and I will deliver my people out of your hand: and you shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: Certain elders of Israel came to me and sat down before me.
text.Rotherham: Then came there unto me men, of the elders of Israel,—and sat before me—
text.KJV: Then came certain of the elders of Israel unto me, and sat before me.
text.NABRE: Some elders of Israel came and sat down before me.
text.DRB: And men of the ancients of Israel came to me, and sat before me.

text.NRSV: And the word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying:
text.KJV: And the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: Then the word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, these men have taken their idols into their hearts, and placed their iniquity as a stumbling block before them; shall I let myself be consulted by them?
text.Rotherham: Son of man These men have brought up their manufactured gods upon their hear, And their stumbling-block of iniquity, have they set straight before their face, —Shall I be at all enquired of by them?
text.KJV: Son of man, these men have set up their idols in their heart, and put the stumblingblock of their iniquity before their face: should I be enquired of at all by them?
text.NABRE: Son of man, these men keep the memory of their idols alive in their hearts, setting the stumbling block of their sin before them. Should I allow myself to be consulted by them?
text.DRB: Son of man, These men have put their uncleanness in their hearts, and the scandal of their iniquity they have set against their face: what, being asked shall I answer them?

text.NRSV: Therefore speak to them, and say to them, Thus says the Lord God: Any of those of the house of Israel who take their idols into their hearts and place their iniquity as a stumbling block before them, and yet come to the prophet—I the Lord will answer those who come with the multitude of their idols,
text.Rotherham: Therefore speak unto them and say unto them, Thus, saith My Lord, Yahweh, Any man whatsoever of the house of Israel Who shall bring up his manufactured gods upon his heart, And set, his stumbling-block of iniquity, straight before his face, And then come in unto the prophet, I Yahweh, have myself answered him thereby. By the multitude of his manufactured gods:
text.KJV: Therefore speak unto them, and say unto them, Thus saith the Lord God; Every man of the house of Israel that setteth up his idols in his heart, and putteth the stumblingblock of his iniquity before his face, and cometh to the prophet; I the Lord will answer him that cometh according to the multitude of his idols;
text.NABRE: Therefore say to them: Thus says the Lord God: If any of the house of Israel who keep the memory of their idols in their hearts, setting the stumbling block of their sin before them, come to a prophet, I the Lord will answer in person because of their many idols,
text.DRB: For this cause speak to them, and thou shalt say to them: Thus saith our Lord God: Man, man of the house of Israel, that shall put his uncleanness in his heart, and set the scandal of his iniquity against his face, and shall come to the Prophet asking me by him: I the Lord will answer him in the multitude of his uncleanness:

text.NRSV: in order that I may take hold of the hearts of the house of Israel, all of whom are estranged from me through their idols.
text.Rotherham: To the end the house of Israel may be taken by their own hearts,—in that they have estranged themselves from me, through their manufactured gods—all of them!
text.KJV: That I may take the house of Israel in their own heart, because they are all estranged from me through their idols.
text.NABRE: in order to catch the hearts of the house of Israel, estranged from me because of all their idols.
text.DRB: that the house of Israel may be taken in their heart, wherewith they have revolted from me in all their idols.

text.NRSV: Therefore say to the house of Israel, Thus says the Lord God: Repent and turn away from your idols; and turn away your faces from all your abominations.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, say thou unto the house of Israel, Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Turn ye—and make good your return—from your manufactured gods; And, from all your abominations, make good the turning away of your faces.
text.KJV: Therefore say unto the house of Israel, Thus saith the Lord God; Repent, and turn yourselves from your idols; and turn away your faces from all your abominations.
text.NABRE: Therefore say to the house of Israel: Thus says the Lord God: Return, turn away from your idols; from all your abominations, turn your faces.
text.DRB: Therefore say to the house of Israel: Thus saith our Lord God: Convert, and depart from your idols, and from all your contaminations turn away your faces.

text.NRSV: For any of those of the house of Israel, or of the aliens who reside in Israel, who separate themselves from me, taking their idols into their hearts and placing their iniquity as a stumbling block before them, and yet come to a prophet to inquire of me by him, I the Lord will answer them myself.
text.Rotherham: For what man soever of the house of Israel Or of the sojourners who sojourn in Israel, Who shall estrange himself from following me And who shall bring up his manufactured gods, on his heart, And whose stumbling-block of iniquity, he shall set straight before his face, And then come in unto the prophet, to enquire by him of me, I Yahweh, will myself answer him, on my own account;
text.KJV: For every one of the house of Israel, or of the stranger that sojourneth in Israel, which separateth himself from me, and setteth up his idols in his heart, and putteth the stumblingblock of his iniquity before his face, and cometh to a prophet to enquire of him concerning me; I the Lord will answer him by myself:
text.NABRE: For if anyone of the house of Israel or any alien residing in Israel who are estranged from me and who keep their idols in their hearts, setting the stumbling block of their sin before them, come to ask a prophet to consult me on their behalf, I the Lord will answer them in person.
text.DRB: Because man man of the house of Israel and of the proselytes, whosoever shall be a stranger in Israel, if he be alienated from me, and put his idols in his heart, and set the scandal of iniquity against his face, and come to the Prophet to ask me by him: I the Lord will answer him by myself.

text.NRSV: I will set my face against them; I will make them a sign and a byword and cut them off from the midst of my people; and you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And will set my face against that man And will make of him a sign and a proverb, And will cut him off out of the midst of my people: So shall ye know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: And I will set my face against that man, and will make him a sign and a proverb, and I will cut him off from the midst of my people; and ye shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: I will set my face against them and make them a sign and a byword, and cut them off from the midst of my people. Thus you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.DRB: And I will set my face upon that man, and will make him for an example, & for a proverb, & will destroy him out of the midst of my people: & you shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: If a prophet is deceived and speaks a word, I, the Lord, have deceived that prophet, and I will stretch out my hand against him, and will destroy him from the midst of my people Israel.
text.Rotherham: Yea the prophet himself when he suffereth himself to be deceived, and speaketh a word, I Yahweh, have suffered that prophet to be deceived, Then will I stretch forth my hand against him, and destroy him out of the midst of my people. Israel:
text.KJV: And if the prophet be deceived when he hath spoken a thing, I the Lord have deceived that prophet, and I will stretch out my hand upon him, and will destroy him from the midst of my people Israel.
text.NABRE: As for the prophet, if he speaks a deceiving word, I the Lord am the one who deceives that prophet. I will stretch out my hand against him and destroy him from the midst of my people Israel.
text.DRB: And the Prophet when he shall err, & shall speak a word: I the Lord have deceived that Prophet: and I will stretch forth my hand upon him, and will rase him out of the midst of my people Israel.

text.NRSV: And they shall bear their punishment—the punishment of the inquirer and the punishment of the prophet shall be the same—
text.Rotherham: So shall they bear their punishment, As the punishment of him that enquireth, So shall the punishment of the prophet be:
text.KJV: And they shall bear the punishment of their iniquity: the punishment of the prophet shall be even as the punishment of him that seeketh unto him;
text.NABRE: They will be punished for their own sins, the inquirer and the prophet alike,
text.DRB: And they shall carry their iniquity: according to the iniquity of him that asketh, so shall the iniquity of the Prophet be.

text.NRSV: so that the house of Israel may no longer go astray from me, nor defile themselves any more with all their transgressions. Then they shall be my people, and I will be their God, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: That the house of Israel may no more go astray from following me, And no more defile themselves by any of their transgressions,— But may become my people, and I, may become their God, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: That the house of Israel may go no more astray from me, neither be polluted any more with all their transgressions; but that they may be my people, and I may be their God, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: so that the house of Israel may no longer stray from me, no longer defile themselves by all their sins. Then they shall be my people, and I shall be their God—oracle of the Lord God. Just Cause.
text.DRB: That the house of Israel may no more err from me, nor be polluted in all their prevarications: but may be my people, & I may be their God, saith the Lord of hosts.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me saying:
text.KJV: The word of the Lord came again to me, saying,
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, when a land sins against me by acting faithlessly, and I stretch out my hand against it, and break its staff of bread and send famine upon it, and cut off from it human beings and animals,
text.Rotherham: Son of man, When a land, shall sin against me by committing treachery, and I shall stretch out my hand against it, And break for it the staff of bread, And send thereon famine, And cut off therefrom man and beast,
text.KJV: Son of man, when the land sinneth against me by trespassing grievously, then will I stretch out mine hand upon it, and will break the staff of the bread thereof, and will send famine upon it, and will cut off man and beast from it:
text.NABRE: Son of man, if a land sins against me by breaking faith, and I stretch out my hand against it, breaking its staff of bread and setting famine loose upon it, cutting off from it human being and beast alike—
text.DRB: Son of man, the land when it shall sin to me, that transgressing it transgresseth, I will stretch forth my hand upon it, and will break the rod of the bread thereof: and I will send famine into it, and will kill out of it man and beast.

text.NRSV: even if Noah, Daniel, and Job, these three, were in it, they would save only their own lives by their righteousness, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: Then should these three men be in the midst thereof, Noah, Daniel, and Job, they by their righteousness should deliver their own lives— Declareth My Lord, Yahweh.
text.KJV: Though these three men, Noah, Daniel, and Job, were in it, they should deliver but their own souls by their righteousness, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: even if these three were in it, Noah, Daniel, and Job, they could only save themselves by their righteousness—oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: And if these three men shall be in the midst thereof, Noe, Daniel, & Job: they by their justice shall deliver their own souls, saith the Lord of hosts.

text.NRSV: If I send wild animals through the land to ravage it, so that it is made desolate, and no one may pass through because of the animals;
text.Rotherham: If a mischievous wild beast, I suffer to pass through the land, and that beast bereave it, so that it become too desolate for any man to pass through, by reason of the wild beast,
text.KJV: If I cause noisome beasts to pass through the land, and they spoil it, so that it be desolate, that no man may pass through because of the beasts:
text.NABRE: If I summoned wild beasts to prowl the land, depopulating it so that it became a wasteland which no one would cross because of the wild beasts,
text.DRB: And if I shall bring most evil beasts also upon the land, to waste it; and it be without passage, for that there is none can pass because of the beasts:

text.NRSV: even if these three men were in it, as I live, says the Lord God, they would save neither sons nor daughters; they alone would be saved, but the land would be desolate.
text.Rotherham: [Were] these three men in the midst thereof, As I live, Declareth My Lord Yahweh, Surely neither sons nor daughters, should they deliver, They alone should be delivered, But the land, should become a desolation.
text.KJV: Though these three men were in it, as I live, saith the Lord God, they shall deliver neither sons nor daughters; they only shall be delivered, but the land shall be desolate.
text.NABRE: and these three were in it, as I live—oracle of the Lord GOD—I swear they could save neither sons nor daughters; they alone would be saved, but the land would become a wasteland.
text.DRB: These three men if they shall be therein, I live, I, saith our Lord, that they shall deliver neither sons nor daughters: but themselves alone shall be delivered, and the land shall be made desolate.

text.NRSV: Or if I bring a sword upon that land and say, “Let a sword pass through the land,” and I cut off human beings and animals from it;
text.Rotherham: Or, a sword, I should bring upon that land, and should say. O sword! thou shalt go through the land, and so I should cut off therefrom, man and beast,
text.KJV: Or if I bring a sword upon that land, and say, Sword, go through the land; so that I cut off man and beast from it:
text.NABRE: Or if I bring the sword upon this land, commanding the sword to pass through the land cutting off from it human being and beast alike,
text.DRB: Or if I shall bring the sword in upon that land, and shall say to the sword: Pass through the land: and shall kill out of it man & beast:

text.NRSV: though these three men were in it, as I live, says the Lord God, they would save neither sons nor daughters, but they alone would be saved.
text.Rotherham: Were these three men in the midst thereof, As I live! Saith My Lord Yahweh, They should deliver neither sons nor daughters,— For they alone, should be delivered.
text.KJV: Though these three men were in it, as I live, saith the Lord God, they shall deliver neither sons nor daughters, but they only shall be delivered themselves.
text.NABRE: and these three were in it, as I live—oracle of the Lord GOD—they could save neither sons nor daughters; they alone would be saved.
text.DRB: And these three men shall be in the midst thereof: I live, I, saith our Lord God, they shall not deliver sons nor daughters, but themselves alone shall be delivered.

text.NRSV: Or if I send a pestilence into that land, and pour out my wrath upon it with blood, to cut off humans and animals from it;
text.Rotherham: Or pestilence, I, should send into that land,—and should pour out mine indignation thereon in blood, to cut off therefrom man and beast
text.KJV: Or if I send a pestilence into that land, and pour out my fury upon it in blood, to cut off from it man and beast:
text.NABRE: Or if I send plague into this land, pouring out upon it my bloody wrath, cutting off from it human being and beast alike,
text.DRB: And if I shall send the pestilence also in upon that land, and shall pour out mine indignation upon it in blood, to take away out of it man and beast:

text.NRSV: even if Noah, Daniel, and Job were in it, as I live, says the Lord God, they would save neither son nor daughter; they would save only their own lives by their righteousness.
