text.Rotherham: Were Noah, Daniel and Job in the midst thereof, As I live, Declareth My Lord Yahweh, Surely neither son nor daughter, should they deliver, They, by their righteousness should deliver only their own lives.
text.KJV: Though Noah, Daniel, and Job, were in it, as I live, saith the Lord God, they shall deliver neither son nor daughter; they shall but deliver their own souls by their righteousness.
text.NABRE: even if Noah, Daniel, and Job were in it, as I live—oracle of the Lord GOD—they could save neither son nor daughter; they would save only themselves by their righteousness.
text.DRB: And Noe, & Daniel, & Job shall be in the midst thereof: live I, saith our Lord God, that son and daughter they shall not deliver: but themselves by their justice shall deliver their own souls.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord God: How much more when I send upon Jerusalem my four deadly acts of judgment, sword, famine, wild animals, and pestilence, to cut off humans and animals from it!
text.Rotherham: For thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, How much less [should they avail] when my four calamitous judgments, sword and famine and mischievous wild beast and pestilence I have sent against Jerusalem,—to cut off therefrom man and beast?
text.KJV: For thus saith the Lord God; How much more when I send my four sore judgments upon Jerusalem, the sword, and the famine, and the noisome beast, and the pestilence, to cut off from it man and beast?
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord God: Even though I send against Jerusalem my four evil punishments—sword, famine, wild beasts, and plague—to cut off from it human being and beast alike,
text.DRB: Because thus saith our Lord: That and if I shall send in upon Jerusalem my four very sore judgements, the sword, and famine, and evil beasts, & the pestilence: to kill out of it man, and beast:

text.NRSV: Yet, survivors shall be left in it, sons and daughters who will be brought out; they will come out to you. When you see their ways and their deeds, you will be consoled for the evil that I have brought upon Jerusalem, for all that I have brought upon it.
text.Rotherham: Yet lo! there hath been left therein a remnant of fugitives to be brought forth —sons and daughters— There they are! coming forth unto you, And so ye shall see their way, and their doings, and be consoled over the calamity which I have brought in upon Jerusalem even all that I have brought in upon her.
text.KJV: Yet, behold, therein shall be left a remnant that shall be brought forth, both sons and daughters: behold, they shall come forth unto you, and ye shall see their way and their doings: and ye shall be comforted concerning the evil that I have brought upon Jerusalem, even concerning all that I have brought upon it.
text.NABRE: there will still be some survivors in it who will bring out sons and daughters. When they come out to you and you see their ways and their deeds, you shall be consoled regarding the evil I brought on Jerusalem, everything I brought upon it.
text.DRB: Yet shall there be left in it salvation of some bringing out sons, and daughters: behold they shall go forth to you, & you shall see their way, and their inventions: and you shall be comforted upon the evil, that I have brought upon Jerusalem in all things, that I have brought in upon it.

text.NRSV: They shall console you, when you see their ways and their deeds; and you shall know that it was not without cause that I did all that I have done in it, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: Yea they shall console you, when ye see their way and their doings,— So shall ye know that not without cause, have I done, anything that I have done with her, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: And they shall comfort you, when ye see their ways and their doings: and ye shall know that I have not done without cause all that I have done in it, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: They shall console you when you see their ways and their deeds, and you shall know that not without reason did I do to it everything I did—oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: And they shall comfort you, when you shall see their way, & their inventions: and you shall know that I have not in vain done all things, which I have done in it, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me saying:
text.KJV: And the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: O mortal, how does the wood of the vine surpass all other wood— the vine branch that is among the trees of the forest?
text.Rotherham: Son of man, What can the vine-tree be more than any other tree,—any branch which hath been found among the trees of the forest?
text.KJV: Son of man, What is the vine tree more than any tree, or than a branch which is among the trees of the forest?
text.NABRE: Son of man, what makes the wood of the vine Better than the wood of branches found on the trees in the forest?
text.DRB: Son of man, what shall be made of the wood of the vine of all the trees of the woods, that are among the trees of the forests?

text.NRSV: Is wood taken from it to make anything? Does one take a peg from it on which to hang any object?
text.Rotherham: Shall there be taken from it wood, to be made into any work? Or will men take therefrom a peg, to hang thereon any vessel?
text.KJV: Shall wood be taken thereof to do any work? or will men take a pin of it to hang any vessel thereon?
text.NABRE: Can wood be taken from it to make something useful? Can someone make even a peg out of it on which to hang a vessel?
text.DRB: Shall there be taken wood of it, that a work may be made, or shall a pin be made thereof, that any vessel may hang thereon?

text.NRSV: It is put in the fire for fuel; when the fire has consumed both ends of it and the middle of it is charred, is it useful for anything?
text.Rotherham: Lo! into the fire, it is given up for fuel,— The two ends thereof, doth the fire devour, And the middle thereof is charred, Is it fit for any work?
text.KJV: Behold, it is cast into the fire for fuel; the fire devoureth both the ends of it, and the midst of it is burned. Is it meet for any work?
text.NABRE: Of course not! If it is fed to the fire for fuel, and the fire devours both ends of it, Leaving the middle charred, is it useful for anything then?
text.DRB: Behold it is given to the fire for food: the fire hath consumed both parts thereof, and the midst thereof is brought into ashes: why, shall it be profitable for a work?

text.NRSV: When it was whole it was used for nothing; how much less—when the fire has consumed it, and it is charred— can it ever be used for anything!
text.Rotherham: Lo! while it was yet whole, it could not be made into any work,— how much less when!the fire hath devoured it. and it is charred, can it still be made into any work?
text.KJV: Behold, when it was whole, it was meet for no work: how much less shall it be meet yet for any work, when the fire hath devoured it, and it is burned?
text.NABRE: Even when it is whole it cannot be used for anything; So when fire has devoured and charred it, how useful can it be?
text.DRB: Yea when it was whole, it was not fit for a work: how much more when the fire hath devoured, and burnt it, shall no work be made thereof?

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God: Like the wood of the vine among the trees of the forest, which I have given to the fire for fuel, so I will give up the inhabitants of Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, As a vine-tree among the trees of the forest, which I have given up to the fire for fuel, so have I given up the inhabitants of Jerusalem.
text.KJV: Therefore thus saith the Lord God; As the vine tree among the trees of the forest, which I have given to the fire for fuel, so will I give the inhabitants of Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: Therefore, thus says the Lord God: Like vine wood among forest trees, which I have given as fuel for fire, So I will give the inhabitants of Jerusalem.
text.DRB: Therefore thus saith our Lord God: As the wood of the vine among the trees of the forests, which I have given to the fire to be devoured, so will I deliver the inhabitants of Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: I will set my face against them; although they escape from the fire, the fire shall still consume them; and you shall know that I am the Lord, when I set my face against them.
text.Rotherham: Therefore will I set my face against them, Out of one fire, have they come forth But another fire, shall devour them,—So shall ye know that, I, am Yahweh, by my setting my face against them;
text.KJV: And I will set my face against them; they shall go out from one fire, and another fire shall devour them; and ye shall know that I am the Lord, when I set my face against them.
text.NABRE: I will set my face against them: Although they have escaped the fire, the fire will still devour them; You shall know that I am the Lord, when I set my face against them.
text.DRB: And I will set my face against them: out of the fire shall they come forth, and fire shall consume them: and you shall know that I am the Lord, when I shall set my face against them,

text.NRSV: And I will make the land desolate, because they have acted faithlessly, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: I will therefore give up the land as a desolation, —because they have committed treachery, Declareth My Lord., Yahweh.
text.KJV: And I will make the land desolate, because they have committed a trespass, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: Yes, I will make the land desolate, because they are so unfaithful— oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: and shall make their land not passable, and desolate, because they have been prevaricators, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying:
text.KJV: Again the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, make known to Jerusalem her abominations,
text.Rotherham: Son of man Let Jerusalem know her abominations,
text.KJV: Son of man, cause Jerusalem to know her abominations,
text.NABRE: Son of man, make known to Jerusalem her abominations.
text.DRB: Son of man make known to Jerusalem her abominations:

text.NRSV: and say, Thus says the Lord God to Jerusalem: Your origin and your birth were in the land of the Canaanites; your father was an Amorite, and your mother a Hittite.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shalt thou say— Thus saith My Lord. Yahweh To Jerusalem, Thine origin and thy birth, were of the land of the Canaanite,— Thy father was the Amorite And thy mother a Hittite
text.KJV: And say, Thus saith the Lord God unto Jerusalem; Thy birth and thy nativity is of the land of Canaan; thy father was an Amorite, and thy mother an Hittite.
text.NABRE: You shall say, Thus says the Lord God to Jerusalem: By origin and birth you belong to the land of the Canaanites; your father was an Amorite, your mother a Hittite.
text.DRB: and thou shalt say: Thus saith our Lord God to Jerusalem: Thy root, and thy generation of the land of Chanaan, thy father an Amorrheite, & thy mother a Cetheite.

text.NRSV: As for your birth, on the day you were born your navel cord was not cut, nor were you washed with water to cleanse you, nor rubbed with salt, nor wrapped in cloths.
text.Rotherham: And, as for thy birth, in the day thou wast born, Thy navel-cord was not cut, And, in water, wast thou not bathed to cleanse thee,— And, as for being salted, thou was not salted, And, as for being bandaged, thou wast not bandaged.
text.KJV: And as for thy nativity, in the day thou wast born thy navel was not cut, neither wast thou washed in water to supple thee; thou wast not salted at all, nor swaddled at all.
text.NABRE: As for your birth, on the day you were born your navel cord was not cut; you were not washed with water or anointed; you were not rubbed with salt or wrapped in swaddling clothes.
text.DRB: And when thou wast born, in the day of thy birth thy navel was not cut, and thou wast not washed with water to health, nor salted with salt, nor swaddled in clouts.

text.NRSV: No eye pitied you, to do any of these things for you out of compassion for you; but you were thrown out in the open field, for you were abhorred on the day you were born.
text.Rotherham: No eye, threw a shield over thee, by doing for thee one of these things taking pity on thee,- But thou wast cast out on the face of the field Because thy person, was abhorred, in the day thou wast born.
text.KJV: None eye pitied thee, to do any of these unto thee, to have compassion upon thee; but thou wast cast out in the open field, to the lothing of thy person, in the day that thou wast born.
text.NABRE: No eye looked on you with pity or compassion to do any of these things for you. Rather, on the day you were born you were left out in the field, rejected.
text.DRB: No eye had mercy on thee to do unto thee one of these things, having pity on thee: but thou wast thrown forth upon the face of the earth in the abjection of thy soul, in the day that thou wast born.

text.NRSV: I passed by you, and saw you flailing about in your blood. As you lay in your blood, I said to you, “Live!
text.Rotherham: And I passed by thee, and looked upon thee, thrusting about thee in thy blood, And said to thee— Despite thy blood, live! Yea I said to thee— Despite thy blood, live!
text.KJV: And when I passed by thee, and saw thee polluted in thine own blood, I said unto thee when thou wast in thy blood, Live; yea, I said unto thee when thou wast in thy blood, Live.
text.NABRE: Then I passed by and saw you struggling in your blood, and I said to you in your blood, “Live!”
text.DRB: And passing by thee I saw thee to be trodden down in thy blood: and I said to thee when thou wast in thy blood: Live. I said to thee, I say: In thy blood live.

text.NRSV: and grow up like a plant of the field.” You grew up and became tall and arrived at full womanhood; your breasts were formed, and your hair had grown; yet you were naked and bare.
text.Rotherham: Into myriads—like the bud of the field, made I thee, And thou didst increase and become well grown, and didst attain to most excellent adornments,— Thy breasts were well-formed And thy hair was grown, But thou thyself wast utterly naked.
text.KJV: I have caused thee to multiply as the bud of the field, and thou hast increased and waxen great, and thou art come to excellent ornaments: thy breasts are fashioned, and thine hair is grown, whereas thou wast naked and bare.
text.NABRE: I helped you grow up like a field plant, so that you grew, maturing into a woman with breasts developed and hair grown; but still you were stark naked.
text.DRB: Multiplied as the spring of the field have I made thee: and thou wast multiplied, & made great, and thou wentest in, and camest to the ornaments of women: thy breasts swelled, and thy hair budded: and thou was naked, and full of confusion.

text.NRSV: I passed by you again and looked on you; you were at the age for love. I spread the edge of my cloak over you, and covered your nakedness: I pledged myself to you and entered into a covenant with you, says the Lord God, and you became mine.
text.Rotherham: And I passed by thee and looked upon thee and lo! thy time was the time for endearments, So I spread my skirt over thee, and covered thy shame, And took an oath to thee And entered into covenant with thee. Declareth My Lord. Yahweh. And thou didst become mine.
text.KJV: Now when I passed by thee, and looked upon thee, behold, thy time was the time of love; and I spread my skirt over thee, and covered thy nakedness: yea, I sware unto thee, and entered into a covenant with thee, saith the Lord God, and thou becamest mine.
text.NABRE: I passed by you again and saw that you were now old enough for love. So I spread the corner of my cloak over you to cover your nakedness; I swore an oath to you and entered into covenant with you—oracle of the Lord GOD—and you became mine.
text.DRB: And I passed by thee, and saw thee: and behold thy time, the time of lovers: and I spread my garment over thee, and covered thy ignominy. And I sware to thee, and I entered a covenant with thee, saith our Lord God: and thou wast made mine.

text.NRSV: Then I bathed you with water and washed off the blood from you, and anointed you with oil.
text.Rotherham: And I bathed thee in water, and rinsed thy blood from off thee, And anointed thee with oil;
text.KJV: Then washed I thee with water; yea, I throughly washed away thy blood from thee, and I anointed thee with oil.
text.NABRE: Then I bathed you with water, washed away your blood, and anointed you with oil.
text.DRB: And I washed thee with water, and made clean thy blood from off thee: and I anointed thee with oil.

text.NRSV: I clothed you with embroidered cloth and with sandals of fine leather; I bound you in fine linen and covered you with rich fabric.
text.Rotherham: And clothed thee with an embroidered dress, And sandalled thee in red leather, And wrapped thee about with fine linen, And put over thee a mantle of silk.
text.KJV: I clothed thee also with broidered work, and shod thee with badgers’ skin, and I girded thee about with fine linen, and I covered thee with silk.
text.NABRE: I clothed you with an embroidered gown, put leather sandals on your feet; I gave you a fine linen sash and silk robes to wear.
text.DRB: And I clothed thee with diverse colours, and shod thee with hyacinth: and I girded thee with silk, and clothed thee with fine garments.

text.NRSV: I adorned you with ornaments: I put bracelets on your arms, a chain on your neck,
text.Rotherham: Then decked I thee with ornaments, And put bracelets upon thy hands, And a neckchain upon thy throat;
text.KJV: I decked thee also with ornaments, and I put bracelets upon thy hands, and a chain on thy neck.
text.NABRE: I adorned you with jewelry, putting bracelets on your arms, a necklace about your neck,
text.DRB: And I adorned thee with ornament, and gave bracelets on thy hands, and a chain about thy neck.

text.NRSV: a ring on your nose, earrings in your ears, and a beautiful crown upon your head.
text.Rotherham: And put a nose-jewel upon thy nose, And earrings in thine ears, And a crown of adorning, upon thy head.
text.KJV: And I put a jewel on thy forehead, and earrings in thine ears, and a beautiful crown upon thine head.
text.NABRE: a ring in your nose, earrings in your ears, and a beautiful crown on your head.
text.DRB: And I gave a jewel upon thy face, and rings to thine ears, and a crown of beauty on thy head.

text.NRSV: You were adorned with gold and silver, while your clothing was of fine linen, rich fabric, and embroidered cloth. You had choice flour and honey and oil for food. You grew exceedingly beautiful, fit to be a queen.
text.Rotherham: Thus wast thou adorned with gold and silver, And thy raiment was of fine linen and silk and embroidered work, Fine flour and honey and oil, didst thou eat,— And so thou becamest exceedingly beautiful, And didst attain unto royalty.
text.KJV: Thus wast thou decked with gold and silver; and thy raiment was of fine linen, and silk, and broidered work; thou didst eat fine flour, and honey, and oil: and thou wast exceeding beautiful, and thou didst prosper into a kingdom.
text.NABRE: Thus you were adorned with gold and silver; your garments made of fine linen, silk, and embroidered cloth. Fine flour, honey, and olive oil were your food. You were very, very beautiful, fit for royalty.
text.DRB: And thou was adorned with gold, and silver, and wast clothed with silk, and embroidered work, and many colours: thou hast eaten flour, and honey, and oil, and wast made very beautiful exceedingly: and didst prosper to a kingdom.

text.NRSV: Your fame spread among the nations on account of your beauty, for it was perfect because of my splendor that I had bestowed on you, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: Then went forth thy fame among the nations. for thy beauty,- For perfect, it was—in my splendour which I had put upon thee, Declareth My Lord, Yahweh.
text.KJV: And thy renown went forth among the heathen for thy beauty: for it was perfect through my comeliness, which I had put upon thee, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: You were renowned among the nations for your beauty, perfected by the splendor I showered on you—oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: And thy name went forth into the Gentiles, because of thy beauty: because thou wast perfect in my beauty, which I had put upon thee, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: But you trusted in your beauty, and played the whore because of your fame, and lavished your whorings on any passer-by.
text.Rotherham: Then didst thou trust in thy beauty, And become unchaste, because of thy fame,- And didst pour out thine unchastity upon every passer-by, his it was!
text.KJV: But thou didst trust in thine own beauty, and playedst the harlot because of thy renown, and pouredst out thy fornications on every one that passed by; his it was.
text.NABRE: But you trusted in your own beauty and used your renown to serve as a prostitute. You poured out your prostitution on every passerby—let it be his.
text.DRB: And having confidence in thy beauty thou hast fornicated in thy name: & thou hast laid forth thy fornication to every passenger to be made his.

text.NRSV: You took some of your garments, and made for yourself colorful shrines, and on them played the whore; nothing like this has ever been or ever shall be.
text.Rotherham: Yea thou didst take of thy raiment and madest thee high places of hangings, And didst commit unchastity thereon,- Which ought not to have befallen And not to have come to pass.
text.KJV: And of thy garments thou didst take, and deckedst thy high places with divers colours, and playedst the harlot thereupon: the like things shall not come, neither shall it be so.
text.NABRE: You took some of your garments and made for yourself gaudy high places, where you served as a prostitute. It has never happened before, nor will it happen again!
text.DRB: And taking off thy garments thou madest thee idols embroidered on each side: & thou didst fornicate upon them, as hath not been done, nor shall not hereafter.

text.NRSV: You also took your beautiful jewels of my gold and my silver that I had given you, and made for yourself male images, and with them played the whore;
text.Rotherham: But thou didst take thine adorning jewels Of my gold and of my silver which I had given thee, And didst make thee images of the male,— And didst act unchastely with them;
text.KJV: Thou hast also taken thy fair jewels of my gold and of my silver, which I had given thee, and madest to thyself images of men, and didst commit whoredom with them,
text.NABRE: You took the splendid gold and silver ornaments that I had given you and made for yourself male images and served as a prostitute with them.
text.DRB: And thou tookest the vessels of thy beauty, of my gold, and my silver, which I gave thee: and thou madest thee images of men, and hast fornicated in them.

text.NRSV: and you took your embroidered garments to cover them, and set my oil and my incense before them.
text.Rotherham: And thou didst take thine embroidered raiment, and cover them,- And mine oil and mine incense, didst thou set before them;
text.KJV: And tookest thy broidered garments, and coveredst them: and thou hast set mine oil and mine incense before them.
text.NABRE: You took your embroidered garments to cover them; my oil and my incense you set before them;
text.DRB: And thou tookest thy garments of many colours, and coveredst them: and mine oil, and mine incense thou didst put before them.

text.NRSV: Also my bread that I gave you—I fed you with choice flour and oil and honey—you set it before them as a pleasing odor; and so it was, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: And my food which I had given thee, Fine flour and oil and honey wherewith I fed thee, And didst set it before them for a satisfying odour Yea so it was,— Saith My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: My meat also which I gave thee, fine flour, and oil, and honey, wherewith I fed thee, thou hast even set it before them for a sweet savour: and thus it was, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: the food I had given you, the fine flour, the oil, and the honey with which I fed you, you set before them as a pleasant odor, says the Lord God.
text.DRB: And my bread, which I gave thee, the flour, and oil, and honey, wherewith I have nourished thee, thou didst set in their sight for an odour of sweetness, and it was done saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: You took your sons and your daughters, whom you had borne to me, and these you sacrificed to them to be devoured. As if your whorings were not enough!
text.Rotherham: And thou didst take thy sons and thy daughters, Whom thou hadst borne unto me, And didst sacrifice them unto them to be devoured,- Is this of thine unchastity. a light thing?
text.KJV: Moreover thou hast taken thy sons and thy daughters, whom thou hast borne unto me, and these hast thou sacrificed unto them to be devoured. Is this of thy whoredoms a small matter,
text.NABRE: The sons and daughters you bore for me you took and offered as sacrifices for them to devour! Was it not enough that you had become a prostitute?
text.DRB: And thou hast taken thy sons, & thy daughters, which thou didst beget to me: and hast immolated the same unto them to devour. Why, is thy fornication small?

text.NRSV: You slaughtered my children and delivered them up as an offering to them.
text.Rotherham: Yea thou didst slay my children,— And didst deliver them up. that they should be caused to pass through the fire unto them.
text.KJV: That thou hast slain my children, and delivered them to cause them to pass through the fire for them?
text.NABRE: You slaughtered and immolated my children to them, making them pass through fire.
text.DRB: Thou hast immolated my sons, & hast given them, consecrating them to the idols.

text.NRSV: And in all your abominations and your whorings you did not remember the days of your youth, when you were naked and bare, flailing about in your blood.
text.Rotherham: And in all thine abominations, and thine unchaste ways, thou rememberedst not the days of thy youth, When thou wast utterly naked. When thou wast thrusting about thee in thy blood!
text.KJV: And in all thine abominations and thy whoredoms thou hast not remembered the days of thy youth, when thou wast naked and bare, and wast polluted in thy blood.
text.NABRE: In all your abominations and prostitutions you did not remember the days of your youth when you were stark naked, struggling in your blood.
text.DRB: And after all thine abominations, & fornications, thou wast not mindful of the days of thy youth, when thou wast naked, & full of confusion, trodden down in thy blood.

text.NRSV: After all your wickedness (woe, woe to you! says the Lord God),
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass after all thy wickedness, Woe! Woe! to thee, Exclaimeth My Lord Yahweh;
text.KJV: And it came to pass after all thy wickedness, (woe, woe unto thee! saith the Lord God;)
text.NABRE: Then after all your evildoing—woe, woe to you! oracle of the Lord GOD—
text.DRB: And it chanced after all thy malice (woe, woe to thee, saith our Lord God)

text.NRSV: you built yourself a platform and made yourself a lofty place in every square;
text.Rotherham: That thou didst build thee a brothel,— and didst make thee a height in every broadway:
text.KJV: That thou hast also built unto thee an eminent place, and hast made thee an high place in every street.
text.NABRE: you built yourself a platform and raised up a dais in every public place.
text.DRB: thou didst also build thee a stews, & madest thee a brothel house in all streets.

text.NRSV: at the head of every street you built your lofty place and prostituted your beauty, offering yourself to every passer-by, and multiplying your whoring.
text.Rotherham: At the head of every road, didst thou build thy height, And bring thy beauty into disgust, And open thy feet to every passer-by, So didst thou make thine unchaste ways to abound.
text.KJV: Thou hast built thy high place at every head of the way, and hast made thy beauty to be abhorred, and hast opened thy feet to every one that passed by, and multiplied thy whoredoms.
text.NABRE: At every intersection you built yourself a dais so that you could degrade your beauty by spreading your legs for every passerby, multiplying your prostitutions.
text.DRB: At every head of the way hast thou built a sign of thy prostitution: & hast made thy beauty abominable: & hast divided thy feet to every one that passeth by, and hast multiplied thy fornications.

text.NRSV: You played the whore with the Egyptians, your lustful neighbors, multiplying your whoring, to provoke me to anger.
text.Rotherham: Then didst thou extend thine unchaste acts unto the sons of Egypt—thy neighbour great of flesh,- And caused thine unchaste ways to abound provoking me to anger.
text.KJV: Thou hast also committed fornication with the Egyptians thy neighbours, great of flesh; and hast increased thy whoredoms, to provoke me to anger.
text.NABRE: You served as a prostitute with the Egyptians, your big-membered neighbors, and multiplied your prostitutions to provoke me.
text.DRB: And thou hast fornicated with the children of Ægypt thy neighbours of great flesh, & hast multiplied thy fornication to provoke me.

text.NRSV: Therefore I stretched out my hand against you, reduced your rations, and gave you up to the will of your enemies, the daughters of the Philistines, who were ashamed of your lewd behavior.
text.Rotherham: Lo! therefore, I have stretched out my hand against thee, And diminished thine allotted portion,— And have delivered thee up unto the desire of them who hate thee The daughters of the Philistines, who are ashamed of thy lewd way.
text.KJV: Behold, therefore I have stretched out my hand over thee, and have diminished thine ordinary food, and delivered thee unto the will of them that hate thee, the daughters of the Philistines, which are ashamed of thy lewd way.
text.NABRE: Therefore I stretched out my hand against you and reduced your allotment, and delivered you over to the whim of your enemies, the Philistines, who were revolted by your depraved conduct.
text.DRB: Behold I will stretch out my hand upon thee, & will take away thy justification: & I will give thee into the souls of the daughters of Palestine that hate thee, that are ashamed of thy wicked way.

text.NRSV: You played the whore with the Assyrians, because you were insatiable; you played the whore with them, and still you were not satisfied.
text.Rotherham: Thou didst also extend thine unchastity unto the sons of Assyria, because thou wast insatiable. Yet though thou didst behave unchastely with them, yet even so couldst thou not be satisfied,
text.KJV: Thou hast played the whore also with the Assyrians, because thou wast unsatiable; yea, thou hast played the harlot with them, and yet couldest not be satisfied.
text.NABRE: You also served as a prostitute for the Assyrians, because you were not satisfied. Even after serving as a prostitute for them, you were still not satisfied.
text.DRB: And thou hast committed fornication with the sons of the Assyrians, because neither as yet wast thou filled: & after thou didst fornicate, neither so wast thou satiated.

text.NRSV: You multiplied your whoring with Chaldea, the land of merchants; and even with this you were not satisfied.
text.Rotherham: Thou didst therefore cause thine unchaste ways to abound unto the land of Canaan as towards Chaldea. Yet even herewith, wast thou not satisfied.
text.KJV: Thou hast moreover multiplied thy fornication in the land of Canaan unto Chaldea; and yet thou wast not satisfied herewith.
text.NABRE: You increased your prostitutions again, now going to Chaldea, the land of traders; but despite this, you were still not satisfied.
text.DRB: And thou didst multiply thy fornication in the Land of Chanaan with the Chaldees: & neither so wast thou satiated.

text.NRSV: How sick is your heart, says the Lord God, that you did all these things, the deeds of a brazen whore;
text.Rotherham: How weak was thy heart! Exclaimeth My Lord Yahweh,— That thou couldst have done all these things, The doing of a lewd woman without shame:
text.KJV: How weak is thine heart, saith the Lord God, seeing thou doest all these things, the work of an imperious whorish woman;
text.NABRE: How wild your lust!—oracle of the Lord GOD—that you did all these works of a shameless prostitute,
text.DRB: Wherein shall I cleanse thy heart, saith our Lord God: whereas thou doest all these the works of a woman that is a whore, & malapert?

text.NRSV: building your platform at the head of every street, and making your lofty place in every square! Yet you were not like a whore, because you scorned payment.
text.Rotherham: That thou couldst have built thy brothel at the head of every road, And thy height, couldst have made in every broadway,- Yet becamest not as a harlot to lay claim to a harlot’s hire.
text.KJV: In that thou buildest thine eminent place in the head of every way, and makest thine high place in every street; and hast not been as an harlot, in that thou scornest hire;
text.NABRE: when you built your platform at every intersection and set up your high place in every public square. But unlike a prostitute, you disdained payment.
text.DRB: Because thou hast built thy brothel house in the head of every way, & thy excelse thou hast made in every street, neither becamest thou as an harlot that by disdain augmenteth the price,

text.NRSV: Adulterous wife, who receives strangers instead of her husband!
text.Rotherham: A wife who committeth adultery, instead of her husband accepteth strangers.
text.KJV: But as a wife that committeth adultery, which taketh strangers instead of her husband!
text.NABRE: Adulterous wife, taking strangers in place of her husband!
text.DRB: but as a woman adulteress, that over her husband bringeth in strangers.

text.NRSV: Gifts are given to all whores; but you gave your gifts to all your lovers, bribing them to come to you from all around for your whorings.
text.Rotherham: To all harlots, they give a present,— But, thou, didst give thy presents to all thy lovers, And didst bribe them to come in unto thee from every side, in thine unchastity!
text.KJV: They give gifts to all whores: but thou givest thy gifts to all thy lovers, and hirest them, that they may come unto thee on every side for thy whoredom.
text.NABRE: Prostitutes usually receive gifts. But you bestowed gifts on all your lovers, bribing them to come to you for prostitution from every side.
text.DRB: To all harlots are rewards given: but thou hast given rewards to all thy lovers, & thou didst give them gifts to come unto thee on every side, to fornicate with thee.

text.NRSV: So you were different from other women in your whorings: no one solicited you to play the whore; and you gave payment, while no payment was given to you; you were different.
text.Rotherham: And so there came about in thee, the reverse of women, in thine unchastity, In that they did not follow thee for purposes of lewdness,— And in that thou gavest a present while, no present, was given to thee So didst thou become, the reverse.
text.KJV: And the contrary is in thee from other women in thy whoredoms, whereas none followeth thee to commit whoredoms: and in that thou givest a reward, and no reward is given unto thee, therefore thou art contrary.
text.NABRE: Thus in your prostitution you were different from any other woman. No one solicited you for prostitution. Instead, you yourself offered payment; what a reversal!
text.DRB: And in thee it was done contrary to the custom of women in thy fornications, and after thee there shall be no fornication: for in that thou gavest rewards, and didst not take rewards, it hath been done contrary in thee.

text.NRSV: Therefore, O whore, hear the word of the Lord:
text.Rotherham: Therefore O harlot, hear thou the word of Yahweh;
text.KJV: Wherefore, O harlot, hear the word of the Lord:
text.NABRE: Therefore, prostitute, hear the word of the Lord!
text.DRB: Therefore hear, o harlot, the word of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God, Because your lust was poured out and your nakedness uncovered in your whoring with your lovers, and because of all your abominable idols, and because of the blood of your children that you gave to them,
text.Rotherham: Thus saith My Lord Yahweh,— Because thy money, was poured out, and thy shame, was uncovered, in thine unchastity, unto thy lovers,—and unto all thine abominable manufactured gods, even as the blood of thy children, whom thou didst deliver up unto them,
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord God; Because thy filthiness was poured out, and thy nakedness discovered through thy whoredoms with thy lovers, and with all the idols of thy abominations, and by the blood of thy children, which thou didst give unto them;
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord God: Because you poured out your lust and exposed your nakedness in your prostitution with your lovers and your abominable idols, because you gave the life-blood of your children to them,
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord God: Because thy brass is poured out, & thine ignominy is revealed in thy fornications upon thy lovers, and upon the idols of thine abominations in the blood of thy children, whom thou gavest them:

text.NRSV: therefore, I will gather all your lovers, with whom you took pleasure, all those you loved and all those you hated; I will gather them against you from all around, and will uncover your nakedness to them, so that they may see all your nakedness.
text.Rotherham: Therefore behold me! gathering together all thy lovers unto whom thou didst make thyself pleasant, Even all whom thou lovedst, With all whom thou hatedst,— Yea I will gather them together unto thee from every side And will uncover thy shame unto them, And they shall see all thy shame.
text.KJV: Behold, therefore I will gather all thy lovers, with whom thou hast taken pleasure, and all them that thou hast loved, with all them that thou hast hated; I will even gather them round about against thee, and will discover thy nakedness unto them, that they may see all thy nakedness.
text.NABRE: therefore, I will now gather together all your lovers with whom you found pleasure, both those you loved and those you hated; I will gather them against you from all sides and expose you naked for them to see.
text.DRB: Behold I will gather together all thy lovers, with whom thou hast taken pleasure, & all, whom thou hast loved, with all, whom thou didst hate: and I will gather them together upon thee on every side, and will discover thine ignominy before them, and they shall see all thy turpitude.

text.NRSV: I will judge you as women who commit adultery and shed blood are judged, and bring blood upon you in wrath and jealousy.
text.Rotherham: So will I judge thee. with the judgments meted out to adulteresses and shedders of blood,- And will repay thee with the blood of indignation and jealousy;
text.KJV: And I will judge thee, as women that break wedlock and shed blood are judged; and I will give thee blood in fury and jealousy.
text.NABRE: I will inflict on you the sentence of adultery and murder; I will bring on you bloody wrath and jealous anger.
text.DRB: And I will judge thee by the judgements of adulteresses, and them that shed blood: and I will give thee into blood of fury and zeal.

text.NRSV: I will deliver you into their hands, and they shall throw down your platform and break down your lofty places; they shall strip you of your clothes and take your beautiful objects and leave you naked and bare.
text.Rotherham: And will deliver thee into their hand. And they shall pull down thy brothel And break in pieces thy heights, And strip thee of thy raiment, And take away thine adorning jewels,- And leave thee utterly naked.
text.KJV: And I will also give thee into their hand, and they shall throw down thine eminent place, and shall break down thy high places: they shall strip thee also of thy clothes, and shall take thy fair jewels, and leave thee naked and bare.
text.NABRE: I will hand you over to them to tear down your platform and demolish your high place, to strip you of your garments and take away your splendid ornaments, leaving you stark naked.
text.DRB: And I will give thee into their hands, and they shall destroy thy brothel-house, and throw down thy stews: and they shall turn thee naked out of thy garments, and shall take away the vessels of thy beauty: and shall leave thee naked, and full of ignominy.

text.NRSV: They shall bring up a mob against you, and they shall stone you and cut you to pieces with their swords.
text.Rotherham: Then will they bring up against thee a gathered host, And they will stone thee with stones,- And cut thee to pieces with their swords;
text.KJV: They shall also bring up a company against thee, and they shall stone thee with stones, and thrust thee through with their swords.
text.NABRE: They shall lead an assembly against you to stone you and hack you to pieces with their swords.
text.DRB: And they shall bring upon thee a multitude, and they shall stone thee with stones, and shall murder thee with their swords.

text.NRSV: They shall burn your houses and execute judgments on you in the sight of many women; I will stop you from playing the whore, and you shall also make no more payments.
text.Rotherham: And burn up thy houses with fire, And execute upon thee judgments before the eyes of many women,—So will I cause thee to cease from acting unchastely, Moreover also a present, shalt thou not give any more.
text.KJV: And they shall burn thine houses with fire, and execute judgments upon thee in the sight of many women: and I will cause thee to cease from playing the harlot, and thou also shalt give no hire any more.
text.NABRE: They shall set fire to your homes and inflict punishments on you while many women watch. Thus I will put an end to your prostitution, and you shall never again offer payment.
text.DRB: And they shall burn thy houses with fire, and shall do judgements in thee in the eyes of very many women: and thou shalt cease to fornicate, and shalt give rewards no more.

text.NRSV: So I will satisfy my fury on you, and my jealousy shall turn away from you; I will be calm, and will be angry no longer.
text.Rotherham: So will I let mine indignation find rest in thee, And my jealousy shall depart from thee,—And I will be quiet, and not be provoked any more.
text.KJV: So will I make my fury toward thee to rest, and my jealousy shall depart from thee, and I will be quiet, and will be no more angry.
text.NABRE: When I have spent my fury upon you I will stop being jealous about you, and calm down, no longer angry.
text.DRB: And mine indignation shall rest in thee: and my zeal shall be taken away from thee, and I will cease, and be angry no more.

text.NRSV: Because you have not remembered the days of your youth, but have enraged me with all these things; therefore, I have returned your deeds upon your head, says the Lord God. Have you not committed lewdness beyond all your abominations?
text.Rotherham: Because thou hast not remembered the days of thy youth, But hast enraged me with all these things Therefore also behold! I thy way, upon thine own head, will place Declareth My Lord Yahweh, And thou shalt not commit a crime above all thine abominations
text.KJV: Because thou hast not remembered the days of thy youth, but hast fretted me in all these things; behold, therefore I also will recompense thy way upon thine head, saith the Lord God: and thou shalt not commit this lewdness above all thine abominations.
text.NABRE: Because you did not remember the days of your youth but enraged me with all these things, see, I am bringing down your ways upon your head—oracle of the Lord God. Have you not added depravity to your other abominations?
text.DRB: For that thou hast not remembered the days of thy youth, and hast provoked me in all these: wherefore I also have given thy ways on thy head, saith our Lord God, & I have not done according to thy wicked deeds in all thine abominations.

text.NRSV: See, everyone who uses proverbs will use this proverb about you, “Like mother, like daughter.”
text.Rotherham: Lo! every one who useth proverbs, against thee, shall use a proverb saying,— Like the mother, so her daughter!
text.KJV: Behold, every one that useth proverbs shall use this proverb against thee, saying, As is the mother, so is her daughter.
text.NABRE: See, everyone who makes proverbs will make this proverb about you, “Like mother, like daughter.”
text.DRB: Behold every one, that speaketh a common proverb, shall take up that against thee, saying: As the mother, so also her daughter.

text.NRSV: You are the daughter of your mother, who loathed her husband and her children; and you are the sister of your sisters, who loathed their husbands and their children. Your mother was a Hittite and your father an Amorite.
text.Rotherham: The daughter of thy mother, thou art! One abhorring her own husband and her own children,— Yea the sister of thy sisters, thou art Who abhorred their own husbands and their own children, Your mother was a Hittite, And your father an Amorite.
text.KJV: Thou art thy mother’s daughter, that lotheth her husband and her children; and thou art the sister of thy sisters, which lothed their husbands and their children: your mother was an Hittite, and your father an Amorite.
text.NABRE: Yes, you are truly the daughter of your mother who rejected her husband and children: you are truly a sister to your sisters who rejected their husbands and children—your mother was a Hittite and your father an Amorite.
text.DRB: Thou art the daughter of thy mother, which did cast off her husband, & her children: & thou art sister of thy sisters, which did cast off their husbands, and their children: your mother was a Cetheite, and your father an Amorrheite.

text.NRSV: Your elder sister is Samaria, who lived with her daughters to the north of you; and your younger sister, who lived to the south of you, is Sodom with her daughters.
text.Rotherham: And thine elder sister, was Samaria, she and her daughters, dwelling on thy left hand,— And thy sister younger than thou dwelling on thy right hand was Sodom and her daughters.
text.KJV: And thine elder sister is Samaria, she and her daughters that dwell at thy left hand: and thy younger sister, that dwelleth at thy right hand, is Sodom and her daughters.
text.NABRE: Your elder sister was Samaria with her daughters to the north of you; and your younger sister was Sodom and her daughters, south of you.
text.DRB: And thy elder sister, Samaria, she & her daughters, that dwell on thy left hand, & thy sister younger than thou, which dwelleth on thy right hand, Sodom, & her daughters.

text.NRSV: You not only followed their ways, and acted according to their abominations; within a very little time you were more corrupt than they in all your ways.
text.Rotherham: Yet not in their ways, didst thou walk, nor according to their abominations, didst thou do,— As though that were quite too little, thou didst corrupt thyself beyond them in all thy ways.
text.KJV: Yet hast thou not walked after their ways, nor done after their abominations: but, as if that were a very little thing, thou wast corrupted more than they in all thy ways.
text.NABRE: Not only did you walk in their ways and act as abominably as they did, but in a very short time you became more corrupt in all your ways than they were.
text.DRB: But neither in their ways has thou walked, nor according to their wicked deeds hast thou done a very little less: thou hast done almost more wicked things than they in all thy ways.

text.NRSV: As I live, says the Lord God, your sister Sodom and her daughters have not done as you and your daughters have done.
text.Rotherham: As I live, Declareth My Lord Yahweh, Very! Sodom thy sister had not done, neither she nor her daughters,—as thou and thy daughters have done.
text.KJV: As I live, saith the Lord God, Sodom thy sister hath not done, she nor her daughters, as thou hast done, thou and thy daughters.
text.NABRE: As I live—oracle of the Lord GOD—I swear that your sister Sodom with her daughters have not done the things you and your daughters have done!
text.DRB: Live I, saith our Lord God, that Sodom thy sister herself hath not done, and her daughters, as thou hast done, and thy daughters.

text.NRSV: This was the guilt of your sister Sodom: she and her daughters had pride, excess of food, and prosperous ease, but did not aid the poor and needy.
text.Rotherham: Lo! this became the iniquity of Sodom thy sister,— Pride, fulness of bread, and careless security came to her and to her daughters, And the hand of the oppressed and the needy, she strengthened not.
text.KJV: Behold, this was the iniquity of thy sister Sodom, pride, fulness of bread, and abundance of idleness was in her and in her daughters, neither did she strengthen the hand of the poor and needy.
text.NABRE: Now look at the guilt of your sister Sodom: she and her daughters were proud, sated with food, complacent in prosperity. They did not give any help to the poor and needy.
text.DRB: Lo this was the iniquity of Sodom thy sister, pride, fullness of bread, and aboundance, and the idleness of her, and of her daughters: and they raught not the hand to the needy, and the poor.

text.NRSV: They were haughty, and did abominable things before me; therefore I removed them when I saw it.
text.Rotherham: So then they became haughty, and committed abomination before me,- And I took them away. when I saw [it],
text.KJV: And they were haughty, and committed abomination before me: therefore I took them away as I saw good.
text.NABRE: Instead, they became arrogant and committed abominations before me; then, as you have seen, I removed them.
text.DRB: And they were elevated, and did abominations before me: & I took them away as thou hast seen.

text.NRSV: Samaria has not committed half your sins; you have committed more abominations than they, and have made your sisters appear righteous by all the abominations that you have committed.
text.Rotherham: Nor did, Samaria, commit, one-half thy sins,— But thou didst multiply thine abominations more than they, And didst cause thy sisters to appear righteous, by all the abominations which thou didst commit.
text.KJV: Neither hath Samaria committed half of thy sins; but thou hast multiplied thine abominations more than they, and hast justified thy sisters in all thine abominations which thou hast done.
text.NABRE: Samaria did not commit half the sins you did. You have done more abominable things than they did. You even made your sisters look righteous, with all the abominations you have done.
text.DRB: And Samaria sinned not the half of thy sins: but thou hast passed them with thy wicked deeds, & hast justified thy sisters in all thine abominations, which thou hast wrought.

text.NRSV: Bear your disgrace, you also, for you have brought about for your sisters a more favorable judgment; because of your sins in which you acted more abominably than they, they are more in the right than you. So be ashamed, you also, and bear your disgrace, for you have made your sisters appear righteous.
text.Rotherham: Thou also bear thine own reproach, which thou didst adjudge to thy sisters, by thy sins in which thou wast more abominable than they, thou didst make them appear more righteous than thou,— Thou also, therefore turn thou pale and bear thine own reproach, for making thy sisters appear righteous!
text.KJV: Thou also, which hast judged thy sisters, bear thine own shame for thy sins that thou hast committed more abominable than they: they are more righteous than thou: yea, be thou confounded also, and bear thy shame, in that thou hast justified thy sisters.
text.NABRE: You, then, must bear your disgrace, for you have made a case for your sisters! Because your sins are more abominable than theirs, they seem righteous compared to you. Blush for shame, and bear the disgrace of having made your sisters appear righteous.
text.DRB: Thou also therefore carry thy confusion, which hast passed thy sisters with thy sins, doing more wickedly than they: for they are justified above thee: thou also therefore be confounded, and bear thine ignominy, which hast justified thy sisters.

text.NRSV: I will restore their fortunes, the fortunes of Sodom and her daughters and the fortunes of Samaria and her daughters, and I will restore your own fortunes along with theirs,
text.Rotherham: When therefore I bring back their captivity, The captivity of Sodom and her daughters, And the captivity of Samaria and her daughters Then will I bring back thy captivities in their midst:
text.KJV: When I shall bring again their captivity, the captivity of Sodom and her daughters, and the captivity of Samaria and her daughters, then will I bring again the captivity of thy captives in the midst of them:
text.NABRE: I will restore their fortunes, the fortunes of Sodom and her daughters, the fortunes of Samaria and her daughters—and your fortunes along with them.
text.DRB: And I will convert restoring them by the conversion of Sodom with her daughters, and by the conversion of Samaria, and her daughters: and I will convert thy reversion in the midst of them,

text.NRSV: in order that you may bear your disgrace and be ashamed of all that you have done, becoming a consolation to them.
text.Rotherham: That thou mayest bear thine own reproach, And take to thyself reproach because of all that thou didst in comforting them.
text.KJV: That thou mayest bear thine own shame, and mayest be confounded in all that thou hast done, in that thou art a comfort unto them.
text.NABRE: Thus you must bear your disgrace and be ashamed of all you have done to bring them comfort.
text.DRB: that thou mayst carry thine ignominy, and mayst be confounded in all things, that thou hast done, comforting them.

text.NRSV: As for your sisters, Sodom and her daughters shall return to their former state, Samaria and her daughters shall return to their former state, and you and your daughters shall return to your former state.
text.Rotherham: When thy sisters Sodom and her daughters shall return to their former estate, And Samaria and her daughters shall return to their former estate Then thou and thy daughters, shall return to your former estate.
text.KJV: When thy sisters, Sodom and her daughters, shall return to their former estate, and Samaria and her daughters shall return to their former estate, then thou and thy daughters shall return to your former estate.
text.NABRE: Yes, your sisters, Sodom and her daughters, Samaria and her daughters, shall return to the way they were, and you and your daughters shall return to the way you were.
text.DRB: And thy sister Sodom, and her daughters shall return to their antiquity: and Samaria, and her daughters shall return to their antiquity: and thou, and thy daughters shall return to your antiquity.

text.NRSV: Was not your sister Sodom a byword in your mouth in the day of your pride,
text.Rotherham: And Sodom thy sister was never heard in thy mouth, —In the day of thy pride:
text.KJV: For thy sister Sodom was not mentioned by thy mouth in the day of thy pride,
text.NABRE: Did you not hold your sister Sodom in bad repute while you felt proud of yourself,
text.DRB: And Sodom thy sister was not heard in thy mouth, in the day of thy pride,

text.NRSV: before your wickedness was uncovered? Now you are a mockery to the daughters of Aram and all her neighbors, and to the daughters of the Philistines, those all around who despise you.
text.Rotherham: Before thy wickedness was discovered, As now, [thou art] the reproach of the daughters of Syria and all round about her the daughters of the Philistines, —who are despising thee on every side.
text.KJV: Before thy wickedness was discovered, as at the time of thy reproach of the daughters of Syria, and all that are round about her, the daughters of the Philistines, which despise thee round about.
text.NABRE: before your evil was exposed? Now you are like her, reproached by the Arameans and all their neighbors, despised on all sides by the Philistines.
text.DRB: before that thy malice was revealed: as at this time for reproach of the daughters of Syria, & of all the daughters of Palesthine in the circuit of thee, which compass thee round about.

text.NRSV: You must bear the penalty of your lewdness and your abominations, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: As for thy crime and thine abominations, thou thyself, dost bear them,— Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: Thou hast borne thy lewdness and thine abominations, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: The penalty of your depravity and your abominations—you must bear it all—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: Thy wickedness, & thine ignominy thou hast carried, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: Yes, thus says the Lord God: I will deal with you as you have done, you who have despised the oath, breaking the covenant;
text.Rotherham: For Thus, saith My Lord, Yahweh, Therefore will I deal with thee just as thou hast dealt,- In that thou didst despise an oath by breaking a covenant.
text.KJV: For thus saith the Lord God; I will even deal with thee as thou hast done, which hast despised the oath in breaking the covenant.
text.NABRE: For thus says the Lord God: I will deal with you for what you did; you despised an oath by breaking a covenant.
text.DRB: Because thus saith our Lord God: And I will do to thee, as thou hast despised the oath, to make the covenant frustrate:

text.NRSV: yet I will remember my covenant with you in the days of your youth, and I will establish with you an everlasting covenant.
text.Rotherham: Therefore will I remember my covenant with thee in the days of thy youth,— And will establish for thee a covenant age-abiding.
text.KJV: Nevertheless I will remember my covenant with thee in the days of thy youth, and I will establish unto thee an everlasting covenant.
text.NABRE: But I will remember the covenant I made with you when you were young; I will set up an everlasting covenant with you.
text.DRB: and I will remember my covenant with thee in the days of thy youth: and I will raise unto thee an everlasting covenant.

text.NRSV: Then you will remember your ways, and be ashamed when I take your sisters, both your elder and your younger, and give them to you as daughters, but not on account of my covenant with you.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt therefore remember thy ways and take to thyself reproach, by receiving thy sisters. The older than thou, And the younger than thou,- And I will give them unto thee for daughters Though not by thine own covenant.
text.KJV: Then thou shalt remember thy ways, and be ashamed, when thou shalt receive thy sisters, thine elder and thy younger: and I will give them unto thee for daughters, but not by thy covenant.
text.NABRE: Then you shall remember your ways and be ashamed when you receive your sisters, those older and younger than you; I give them to you as daughters, but not by reason of your covenant.
text.DRB: And thou shalt remember thy ways, & shalt be confounded: when thou shalt receive thy sisters elder than thee with thy younger: and I will give them to thee for daughters, but not by thy covenant.

text.NRSV: I will establish my covenant with you, and you shall know that I am the Lord,
text.Rotherham: But I myself, will establish my covenant with thee— And thou shalt know that I, am Yahweh:
text.KJV: And I will establish my covenant with thee; and thou shalt know that I am the Lord:
text.NABRE: For I will re-establish my covenant with you, that you may know that I am the Lord,
text.DRB: And I will raise up my covenant with thee: and thou shalt know that I am the Lord,

text.NRSV: in order that you may remember and be confounded, and never open your mouth again because of your shame, when I forgive you all that you have done, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: To the end thou mayest remember and turn pale, and there be to thee no more, an opening of mouth, because of thy reproach,- In that I have accepted a propitiatory covering for thee as to all that thou hast done, Declareth My Lord. Yahweh.
text.KJV: That thou mayest remember, and be confounded, and never open thy mouth any more because of thy shame, when I am pacified toward thee for all that thou hast done, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: that you may remember and be ashamed, and never again open your mouth because of your disgrace, when I pardon you for all you have done—oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: that thou mayst remember, and be confounded, and mayst no more open thy mouth because of thy confusion, when I shall be pacified toward thee in all that thou hast done, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me saying:
text.KJV: And the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: O mortal, propound a riddle, and speak an allegory to the house of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Son of man Put thou forth a riddle And speak thou a parable,- Unto the house of Israel:
text.KJV: Son of man, put forth a riddle, and speak a parable unto the house of Israel;
text.NABRE: Son of man, propose a riddle, and tell this proverb to the house of Israel:
text.DRB: Son of man, propose a dark speech, and tell a parable to the house of Israel,

text.NRSV: Say: Thus says the Lord God: A great eagle, with great wings and long pinions, rich in plumage of many colors, came to the Lebanon. He took the top of the cedar,
text.Rotherham: So then thou shalt say, Thus, saith My Lord. Yahweh,— A great eagle, with large wings of long pinion, full of plumage, which had divers colours, came unto Lebanon, and took the highest branch of the cedar:
text.KJV: And say, Thus saith the Lord God; A great eagle with great wings, longwinged, full of feathers, which had divers colours, came unto Lebanon, and took the highest branch of the cedar:
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord God: The great eagle, with wide wingspan and long feathers, with thick plumage, many-hued, came to Lebanon. He plucked the crest of the cedar,
text.DRB: and thou shalt say: Thus saith our Lord God: A great eagle with great wings, with a long reach of members, full of feathers, and of variety, came to Libanus, and took the marrow of the cedar.

text.NRSV: broke off its topmost shoot; He carried it to a land of trade, set it in a city of merchants.
text.Rotherham: The crown of its young twigs, plucked he off, and brought it into a land of traffic, in a city of merchants, he set it.
text.KJV: He cropped off the top of his young twigs, and carried it into a land of traffick; he set it in a city of merchants.
text.NABRE: broke off its topmost branch, And brought it to a land of merchants, set it in a city of traders.
text.DRB: He plucked away the top of the boughs thereof: and transported it into the Land of Chanaan, in the city of merchants he did put it.

text.NRSV: Then he took a seed from the land, placed it in fertile soil; A plant by abundant waters, he set it like a willow twig.
text.Rotherham: So he took of the seed of the land, and planted it in a field for seed,- He took it near mighty waters, Like a willow-tree, he set it.
text.KJV: He took also of the seed of the land, and planted it in a fruitful field; he placed it by great waters, and set it as a willow tree.
text.NABRE: Then he took some native seed and planted it in fertile soil; A shoot beside plentiful waters, like a willow he planted it,
text.DRB: And he took of the seed of the land, & put it in the ground for seed, that it might fasten the root over many waters: he planted it in the overmost part of the earth.

text.NRSV: It sprouted and became a vine spreading out, but low; Its branches turned toward him, its roots remained where it stood. So it became a vine; it brought forth branches, put forth foliage.
text.Rotherham: And it shot forth, and became a spreading vine of lowly stature, Its branches turning towards him, And its roots under him spread,- So it became a vine, And brought forth shoots, And sent forth boughs.
text.KJV: And it grew, and became a spreading vine of low stature, whose branches turned toward him, and the roots thereof were under him: so it became a vine, and brought forth branches, and shot forth sprigs.
text.NABRE: That it might sprout and become a vine, dense and low-lying, With its branches turned toward him, its roots beneath it. Thus it became a vine, produced branches, and put forth shoots.
text.DRB: And when it had budded, it grew into a broad spreading vine of low stature, the boughs thereof looking toward it: and the roots thereof were under it. It became a vine then, and fructified into branches, and put forth shoots.

text.NRSV: There was another great eagle, with great wings and much plumage. And see! This vine stretched out its roots toward him; It shot out its branches toward him, so that he might water it. From the bed where it was planted
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that there was another great eagle, with large wings, and abundant plumage,- And lo! this vine, stretched its roots hungry towards him. And its branches, sent it forth unto him, That he might cause it to drink out of the beds where it was planted:
text.KJV: There was also another great eagle with great wings and many feathers: and, behold, this vine did bend her roots toward him, and shot forth her branches toward him, that he might water it by the furrows of her plantation.
text.NABRE: Then another great eagle appeared, with wide wingspan, rich in plumage, And see! This vine bent its roots to him, sent out branches for him to water. From the bed where it was planted,
text.DRB: And there was made another great eagle, with great wings, and many feathers: and behold this vine as it were spreading her roots towards it, stretched forth her branches to it, that she might water it from the beds of her spring.

text.NRSV: it was transplanted to good soil by abundant waters, so that it might produce branches and bear fruit and become a noble vine.
text.Rotherham: In a goodly field by mighty waters, was it planted,- That it might bring forth boughs, and bear fruit, That it might become a splendid vine.
text.KJV: It was planted in a good soil by great waters, that it might bring forth branches, and that it might bear fruit, that it might be a goodly vine.
text.NABRE: it was transplanted to a fertile field By abundant waters, to produce branches, to bear fruit, to become a majestic vine.
text.DRB: In a good ground upon many waters it was planted, that it might bring forth branches, & bear fruit, that it might be into a great vine.

text.NRSV: Say: Thus says the Lord God: Will it prosper? Will he not pull up its roots, cause its fruit to rot and wither, its fresh sprouting leaves to fade? No strong arm or mighty army will be needed to pull it from its roots.
text.Rotherham: Say thou, Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Shall it thrive? Its roots, will he not tear up? Its fruit, will he not cut off, that it wither? All the fresh leaves it hath caused to spring forth shall wither without a strong arm or many people, to pull it up by its roots.
text.KJV: Say thou, Thus saith the Lord God; Shall it prosper? shall he not pull up the roots thereof, and cut off the fruit thereof, that it wither? it shall wither in all the leaves of her spring, even without great power or many people to pluck it up by the roots thereof.
text.NABRE: Say: Thus says the Lord God: Can it thrive? Will he not tear up its roots and strip its fruit? Then all its green leaves will wither— neither strong arm nor mighty nation is needed to uproot it.
text.DRB: Say: Thus saith our Lord God: shall it prosper then? Shall he not pluck up the roots thereof, & strip off the fruits thereof, dry up all the branches of the spring thereof, & it shall wither: & not in a great arm, nor in much people, to pluck it up by the root?

text.NRSV: When it is transplanted, will it thrive? When the east wind strikes it, will it not utterly wither, wither on the bed where it grew?
text.Rotherham: Lo! therefore though [it remain] planted, shall it thrive? As soon as an east wind toucheth it, will it not utterly wither? On the beds where it sprang up, will it not wither?
text.KJV: Yea, behold, being planted, shall it prosper? shall it not utterly wither, when the east wind toucheth it? it shall wither in the furrows where it grew.
text.NABRE: True, it is planted; but will it thrive? Will it not wither up When the east wind strikes it, wither in the very bed where it sprouted?
text.DRB: Behold it is planted: shall it prosper them? Shall it not be dried when the burning wind shall touch it, and wither in the beds of the spring thereof?

text.NRSV: Then the word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me saying:
text.KJV: Moreover the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me saying:

text.NRSV: Say now to the rebellious house: Do you not know what these things mean? Tell them: The king of Babylon came to Jerusalem, took its king and its officials, and brought them back with him to Babylon.
text.Rotherham: Say, I pray thee unto the perverse house, Know ye not what these things are? Say thou Lo! the King of Babylon entered Jerusalem And took her king and her princes, And brought them unto him in Babylon;
text.KJV: Say now to the rebellious house, Know ye not what these things mean? tell them, Behold, the king of Babylon is come to Jerusalem, and hath taken the king thereof, and the princes thereof, and led them with him to Babylon;
text.NABRE: Now say to the rebellious house: Do you not understand this? Tell them! The king of Babylon came to Jerusalem and took away its king and officials and brought them to him in Babylon.
text.DRB: Say to the exasperating house: Know you not what these things do signify? Say: Behold the king of Babylon cometh into Jerusalem: and he shall take the king and the princes thereof, and shall bring them to himself into Babylon.

text.NRSV: He took one of the royal offspring and made a covenant with him, putting him under oath (he had taken away the chief men of the land),
text.Rotherham: Yea took of the seed royal And solemnised with him a covenant, And brought him into an oath, Also the mighty ones of the land, did he take.
text.KJV: And hath taken of the king’s seed, and made a covenant with him, and hath taken an oath of him: he hath also taken the mighty of the land:
text.NABRE: After removing the nobles from the land, he then took one of the royal line And made a covenant with him, binding him under oath,
text.DRB: And he shall take of the seed of the kingdom, and shall make a covenant with it, and shall take an oath of it. Yea and he shall take away the strong of the land,

text.NRSV: so that the kingdom might be humble and not lift itself up, and that by keeping his covenant it might stand.
text.Rotherham: That, the kingdom might be abased, so as not to lift itself up,— By the keeping of his covenant, might be made to stand.
text.KJV: That the kingdom might be base, that it might not lift itself up, but that by keeping of his covenant it might stand.
text.NABRE: To be a humble kingdom, without high aspirations, to keep his covenant and so survive.
text.DRB: that it may be a low kingdom, & not be elevated, but keep his covenant, and observe it.

text.NRSV: But he rebelled against him by sending ambassadors to Egypt, in order that they might give him horses and a large army. Will he succeed? Can one escape who does such things? Can he break the covenant and yet escape?
text.Rotherham: But he hath rebelled against him by sending his messengers to Egypt, that there should be given to him horses, and much people. Shall he thrive? Shall he escape that doeth these things? Shall he break a covenant and escape?
text.KJV: But he rebelled against him in sending his ambassadors into Egypt, that they might give him horses and much people. Shall he prosper? shall he escape that doeth such things? or shall he break the covenant, and be delivered?
text.NABRE: But this one rebelled against him by sending envoys to Egypt To obtain horses and a mighty army. Can he thrive? Can he escape if he does this? Can he break a covenant and go free?
text.DRB: Who revolting from him sent messengers to Ægypt, that it would give him horses, and much people. What, shall he prosper, or get salvation that hath done these things? And he that broke the covenant, shall he escape?

text.NRSV: As I live, says the Lord God, surely in the place where the king resides who made him king, whose oath he despised, and whose covenant with him he broke—in Babylon he shall die.
text.Rotherham: As I live, Declareth My Lord Yahweh, Very! in the place where dwelleth the king that made him king, Whose oath he hath despised, And whose covenant he hath broken With him in the midst of Babylon, shall he die.
text.KJV: As I live, saith the Lord God, surely in the place where the king dwelleth that made him king, whose oath he despised, and whose covenant he brake, even with him in the midst of Babylon he shall die.
text.NABRE: As I live—oracle of the Lord GOD— in the house of the king who set him up to rule, Whose oath he ignored and whose covenant he broke, there in Babylon I swear he shall die!
text.DRB: Live I, saith our Lord God: that in the place of the king that made him king, whose oath he made frustrate, and brake the covenant, that he had with him, in the midst of Babylon shall he die.

text.NRSV: Pharaoh with his mighty army and great company will not help him in war, when ramps are cast up and siege walls built to cut off many lives.
text.Rotherham: Neither shall Pharaoh with a great force. or with a large gathered host, work with him in the war, by casting up an earthwork and by building a siege-wall, —to the cuting off of many lives.
text.KJV: Neither shall Pharaoh with his mighty army and great company make for him in the war, by casting up mounts, and building forts, to cut off many persons:
text.NABRE: Pharaoh shall not help him on the day of battle, with a great force and mighty horde, When ramps are thrown up and siege works built for the cutting down of many lives.
text.DRB: And not in a great army, nor in much people shall Pharao make battle against him: in the casting up of a ramper, and in the building of bulwarks, to kill many souls.

text.NRSV: Because he despised the oath and broke the covenant, because he gave his hand and yet did all these things, he shall not escape.
text.Rotherham: Seeing he hath despised an oath. by breaking a covenant—yea lo! hath given his hand and all these things hath done he shall not escape.
text.KJV: Seeing he despised the oath by breaking the covenant, when, lo, he had given his hand, and hath done all these things, he shall not escape.
text.NABRE: He ignored his oath, breaking his covenant; even though he gave his hand, he did all these things— he shall not escape!
text.DRB: For he had despised the oath, that he might break the covenant, and behold he gave his hand, & when he hath done all these things, he shall not escape.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God: As I live, I will surely return upon his head my oath that he despised, and my covenant that he broke.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus saith My Lord Yahweh As I live, Surely it is mine oath which he hath despised, and my covenant which he hath broken, Therefore will I bring it upon his own head:
text.KJV: Therefore thus saith the Lord God; As I live, surely mine oath that he hath despised, and my covenant that he hath broken, even it will I recompense upon his own head.
text.NABRE: Therefore, thus says the Lord God: As I live, my oath which he spurned, And my covenant which he broke, I will bring down on his head.
text.DRB: Therefore thus saith our Lord God: Live I, that the oath which he hath despised, and the covenant, that he transgressed, I will lay on his head.

text.NRSV: I will spread my net over him, and he shall be caught in my snare; I will bring him to Babylon and enter into judgment with him there for the treason he has committed against me.
text.Rotherham: And I will spread over him my net, And he shall be taken in my snare, And I will bring him into Babylon and will enter into judgment with him there, as to his treachery wherewith he hath been treacherous against me;
text.KJV: And I will spread my net upon him, and he shall be taken in my snare, and I will bring him to Babylon, and will plead with him there for his trespass that he hath trespassed against me.
text.NABRE: I will spread my net over him, and he will be caught in my snare. I will bring him to Babylon to judge him there because he broke faith with me.
text.DRB: And I will spread my net over him, and he shall be taken in my net: and I will bring him into Babylon, and will judge him there in the prevarication, whereby he hath despised me.

text.NRSV: All the pick of his troops shall fall by the sword, and the survivors shall be scattered to every wind; and you shall know that I, the Lord, have spoken.
text.Rotherham: And all his fugitives throughout all his bands by the sword, shall fall, And they who are left, to every wind, shall be scattered,- [So shall ye know that I, Yahweh, have spoken!]
text.KJV: And all his fugitives with all his bands shall fall by the sword, and they that remain shall be scattered toward all winds: and ye shall know that I the Lord have spoken it.
text.NABRE: Any among his forces who escape will fall by the sword, And whoever might survive will be scattered to the winds. Thus you will know that I the Lord have spoken.
text.DRB: And all his fugitives with all their troop shall fall by the sword: and the residue shall be dispersed into every wind: and you shall know that I the Lord have spoken.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: I myself will take a sprig from the lofty top of a cedar; I will set it out. I will break off a tender one from the topmost of its young twigs; I myself will plant it on a high and lofty mountain.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Therefore will I, Myself, take of the highest branch of the lofty cedar and set it,— Of the crown of its young twigs, a tender one, will I pluck off, and I. myself will plant it upon a mountain high and terraced,
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord God; I will also take of the highest branch of the high cedar, and will set it; I will crop off from the top of his young twigs a tender one, and will plant it upon an high mountain and eminent:
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord God: I, too, will pluck from the crest of the cedar the highest branch. From the top a tender shoot I will break off and transplant on a high, lofty mountain.
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord God: And I will take of the marrow of the high cedar, and will set it: of the top of the boughs thereof the tender one I will strip off, and will plant it upon a mountain high and eminent.

text.NRSV: On the mountain height of Israel I will plant it, in order that it may produce boughs and bear fruit, and become a noble cedar. Under it every kind of bird will live; in the shade of its branches will nest winged creatures of every kind.
text.Rotherham: In the mountain of the height of Israel, will I plant it, And it shall put forth boughs And bear fruit, And become a majestic cedar,- And every bird of every wing shall dwell under it, In the shade of its branches, shall they dwell.
