text.KJV: In the mountain of the height of Israel will I plant it: and it shall bring forth boughs, and bear fruit, and be a goodly cedar: and under it shall dwell all fowl of every wing; in the shadow of the branches thereof shall they dwell.
text.NABRE: On the mountain height of Israel I will plant it. It shall put forth branches and bear fruit, and become a majestic cedar. Every small bird will nest under it, all kinds of winged birds will dwell in the shade of its branches.
text.DRB: On the high mountains of Israel will I plant it, and it shall shoot forth into a bud, and shall yield fruit, and it shall be into a great cedar: and all birds, and every fowl shall dwell under the shadow of the boughs thereof, and shall there make their nest.

text.NRSV: All the trees of the field shall know that I am the Lord. I bring low the high tree, I make high the low tree; I dry up the green tree and make the dry tree flourish. I the Lord have spoken; I will accomplish it.
text.Rotherham: So shall all the trees of the field know that I Yahweh Have laid low the high tree Have exalted the low tree, Have dried up the moist tree, And have caused to flourish the tree, that was dry,— I, Yahweh, have spoken, and performed.
text.KJV: And all the trees of the field shall know that I the Lord have brought down the high tree, have exalted the low tree, have dried up the green tree, and have made the dry tree to flourish: I the Lord have spoken and have done it.
text.NABRE: Every tree of the field will know that I am the Lord. I bring low the high tree, lift high the lowly tree, Wither up the green tree, and make the dry tree bloom. As I, the Lord, have spoken, so will I do!
text.DRB: And all the trees of the country shall know, that I the Lord have humbled the high tree, & exalted the low tree: and have dried the green tree, and have caused the dry tree to spring, I the Lord have spoken, and have done it.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me saying:
text.KJV: The word of the Lord came unto me again, saying,
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord came to me: Son of man,
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: What do you mean by repeating this proverb concerning the land of Israel, “The parents have eaten sour grapes, and the children’s teeth are set on edge”?
text.Rotherham: What occasion have ye, to be using this proverb, concerning the soil of Israel, saying, —Fathers eat sour grapes, And the children’s teeth are blunted?
text.KJV: What mean ye, that ye use this proverb concerning the land of Israel, saying, The fathers have eaten sour grapes, and the children’s teeth are set on edge?
text.NABRE: what is the meaning of this proverb you recite in the land of Israel: “Parents eat sour grapes, but the children’s teeth are set on edge”?
text.DRB: What is that among you, you turn a parable into this proverb in the land of Israel, saying: The fathers did eat a sour grape, & the teeth of the children are set on edge?

text.NRSV: As I live, says the Lord God, this proverb shall no more be used by you in Israel.
text.Rotherham: As I live, Declareth My Lord, Yahweh, Surely ye shall have occasion no longer to use this proverb, in Israel.
text.KJV: As I live, saith the Lord God, ye shall not have occasion any more to use this proverb in Israel.
text.NABRE: As I live—oracle of the Lord God: I swear that none of you will ever repeat this proverb in Israel.
text.DRB: Live I, saith our Lord God, if this parable shall be unto you any more for a proverb in Israel.

text.NRSV: Know that all lives are mine; the life of the parent as well as the life of the child is mine: it is only the person who sins that shall die.
text.Rotherham: Lo! as for all persons, mine they are, As the person of the father, so also the person of the son, mine they are,—The person that sinneth, the same shall die.
text.KJV: Behold, all souls are mine; as the soul of the father, so also the soul of the son is mine: the soul that sinneth, it shall die.
text.NABRE: For all life is mine: the life of the parent is like the life of the child, both are mine. Only the one who sins shall die!
text.DRB: Behold all souls are mine: as the soul of the father, so also the soul of the son is mine: the soul that shall sin, the same shall die.

text.NRSV: If a man is righteous and does what is lawful and right—
text.Rotherham: But when, any man, shall be righteous, and do justice and righteousness:
text.KJV: But if a man be just, and do that which is lawful and right,
text.NABRE: If a man is just—if he does what is right,
text.DRB: And a man if he shall be just, and hath done judgement and justice,

text.NRSV: if he does not eat upon the mountains or lift up his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel, does not defile his neighbor’s wife or approach a woman during her menstrual period,
text.Rotherham: Upon the mountains, hath not eaten, And his eyes, hath not lifted up unto the manufactured gods of the house of Israel, And the wife of his neighbour, hath not defiled, And unto a woman during her removal, hath not approached;
text.KJV: And hath not eaten upon the mountains, neither hath lifted up his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel, neither hath defiled his neighbour’s wife, neither hath come near to a menstruous woman,
text.NABRE: if he does not eat on the mountains, or raise his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel; if he does not defile a neighbor’s wife, or have relations with a woman during her period;
text.DRB: hath not eaten on the mountains, and not lifted up his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel: and hath not violated his neighbour's wife, & approached not to a menstruous woman:

text.NRSV: does not oppress anyone, but restores to the debtor his pledge, commits no robbery, gives his bread to the hungry and covers the naked with a garment,
text.Rotherham: And no man, hath treated with violence, But his debt-pledge, hath restored. Plunder, hath not seized,— His bread—to the famished, hath given, And the naked, hath covered with clothing;
text.KJV: And hath not oppressed any, but hath restored to the debtor his pledge, hath spoiled none by violence, hath given his bread to the hungry, and hath covered the naked with a garment;
text.NABRE: if he oppresses no one, gives back the pledge received for a debt, commits no robbery; gives food to the hungry and clothes the naked;
text.DRB: and made no man sorrowful: hath restored the pledge to the debter, taken nothing away by violence: hath given his bread to the hungry, and covered the naked with a garment:

text.NRSV: does not take advance or accrued interest, withholds his hand from iniquity, executes true justice between contending parties,
text.Rotherham: Upon interest, hath not put out his money. And increase, hath not accepted, From dishonesty, hath turned back his hand,— Justice in truth, hath done, between man and man;
text.KJV: He that hath not given forth upon usury, neither hath taken any increase, that hath withdrawn his hand from iniquity, hath executed true judgment between man and man,
text.NABRE: if he does not lend at interest or exact usury; if he refrains from evildoing and makes a fair judgment between two opponents;
text.DRB: hath not lent to usury, and not taken more: hath turned away his hand from iniquity, and done true judgement between man & man:

text.NRSV: follows my statutes, and is careful to observe my ordinances, acting faithfully—such a one is righteous; he shall surely live, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: In my statutes, hath walked. And my regulations, hath observed to do them in truth, Righteous, he is, He shall surely live, Declareth My Lord. Yahweh.
text.KJV: Hath walked in my statutes, and hath kept my judgments, to deal truly; he is just, he shall surely live, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: if he walks by my statutes and is careful to observe my ordinances, that man is just—he shall surely live—oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: hath walked in my precepts, and kept my judgements, to do truth: this man is just, living he shall live, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: If he has a son who is violent, a shedder of blood,
text.Rotherham: But he hath begotten a son Who is a violent man A shedder of blood,- Who doeth the like of any of these things;
text.KJV: If he beget a son that is a robber, a shedder of blood, and that doeth the like to any one of these things,
text.NABRE: But if he begets a son who is violent and commits murder, or does any of these things,
text.DRB: And if he hath begotten a son that is a robber shedding blood, and he do one of these things:

text.NRSV: who does any of these things (though his father does none of them), who eats upon the mountains, defiles his neighbor’s wife,
text.Rotherham: Whereas he, none of those other things, doeth, For indeed upon the mountains, he hath eaten, And the wife of his neighbour, hath defiled;
text.KJV: And that doeth not any of those duties, but even hath eaten upon the mountains, and defiled his neighbour’s wife,
text.NABRE: even though the father does none of them—a son who eats on the mountains, defiles the wife of his neighbor,
text.DRB: and that doth not indeed all these things, but eateth on the mountains, and defileth his neighbour's wife:

text.NRSV: oppresses the poor and needy, commits robbery, does not restore the pledge, lifts up his eyes to the idols, commits abomination,
text.Rotherham: The oppressed and the needy, hath treated with violence, Hath ruthlessly plundered, The pledge, hath not restored, But unto the manufactured gods, hath lifted up his eyes, Abomination, hath wrought;
text.KJV: Hath oppressed the poor and needy, hath spoiled by violence, hath not restored the pledge, and hath lifted up his eyes to the idols, hath committed abomination,
text.NABRE: oppresses the poor and needy, commits robbery, does not give back a pledge, raises his eyes to idols, does abominable things,
text.DRB: that maketh the needy and poor sorrowful, violently committeth robberies, restoreth not the pledge, & lifteth his eyes to idols, doth abomination:

text.NRSV: takes advance or accrued interest; shall he then live? He shall not. He has done all these abominable things; he shall surely die; his blood shall be upon himself.
text.Rotherham: Upon interest, hath put out. And increase, hath accepted—, And shall he live? He shall not live All these abominations, he hath done, He shall surely be put to death, His blood, upon himself, shall be.
text.KJV: Hath given forth upon usury, and hath taken increase: shall he then live? he shall not live: he hath done all these abominations; he shall surely die; his blood shall be upon him.
text.NABRE: lends at interest and exacts usury—this son certainly shall not live. Because he practiced all these abominations, he shall surely be put to death; his own blood shall be on him.
text.DRB: that giveth to usury, & taketh more: what, shall he live? He shall not live. Whereas he hath done all these detestable things, dying he shall die, his blood shall be upon him.

text.NRSV: But if this man has a son who sees all the sins that his father has done, considers, and does not do likewise,
text.Rotherham: But lo! he hath begotten a son, Who hath considered all the sins of his father which he hath done,- Yea he hath considered and not done like them:
text.KJV: Now, lo, if he beget a son, that seeth all his father’s sins which he hath done, and considereth, and doeth not such like,
text.NABRE: But, in turn, if he begets a son who sees all the sins his father commits, yet fears and does not imitate him—
text.DRB: But and if he hath begotten a son, that seeing all his father's sins, which he hath done, is afraid, and shall not do the like to them:

text.NRSV: who does not eat upon the mountains or lift up his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel, does not defile his neighbor’s wife,
text.Rotherham: Upon the mountains, hath not eaten, And his eyes, hath not lifted up unto the manufactured gods of the house of Israel, The wife of his neighbour, hath not defiled;
text.KJV: That hath not eaten upon the mountains, neither hath lifted up his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel, hath not defiled his neighbour’s wife,
text.NABRE: a son who does not eat on the mountains, or raise his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel, or defile a neighbor’s wife;
text.DRB: hath not eaten upon the mountains, & not lifted up his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel, & hath not violated his neighbour's wife:

text.NRSV: does not wrong anyone, exacts no pledge, commits no robbery, but gives his bread to the hungry and covers the naked with a garment,
text.Rotherham: And no man, hath he treated with violence, Hath by no means withholden the pledge, And plunder, hath not seized,— His bread—to the famished, hath given And the naked, hath covered with clothing;
text.KJV: Neither hath oppressed any, hath not withholden the pledge, neither hath spoiled by violence, but hath given his bread to the hungry, and hath covered the naked with a garment,
text.NABRE: who does not oppress anyone, or exact a pledge, or commit robbery; who gives his food to the hungry and clothes the naked;
text.DRB: and hath made no man sorrowful, hath not withheld the pledge, and hath not violently committed robbery, hath given his bread to the hungry, and covered the naked with clothing:

text.NRSV: withholds his hand from iniquity, takes no advance or accrued interest, observes my ordinances, and follows my statutes; he shall not die for his father’s iniquity; he shall surely live.
text.Rotherham: From dishonesty, hath turned back his hand Neither interest nor increase, hath accepted, my regulations, hath executed, In my statutes, hath walked,— He shall not die for the iniquity of his father He shall surely live.
text.KJV: That hath taken off his hand from the poor, that hath not received usury nor increase, hath executed my judgments, hath walked in my statutes; he shall not die for the iniquity of his father, he shall surely live.
text.NABRE: who refrains from evildoing, accepts no interest or usury, but keeps my ordinances and walks in my statutes—this one shall not die for the sins of his father. He shall surely live!
text.DRB: hath turned away his hand from injury of the poor, hath not taken usury and overplus, hath done my judgements, hath walked in my precepts: this man shall not die in the iniquity of his father, but living he shall live.

text.NRSV: As for his father, because he practiced extortion, robbed his brother, and did what is not good among his people, he dies for his iniquity.
text.Rotherham: His father Because he exacted unjust gain Seized plunder of a brother, And that which was not good, had done in the midst of his people Therefore lo! he died, in his iniquity.
text.KJV: As for his father, because he cruelly oppressed, spoiled his brother by violence, and did that which is not good among his people, lo, even he shall die in his iniquity.
text.NABRE: Only the father, since he committed extortion and robbed his brother, and did what was not good among his people—he will die because of his sin!
text.DRB: His father because he did calumniate, and did violence to his brother, and wrought evil in the midst of his people, behold he is dead in his own iniquity.

text.NRSV: Yet you say, “Why should not the son suffer for the iniquity of the father?” When the son has done what is lawful and right, and has been careful to observe all my statutes, he shall surely live.
text.Rotherham: Will ye then say. Why hath not the son borne a part of the iniquity of the father? But the son hath done justice and righteousness, All my statutes, hath observed and done them He shall surely live.
text.KJV: Yet say ye, Why? doth not the son bear the iniquity of the father? When the son hath done that which is lawful and right, and hath kept all my statutes, and hath done them, he shall surely live.
text.NABRE: You ask: “Why is not the son charged with the guilt of his father?” Because the son has done what is just and right and has been careful to observe all my statutes—he shall surely live!
text.DRB: And you say: Why hath not the son borne the iniquity of his father? Verily because the son hath wrought judgement and justice, he hath kept all my precepts, and done them, living he shall live.

text.NRSV: The person who sins shall die. A child shall not suffer for the iniquity of a parent, nor a parent suffer for the iniquity of a child; the righteousness of the righteous shall be his own, and the wickedness of the wicked shall be his own.
text.Rotherham: The person that sinneth, the same, shall die,— A, son, shall not bear a part of the iniquity of, the father Neither shall, a father, bear a part of the iniquity of the son, the righteousness of the righteous, upon himself, shall be. And, the lawlessness of a lawless man, upon himself, shall be.
text.KJV: The soul that sinneth, it shall die. The son shall not bear the iniquity of the father, neither shall the father bear the iniquity of the son: the righteousness of the righteous shall be upon him, and the wickedness of the wicked shall be upon him.
text.NABRE: Only the one who sins shall die. The son shall not be charged with the guilt of his father, nor shall the father be charged with the guilt of his son. Justice belongs to the just, and wickedness to the wicked.
text.DRB: The soul that shall sin, the same shall die: the son shall not bear the iniquity of the father, and the father shall not bear the iniquity of the son: the justice of the just shall be upon him, and the impiety of the impious shall be upon him.

text.NRSV: But if the wicked turn away from all their sins that they have committed and keep all my statutes and do what is lawful and right, they shall surely live; they shall not die.
text.Rotherham: But as for the lawless man— When he shall turn back from all his sins which he hath committed, And observe all my statutes, And do justice and righteousness He shall surely live. He shall not die:
text.KJV: But if the wicked will turn from all his sins that he hath committed, and keep all my statutes, and do that which is lawful and right, he shall surely live, he shall not die.
text.NABRE: But if the wicked man turns away from all the sins he has committed, if he keeps all my statutes and does what is just and right, he shall surely live. He shall not die!
text.DRB: But if the impious shall do penance from all his sins, which he hath wrought, and shall keep all my precepts, and do judgement, and justice: living he shall live, and shall not die.

text.NRSV: None of the transgressions that they have committed shall be remembered against them; for the righteousness that they have done they shall live.
text.Rotherham: None of his transgressions which he hath committed shall be remembered against him, —In his righteousness which he hath done, he shall live.
text.KJV: All his transgressions that he hath committed, they shall not be mentioned unto him: in his righteousness that he hath done he shall live.
text.NABRE: None of the crimes he has committed shall be remembered against him; he shall live because of the justice he has shown.
text.DRB: All his iniquities, which he hath wrought, I will not remember them: in his justice which he hath wrought, he shall live.

text.NRSV: Have I any pleasure in the death of the wicked, says the Lord God, and not rather that they should turn from their ways and live?
text.Rotherham: Could I take delight, in the death of the lawless? Demandeth My Lord. Yahweh. Must it not be in his turning from his ways in which case he shall live?
text.KJV: Have I any pleasure at all that the wicked should die? saith the Lord God: and not that he should return from his ways, and live?
text.NABRE: Do I find pleasure in the death of the wicked—oracle of the Lord God? Do I not rejoice when they turn from their evil way and live?
text.DRB: Why, is the death of a sinner my will, saith our Lord God, and not that he convert from his ways, and live?

text.NRSV: But when the righteous turn away from their righteousness and commit iniquity and do the same abominable things that the wicked do, shall they live? None of the righteous deeds that they have done shall be remembered; for the treachery of which they are guilty and the sin they have committed, they shall die.
text.Rotherham: But, when a righteous man, shall turn away, from his righteousness and do that which is perverse, Shall do, according to all the abominations which the lawless man hath done, Shall he live? None of his righteous acts which he hath done, shall be remembered, In his treachery wherein he hath been treacherous, And in his sin wherein he hath sinned, In them, shall he die.
text.KJV: But when the righteous turneth away from his righteousness, and committeth iniquity, and doeth according to all the abominations that the wicked man doeth, shall he live? All his righteousness that he hath done shall not be mentioned: in his trespass that he hath trespassed, and in his sin that he hath sinned, in them shall he die.
text.NABRE: And if the just turn from justice and do evil, like all the abominations the wicked do, can they do this evil and still live? None of the justice they did shall be remembered, because they acted treacherously and committed these sins; because of this, they shall die.
text.DRB: But if the just man shall turn away himself from his justice, and do iniquity according to all the abominations, which the impious useth to work, shall he live? All his justices, which he had done, shall not be remembered: in the prevarication, which he hath prevaricated, and in his sin, which he hath sinned, in them he shall die.

text.NRSV: Yet you say, “The way of the Lord is unfair.” Hear now, O house of Israel: Is my way unfair? Is it not your ways that are unfair?
text.Rotherham: Can ye then say— The way of My Lord will not be equal? Hear I pray you O house of Israel, Will my way, not be equal? Will not your ways be unequal?
text.KJV: Yet ye say, The way of the Lord is not equal. Hear now, O house of Israel; Is not my way equal? are not your ways unequal?
text.NABRE: You say, “The LORD’s way is not fair!” Hear now, house of Israel: Is it my way that is unfair? Are not your ways unfair?
text.DRB: And you have said: The way of our Lord is not right. Hear ye therefore, o house of Israel: What, is not my way right, and are not rather your ways perverse?

text.NRSV: When the righteous turn away from their righteousness and commit iniquity, they shall die for it; for the iniquity that they have committed they shall die.
text.Rotherham: When a righteous man shall turn away from his righteousness and do that which is perverse and die because of those things In his own perversity which he hath done, shall he die.
text.KJV: When a righteous man turneth away from his righteousness, and committeth iniquity, and dieth in them; for his iniquity that he hath done shall he die.
text.NABRE: When the just turn away from justice to do evil and die, on account of the evil they did they must die.
text.DRB: For when the just shall turn away himself from his justice, and doth iniquity, he shall die in them: in the injustice, that he hath wrought he shall die.

text.NRSV: Again, when the wicked turn away from the wickedness they have committed and do what is lawful and right, they shall save their life.
text.Rotherham: But when a lawless man turneth away from his lawlessness which he hath done, And hath done justice and righteousness He, shall save his own soul, alive:
text.KJV: Again, when the wicked man turneth away from his wickedness that he hath committed, and doeth that which is lawful and right, he shall save his soul alive.
text.NABRE: But if the wicked turn from the wickedness they did and do what is right and just, they save their lives;
text.DRB: And when the impious shall turn away himself from his impiety, which he hath wrought, and shall do judgement and justice he shall vivificate his soul.

text.NRSV: Because they considered and turned away from all the transgressions that they had committed, they shall surely live; they shall not die.
text.Rotherham: When he considered, then he turned away from all his transgressions which he had committed, He shall surely live. He shall not die.
text.KJV: Because he considereth, and turneth away from all his transgressions that he hath committed, he shall surely live, he shall not die.
text.NABRE: since they turned away from all the sins they committed, they shall live; they shall not die.
text.DRB: For considering, and turning away himself from all his iniquities, which he hath wrought, living he shall live, and not die.

text.NRSV: Yet the house of Israel says, “The way of the Lord is unfair.” O house of Israel, are my ways unfair? Is it not your ways that are unfair?
text.Rotherham: Can the house of Israel then say- The way of My Lord will not be equal? Will my ways, not be equal O house of Israel? Will not your ways be unequal?
text.KJV: Yet saith the house of Israel, The way of the Lord is not equal. O house of Israel, are not my ways equal? are not your ways unequal?
text.NABRE: But the house of Israel says, “The Lord’s way is not fair!” Is it my way that is not fair, house of Israel? Is it not your ways that are not fair?
text.DRB: And the children of Israel say: The way of our Lord is not right. What, are not my ways right, o house of Israel, and not rather your ways perverse?

text.NRSV: Therefore I will judge you, O house of Israel, all of you according to your ways, says the Lord God. Repent and turn from all your transgressions; otherwise iniquity will be your ruin.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Every man according to his own ways, will I judge you O house of Israel, Declareth My Lord Yahweh, Return ye—and make good your return—from all your transgressions, That they become not unto you a stumbling block of iniquity.
text.KJV: Therefore I will judge you, O house of Israel, every one according to his ways, saith the Lord God. Repent, and turn yourselves from all your transgressions; so iniquity shall not be your ruin.
text.NABRE: Therefore I will judge you, house of Israel, all of you according to your ways—oracle of the Lord God. Turn, turn back from all your crimes, that they may not be a cause of sin for you ever again.
text.DRB: Therefore will I judge every man according to his ways, o house of Israel, saith our Lord God. Convert, and do penance from all your iniquities: & iniquity shall not be a ruin to you.

text.NRSV: Cast away from you all the transgressions that you have committed against me, and get yourselves a new heart and a new spirit! Why will you die, O house of Israel?
text.Rotherham: Cast off from you all your transgressions which ye have committed against me, And make you a new heart; And a new spirit,- For why should ye die, O house of Israel?
text.KJV: Cast away from you all your transgressions, whereby ye have transgressed; and make you a new heart and a new spirit: for why will ye die, O house of Israel?
text.NABRE: Cast away from you all the crimes you have committed, and make for yourselves a new heart and a new spirit. Why should you die, house of Israel?
text.DRB: Cast away from you all your prevarications, wherein you have prevaricated, and make to yourselves a new heart, and a new spirit: and why will you die, o house of Israel?

text.NRSV: For I have no pleasure in the death of anyone, says the Lord God. Turn, then, and live.
text.Rotherham: For I cannot take delight in the death of him that dieth, Declareth My Lord Yahweh, Make good your return then and, live!
text.KJV: For I have no pleasure in the death of him that dieth, saith the Lord God: wherefore turn yourselves, and live ye.
text.NABRE: For I find no pleasure in the death of anyone who dies—oracle of the Lord God. Turn back and live!
text.DRB: Because I will not the death of him that dieth, saith our Lord God, return ye, and live.

text.NRSV: As for you, raise up a lamentation for the princes of Israel,
text.Rotherham: Thou. therefore take up a dirge, for the prince of Israel;
text.KJV: Moreover take thou up a lamentation for the princes of Israel,
text.NABRE: As for you, raise a lamentation over the princes of Israel,
text.DRB: And thou take up lamentation upon the Princes of Israel,

text.NRSV: and say: What a lioness was your mother among lions! She lay down among young lions, rearing her cubs.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt say— How was thy mother a lioness, Between lions, she lay down,- In the midst of young lions, She nourished her whelps;
text.KJV: And say, What is thy mother? A lioness: she lay down among lions, she nourished her whelps among young lions.
text.NABRE: and say: What a lioness was your mother, a lion among lions! She made her lair among young lions, to raise her cubs;
text.DRB: and thou shalt say: Why lay thy mother a lioness among the lions, in the midst of young lions brought up her whelps?

text.NRSV: She raised up one of her cubs; he became a young lion, and he learned to catch prey; he devoured humans.
text.Rotherham: And she reared up one of her whelps— A young lion, he became, And he learned to rend prey. Men, he devoured.
text.KJV: And she brought up one of her whelps: it became a young lion, and it learned to catch the prey; it devoured men.
text.NABRE: One cub she raised up, a young lion he became; He learned to tear apart prey, he devoured people.
text.DRB: And she brought out one of her young lions, he became a lion: and he learned to catch preys, and to eat man.

text.NRSV: The nations sounded an alarm against him; he was caught in their pit; and they brought him with hooks to the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Then nations heard of him. In their pit, was he caught,— So they brought him with hooks. Into the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: The nations also heard of him; he was taken in their pit, and they brought him with chains unto the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: Nations heard about him; in their pit he was caught; They took him away with hooks to the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: And the Gentiles heard of him, and not without their wounds they took him: & they brought him in chains into the Land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: When she saw that she was thwarted, that her hope was lost, she took another of her cubs and made him a young lion.
text.Rotherham: And she saw she had waited Lost was her hope, Then took she another of her whelps. A young lion, she made him.
text.KJV: Now when she saw that she had waited, and her hope was lost, then she took another of her whelps, and made him a young lion.
text.NABRE: When she realized she had waited in vain, she lost hope. She took another of her cubs, and made him a young lion.
text.DRB: Who when she saw that she was weakened, and her expectation was lost, she took one of her young lions, she made him a lion.

text.NRSV: He prowled among the lions; he became a young lion, and he learned to catch prey; he devoured people.
text.Rotherham: Yea he went to and fro amidst lions A young lion, he became,- And he learned to rend prey. Men, he devoured;
text.KJV: And he went up and down among the lions, he became a young lion, and learned to catch the prey, and devoured men.
text.NABRE: He prowled among the lions, became a young lion; He learned to tear apart prey, he devoured people.
text.DRB: Who went among the lions, & became a lion: and he learned to take prey, & to devour men.

text.NRSV: And he ravaged their strongholds, and laid waste their towns; the land was appalled, and all in it, at the sound of his roaring.
text.Rotherham: And he injured his widows, And their cities, laid waste,- And deserted was the land and its fulness, At the noise of his roaring.
text.KJV: And he knew their desolate palaces, and he laid waste their cities; and the land was desolate, and the fulness thereof, by the noise of his roaring.
text.NABRE: He ravaged their strongholds, laid waste their cities. The earth and everything in it were terrified at the sound of his roar.
text.DRB: He learned to make widows, and to bring their cities into a desert: and the land was made desolate, and the fullness thereof by the voice of his roaring.

text.NRSV: The nations set upon him from the provinces all around; they spread their net over him; he was caught in their pit.
text.Rotherham: Then set upon him the nations round about. From the provinces,- And spread over him their net In their pit, was he caught;
text.KJV: Then the nations set against him on every side from the provinces, and spread their net over him: he was taken in their pit.
text.NABRE: Nations laid out against him snares all around; They spread their net for him, in their pit he was caught.
text.DRB: And the Gentiles came together against him on every side out of the Provinces, and they spread their net upon him, in their wounds he was taken.

text.NRSV: With hooks they put him in a cage, and brought him to the king of Babylon; they brought him into custody, so that his voice should be heard no more on the mountains of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And they put him in a cage with hooks, And brought him to Babylon’s king,— They brought him into strong-holds, That his noise might be heard no more Among the mountains of Israel.
text.KJV: And they put him in ward in chains, and brought him to the king of Babylon: they brought him into holds, that his voice should no more be heard upon the mountains of Israel.
text.NABRE: They put him in fetters and took him away to the king of Babylon, So his roar would no longer be heard on the mountains of Israel.
text.DRB: And they put him into a cave, in chains they brought him to the king of Babylon: and they cast him into prison, that his voice might no more be heard upon the mountains of Israel.

text.NRSV: Your mother was like a vine in a vineyard transplanted by the water, fruitful and full of branches from abundant water.
text.Rotherham: Thy mother [was] like a vine of thy vineyard, By waters, was planted,— Fruitful and full of branches, she became By reason of waters abundant;
text.KJV: Thy mother is like a vine in thy blood, planted by the waters: she was fruitful and full of branches by reason of many waters.
text.NABRE: Allegory of the Vine Branch Your mother was like a leafy vine planted by water, Fruitful and full of branches because of abundant water.
text.DRB: Thy mother as it were a vine in thy blood is planted upon the water: her fruit and her branches have grown out of many waters.

text.NRSV: Its strongest stem became a ruler’s scepter; it towered aloft among the thick boughs; it stood out in its height with its mass of branches.
text.Rotherham: And they served her as staves of power For the sceptres of rulers, And high became the stature thereof With its interwoven foliage,- And it was seen by its height, By its multitude of branches.
text.KJV: And she had strong rods for the sceptres of them that bare rule, and her stature was exalted among the thick branches, and she appeared in her height with the multitude of her branches.
text.NABRE: One strong branch grew into a royal scepter. So tall it towered among the clouds, conspicuous in height, with dense foliage.
text.DRB: And there were made to her strong rods for the sceptres of them that rule, and her stature was exalted among the branches: and she saw her height in the multitude of her branches.

text.NRSV: But it was plucked up in fury, cast down to the ground; the east wind dried it up; its fruit was stripped off, its strong stem was withered; the fire consumed it.
text.Rotherham: Then was she uprooted in indignation To the ground, was she cast, And an east wind, dried up her fruit,—Broken off and withered were her staves of power, A fire, devoured them.
text.KJV: But she was plucked up in fury, she was cast down to the ground, and the east wind dried up her fruit: her strong rods were broken and withered; the fire consumed them.
text.NABRE: But she was torn out in fury and flung to the ground; The east wind withered her up, her fruit was plucked away; Her strongest branch dried up, fire devoured it.
text.DRB: And she was plucked up in wrath, and cast on the ground: and the burning wind hath dried up her fruit: the rods of her strength are withered, & dried up: fire hath eaten her.

text.NRSV: Now it is transplanted into the wilderness, into a dry and thirsty land.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, is she planted in a desert, -In a land parched and dry;
text.KJV: And now she is planted in the wilderness, in a dry and thirsty ground.
text.NABRE: Now she is planted in a wilderness, in a dry, parched land.
text.DRB: And now she is transplanted into the desert, in a land not passable, and dry.

text.NRSV: And fire has gone out from its stem, has consumed its branches and fruit, so that there remains in it no strong stem, no scepter for ruling. This is a lamentation, and it is used as a lamentation.
text.Rotherham: And there hath gone forth a fire out of her staves of rods. Her fruit, hath it devoured, And there is in her no staff of power. As a sceptre to bear rule. A dirge, it is, and hath been made a dirge.
text.KJV: And fire is gone out of a rod of her branches, which hath devoured her fruit, so that she hath no strong rod to be a sceptre to rule. This is a lamentation, and shall be for a lamentation.
text.NABRE: Fire flashed from her branch, and devoured her shoots; Now she does not have a strong branch, a royal scepter! This is a lamentation and serves as a lamentation.
text.DRB: And there came forth fire from the rod of her boughs, which hath eaten her fruit: and there was not in her a strong rod, the sceptre of rulers. Lamentation it is, and it shall be into lamentation.

text.NRSV: In the seventh year, in the fifth month, on the tenth day of the month, certain elders of Israel came to consult the Lord, and sat down before me.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass in the seventh year in the fifth month, on the tent of the month, that there came in men of the elders of Israel to enquire of Yahweh,—and they sat before me.
text.KJV: And it came to pass in the seventh year, in the fifth month, the tenth day of the month, that certain of the elders of Israel came to enquire of the Lord, and sat before me.
text.NABRE: In the seventh year, on the tenth day of the fifth month, some of the elders of Israel came to consult the Lord and sat down before me.
text.DRB: And it came to pass in the seventh year, in the fifth, the tenth of the month: there came men of the ancients of Israel to ask our Lord, and they sat before me.

text.NRSV: And the word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me saying:-
text.KJV: Then came the word of the Lord unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: Then the word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, speak to the elders of Israel, and say to them: Thus says the Lord God: Why are you coming? To consult me? As I live, says the Lord God, I will not be consulted by you.
text.Rotherham: Son of man Speak thou with the elders of Israel and say unto them, Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, To enquire of me, are ye coming in? As I live, verily I will not be enquired of by you, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: Son of man, speak unto the elders of Israel, and say unto them, Thus saith the Lord God; Are ye come to enquire of me? As I live, saith the Lord God, I will not be enquired of by you.
text.NABRE: Son of man, speak to the elders of Israel and say to them: Thus says the Lord God: Have you come to consult me? As I live, I will not allow myself to be consulted by you!—oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: Son of man, speak to the ancients of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: Thus saith our Lord God: Why, are you come to ask me? Live I, that I will not answer you, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: Will you judge them, mortal, will you judge them? Then let them know the abominations of their ancestors,
text.Rotherham: Wilt thou judge them, wilt thou judge, O son of man? The abominations of their fathers, let them know:
text.KJV: Wilt thou judge them, son of man, wilt thou judge them? cause them to know the abominations of their fathers:
text.NABRE: Will you judge them? Will you judge, son of man? Tell them about the abominations of their ancestors,
text.DRB: Doest thou judge them, doest thou judge o son of man? Shew to them the abominations of their fathers.

text.NRSV: and say to them: Thus says the Lord God: On the day when I chose Israel, I swore to the offspring of the house of Jacob—making myself known to them in the land of Egypt—I swore to them, saying, I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: So then thou shalt say unto them Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, In the day when I made choice of Israel, then lifted I up my hand. to the seed of the house of Jacob, and made myself known to them, in the land of Egypt,- Yea I lifted up my hand to them, saying, I Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: And say unto them, Thus saith the Lord God; In the day when I chose Israel, and lifted up mine hand unto the seed of the house of Jacob, and made myself known unto them in the land of Egypt, when I lifted up mine hand unto them, saying, I am the Lord your God;
text.NABRE: and say to them: Thus says the Lord God: The day I chose Israel, I swore to the descendants of the house of Jacob; I revealed myself to them in the land of Egypt and swore to them, saying: I am the Lord, your God.
text.DRB: And thou shalt say to them: Thus saith our Lord God: In the day that I chose Israel, and lifted up my hand for the stock of the house of Jacob: and appeared to them in the Land of Ægypt, & lifted up my hand for them, saying: I the Lord your God:

text.NRSV: On that day I swore to them that I would bring them out of the land of Egypt into a land that I had searched out for them, a land flowing with milk and honey, the most glorious of all lands.
text.Rotherham: In that day, lifted I up my hand to them, to bring them forth out of the land of Egypt,—into a land that I had looked out for them flowing with milk and honey, The beauty, it was of all lands,
text.KJV: In the day that I lifted up mine hand unto them, to bring them forth of the land of Egypt into a land that I had espied for them, flowing with milk and honey, which is the glory of all lands:
text.NABRE: That day I swore to bring them out of the land of Egypt to the land I had searched out for them, a land flowing with milk and honey, a jewel among all lands.
text.DRB: in that day I lifted up my hand for them, that I might bring them out of the Land of Ægypt, into a Land which I had provided for them, flowing with milk & honey, which is excellent among all lands.

text.NRSV: And I said to them, Cast away the detestable things your eyes feast on, every one of you, and do not defile yourselves with the idols of Egypt; I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: And I said unto them. Every one the abomination of his eyes, cast ye away, And with the manufactured gods of Egypt, let it not be that ye defile yourselves: I, Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: Then said I unto them, Cast ye away every man the abominations of his eyes, and defile not yourselves with the idols of Egypt: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: Then I said to them: Throw away, each of you, the detestable things that held your eyes; do not defile yourselves with the idols of Egypt: I am the Lord, your God.
text.DRB: And I said to them: Let every man cast away the scandals of his eyes, & in the idols of Ægypt be ye not polluted: I the Lord your God.

text.NRSV: But they rebelled against me and would not listen to me; not one of them cast away the detestable things their eyes feasted on, nor did they forsake the idols of Egypt. Then I thought I would pour out my wrath upon them and spend my anger against them in the midst of the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: But they rebelled against me And were not willing to hearken unto me, Every man the abominations of their eyes, they cast not away, And the manufactured gods of Egypt, forsook they not; Therefore spake I of pouring out mine indignation upon them to bring mine anger to an end against them, in the midst of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: But they rebelled against me, and would not hearken unto me: they did not every man cast away the abominations of their eyes, neither did they forsake the idols of Egypt: then I said, I will pour out my fury upon them, to accomplish my anger against them in the midst of the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: But they rebelled and refused to listen to me; none of them threw away the detestable things that held their eyes, nor did they abandon the idols of Egypt. Then I considered pouring out my fury and spending my anger against them there in the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: And they provoked me, & would not hear me: every one did not cast away the abominations of his eyes, neither did they leave the idols of Ægypt: and I said I would pour out mine indignation upon them, and fill my wrath in them, in the midst of the Land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: But I acted for the sake of my name, that it should not be profaned in the sight of the nations among whom they lived, in whose sight I made myself known to them in bringing them out of the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit I wrought with effect, for the sake of mine own Name, that it might not be profaned—before the eyes of the nations in whose midst they were,—before whose eyes I made myself known unto them, by bringing them forth out of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: But I wrought for my name’s sake, that it should not be polluted before the heathen, among whom they were, in whose sight I made myself known unto them, in bringing them forth out of the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: I acted for the sake of my name, that it should not be desecrated in the eyes of the nations among whom they were: in the eyes of the nations I had made myself known to them, to bring them out of the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: And I did for my name sake, that it might not be violated before the Gentiles, in the midst of whom they were, & among whom I appeared to them to bring them out of the Land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: So I led them out of the land of Egypt and brought them into the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: Therefore brought I them forth out of the land of Egypt,—and caused them to come into the desert.
text.KJV: Wherefore I caused them to go forth out of the land of Egypt, and brought them into the wilderness.
text.NABRE: Therefore I led them out of the land of Egypt and brought them into the wilderness.
text.DRB: I cast them out therefore of the Land of Ægypt, and brought them forth into the desert.

text.NRSV: I gave them my statutes and showed them my ordinances, by whose observance everyone shall live.
text.Rotherham: Then gave I unto them my statutes, And my regulations, caused I them to know,- By the which the man that doeth them shall live.
text.KJV: And I gave them my statutes, and shewed them my judgments, which if a man do, he shall even live in them.
text.NABRE: Then I gave them my statutes and made known to them my ordinances, so that everyone who keeps them has life through them.
text.DRB: And I gave them my precepts, and I shewed to them my judgements, which a man doing, shall live in them.

text.NRSV: Moreover I gave them my sabbaths, as a sign between me and them, so that they might know that I the Lord sanctify them.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also my sabbaths, gave I unto them, to become a sign betwixt me and them,—that it might be known that, I, Yahweh, was hallowing them.
text.KJV: Moreover also I gave them my sabbaths, to be a sign between me and them, that they might know that I am the Lord that sanctify them.
text.NABRE: I also gave them my sabbaths to be a sign between me and them, to show that it is I, the Lord, who makes them holy.
text.DRB: Moreover also my sabbaths I gave to them, to be a sign between me and them: & that they might know that I am the Lord sanctifying them.

text.NRSV: But the house of Israel rebelled against me in the wilderness; they did not observe my statutes but rejected my ordinances, by whose observance everyone shall live; and my sabbaths they greatly profaned. Then I thought I would pour out my wrath upon them in the wilderness, to make an end of them.
text.Rotherham: But the house of Israel, rebelled against me, in the desert, In my statutes, walked they not, And my regulations, they rejected By the which the man, that doeth them, shall live, My sabbaths also, they profaned exceedingly. Therefore spake I of pouring out mine indignation upon them in the desert, to make an end of them.
text.KJV: But the house of Israel rebelled against me in the wilderness: they walked not in my statutes, and they despised my judgments, which if a man do, he shall even live in them; and my sabbaths they greatly polluted: then I said, I would pour out my fury upon them in the wilderness, to consume them.
text.NABRE: But the house of Israel rebelled against me in the wilderness. They did not observe my statutes, and they rejected my ordinances that bring life to those who keep them. My sabbaths, too, they desecrated grievously. Then I considered pouring out my fury on them in the wilderness to put an end to them,
text.DRB: And the houses of Israel provoked me in the desert; they walked not in my precepts, and my judgements they rejected, which a man doing shall live in them: & my sabbaths they violated exceedingly. I said therefore I would pour out my fury upon them in the desert, and would consume them.

text.NRSV: But I acted for the sake of my name, so that it should not be profaned in the sight of the nations, in whose sight I had brought them out.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit I wrought with effect for the sake of mine own Name,—that it should not be profaned in the eyes of the nations, in whose eyes I had brought them forth.
text.KJV: But I wrought for my name’s sake, that it should not be polluted before the heathen, in whose sight I brought them out.
text.NABRE: but I acted for the sake of my name, so it would not be desecrated in the eyes of the nations in whose sight I had brought them out.
text.DRB: And I did for my name sake, lest it should be violated before the Gentiles, from which I cast them out, in their sight.

text.NRSV: Moreover I swore to them in the wilderness that I would not bring them into the land that I had given them, a land flowing with milk and honey, the most glorious of all lands,
text.Rotherham: Yet even I lifted up my hand to them. in the desert,— That I would not bring them into the land which I had given to them flowing with milk and honey, The beauty, it was. of all lands:
text.KJV: Yet also I lifted up my hand unto them in the wilderness, that I would not bring them into the land which I had given them, flowing with milk and honey, which is the glory of all lands;
text.NABRE: Nevertheless in the wilderness I swore to them that I would not bring them into the land I had given them—a land flowing with milk and honey, a jewel among all the lands.
text.DRB: Itherefore lifted up my hand upon them in the desert, not to bring them into the Land, which I gave them flowing with milk and honey, the chief of all lands.

text.NRSV: because they rejected my ordinances and did not observe my statutes, and profaned my sabbaths; for their heart went after their idols.
text.Rotherham: Because my regulations, they had rejected. And as for my statutes, they had not walked therein, My sabbaths also, had they profaned; For after their manufactured gods, their heart had been going.
text.KJV: Because they despised my judgments, and walked not in my statutes, but polluted my sabbaths: for their heart went after their idols.
text.NABRE: Their hearts followed after their idols so closely that they did not live by my statutes, but rejected my ordinances and desecrated my sabbaths.
text.DRB: Because they rejected my judgements, and walked not in my precepts, and violated my sabbaths: for their heart went after idols.

text.NRSV: Nevertheless my eye spared them, and I did not destroy them or make an end of them in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless mine eye threw a shield over them that they should not be destroyed; So I made not of them a full end in the desert.
text.KJV: Nevertheless mine eye spared them from destroying them, neither did I make an end of them in the wilderness.
text.NABRE: But I looked on them with pity, not wanting to destroy them, so I did not put an end to them in the wilderness.
text.DRB: And mine eye was merciful on them, that I killed them not: neither did I consume them in the desert.

text.NRSV: I said to their children in the wilderness, Do not follow the statutes of your parents, nor observe their ordinances, nor defile yourselves with their idols.
text.Rotherham: But I said unto their children in the desert, In the statutes of your fathers, let it not be that ye walk, And their regulations, let it not be that ye observe,— And with their manufactured gods, let it not be that ye defile yourselves.
text.KJV: But I said unto their children in the wilderness, Walk ye not in the statutes of your fathers, neither observe their judgments, nor defile yourselves with their idols:
text.NABRE: Then I said to their children in the wilderness: Do not follow the statutes of your parents. Do not keep their ordinances. Do not defile yourselves with their idols.
text.DRB: But I said to their children in the wilderness: In the precepts of your fathers walk not, neither keep ye their judgments, nor be polluted in their idols:

text.NRSV: I the Lord am your God; follow my statutes, and be careful to observe my ordinances,
text.Rotherham: I, Yahweh, am your God, In my statutes, walk ye,— And my regulations, observe ye and do them;
text.KJV: I am the Lord your God; walk in my statutes, and keep my judgments, and do them;
text.NABRE: I am the Lord, your God: follow my statutes and be careful to observe my ordinances;
text.DRB: I the Lord your God: walk ye in my precepts, and keep my judgements, and do them.

text.NRSV: and hallow my sabbaths that they may be a sign between me and you, so that you may know that I the Lord am your God.
text.Rotherham: My sabbaths also, hallow ye,— And they shall become a sign betwixt me and you, That it may be known that I Yahweh am your God.
text.KJV: And hallow my sabbaths; and they shall be a sign between me and you, that ye may know that I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: keep holy my sabbaths as a sign between me and you so that you may know that I am the Lord, your God.
text.DRB: And sanctify ye my sabbaths, that they may be a sign between me and you: and ye may know that I am the Lord your God.

text.NRSV: But the children rebelled against me; they did not follow my statutes, and were not careful to observe my ordinances, by whose observance everyone shall live; they profaned my sabbaths. Then I thought I would pour out my wrath upon them and spend my anger against them in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: Notwithstanding the children rebelled against me, In my statutes, walked they not, And my regulations, observed they not to do them Wherein the man who shall do them shall live, My sabbaths, they profaned. Therefore spake I of pouring out mine indignation upon them to bring mine anger to an end against them. in the desert.
text.KJV: Notwithstanding the children rebelled against me: they walked not in my statutes, neither kept my judgments to do them, which if a man do, he shall even live in them; they polluted my sabbaths: then I said, I would pour out my fury upon them, to accomplish my anger against them in the wilderness.
text.NABRE: But their children rebelled against me: they did not follow my statutes or keep my ordinances that bring life to those who observe them; my sabbaths they desecrated. Then I considered pouring out my fury on them, spending my anger against them in the wilderness;
text.DRB: And the children exasperated me, in my precepts they walked not: and my judgements they kept not, to do them: which when a man shall do, he shall live in them: and they violated my sabbaths: and I threatened to pour out my fury upon them, and to fill my wrath in them in the desert.

text.NRSV: But I withheld my hand, and acted for the sake of my name, so that it should not be profaned in the sight of the nations, in whose sight I had brought them out.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit I withdrew my hand, and wrought with effect for the sake of mine own Name, —that it should not be profaned before the eyes of the nations, before whose eyes I had brought them forth:
text.KJV: Nevertheless I withdrew mine hand, and wrought for my name’s sake, that it should not be polluted in the sight of the heathen, in whose sight I brought them forth.
text.NABRE: but I stayed my hand, acting for the sake of my name, lest it be desecrated in the eyes of the nations, in whose sight I had brought them out.
text.DRB: But I turned away my hand, & did for my name's sake, that it might not be violated before the Gentiles, out of which I did cast them forth in their eyes.

text.NRSV: Moreover I swore to them in the wilderness that I would scatter them among the nations and disperse them through the countries,
text.Rotherham: Even, I, lifted up my hand to them in the desert,— That I would disperse them throughout the nations, And scatter them throughout the lands;
text.KJV: I lifted up mine hand unto them also in the wilderness, that I would scatter them among the heathen, and disperse them through the countries;
text.NABRE: Nevertheless I swore to them in the wilderness that I would disperse them among the nations and scatter them in other lands,
text.DRB: Again I lifted up my hand upon them in the wilderness, that I might disperse them into nations, and scatter them into lands:

text.NRSV: because they had not executed my ordinances, but had rejected my statutes and profaned my sabbaths, and their eyes were set on their ancestors’ idols.
text.Rotherham: Because my regulations, had they not done And my statutes, they had rejected, My sabbaths also, they had profaned,- And after the manufactured gods of their fathers, were their eyes turned.
text.KJV: Because they had not executed my judgments, but had despised my statutes, and had polluted my sabbaths, and their eyes were after their fathers’ idols.
text.NABRE: because they did not carry out my ordinances, but rejected my statutes and desecrated my sabbaths, having eyes only for the idols of their ancestors.
text.DRB: for that they had not done my judgements, and had rejected my precepts, and had violated my sabbaths, and their eyes had been after the idols of their fathers.

text.NRSV: Moreover I gave them statutes that were not good and ordinances by which they could not live.
text.Rotherham: I myself, indeed, suffered them to walk in statutes which were not good,— And regulations in which they could not live.
text.KJV: Wherefore I gave them also statutes that were not good, and judgments whereby they should not live;
text.NABRE: Therefore I gave them statutes that were not good, and ordinances through which they could not have life.
text.DRB: Therefore I also gave them precepts not good, & judgements, in which they shall not live.

text.NRSV: I defiled them through their very gifts, in their offering up all their firstborn, in order that I might horrify them, so that they might know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Yea I let them defile themselves with their gifts, when they caused every firstborn to pass through the fire,-.that I might make them desolate. To the end they might know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: And I polluted them in their own gifts, in that they caused to pass through the fire all that openeth the womb, that I might make them desolate, to the end that they might know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: I let them become defiled by their offerings, by having them make a fiery offering of every womb’s firstborn, in order to ruin them so they might know that I am the Lord.
text.DRB: And I polluted them in their gifts, when they offered all that opened the matrice, for their offences: and they shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: Therefore, mortal, speak to the house of Israel and say to them, Thus says the Lord God: In this again your ancestors blasphemed me, by dealing treacherously with me.
text.Rotherham: Therefore speak thou unto the house of Israel Son of man, and say unto them, Thus saith My Lord Yahweh,— Yet further in this, did your fathers insult me, by the treachery wherewith they dealt treacherously against me;
text.KJV: Therefore, son of man, speak unto the house of Israel, and say unto them, Thus saith the Lord God; Yet in this your fathers have blasphemed me, in that they have committed a trespass against me.
text.NABRE: Therefore speak to the house of Israel, son of man, and tell them: Thus says the Lord God: In this way also your ancestors blasphemed me, breaking faith with me.
text.DRB: Wherefore speak to the house of Israel, o son of man: and thou shalt say to them: Thus saith our Lord God: Yet also in this did your fathers blaspheme me, when contemning they had despised me:

text.NRSV: For when I had brought them into the land that I swore to give them, then wherever they saw any high hill or any leafy tree, there they offered their sacrifices and presented the provocation of their offering; there they sent up their pleasing odors, and there they poured out their drink offerings.
text.Rotherham: When I brought them into the land as to which I had lifted up mine hand to give it them, then beheld they every high hill and every tangled tree And offered there their sacrifices, And presented there, their provoking gift, And placed there, their satisfying odour, And poured out there, their drink- offerings.
text.KJV: For when I had brought them into the land, for the which I lifted up mine hand to give it to them, then they saw every high hill, and all the thick trees, and they offered there their sacrifices, and there they presented the provocation of their offering: there also they made their sweet savour, and poured out there their drink offerings.
text.NABRE: When I brought them to the land I had sworn to give them, and they saw all its high hills and leafy trees, there they offered sacrifices, there they made offerings to provoke me, there they sent up sweet-smelling oblations, there they poured out their libations.
text.DRB: and I had brought them into the Land, upon which I lifted up my hand to give it them: they saw every high hill, & every woody tree, and there they immolated their victims: and there they gave the irritation of their oblation, and there they put the odour of their sweetness, and offered their libations.

text.NRSV: (I said to them, What is the high place to which you go? So it is called Bamah to this day.)
text.Rotherham: Therefore said I unto them, What! a high-place, whereinto ye are entering? And so the name thereof hath been called High-place, until this day.
text.KJV: Then I said unto them, What is the high place whereunto ye go? And the name thereof is called Bamah unto this day.
text.NABRE: So I said to them, “What is this high place to which you go?” Thus its name became “high place” even to this day.
text.DRB: And I said to them: What is the excelse, unto which you go? & the name thereof was called Excelse even to this day.

text.NRSV: Therefore say to the house of Israel, Thus says the Lord God: Will you defile yourselves after the manner of your ancestors and go astray after their detestable things?
text.Rotherham: Therefore say thou unto the house of Israel. Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, Is it in the way of your fathers, ye are defiling yourselves,- And after their abominations, that ye are committing lewdness?
text.KJV: Wherefore say unto the house of Israel, Thus saith the Lord God; Are ye polluted after the manner of your fathers? and commit ye whoredom after their abominations?
text.NABRE: Therefore say to the house of Israel: Thus says the Lord God: Will you defile yourselves in the way your ancestors did? Will you lust after their detestable idols?
text.DRB: Therefore say to the house of Israel: Thus saith our Lord God: Surely in the way of your fathers you are polluted, and after their scandals you do fornicate.

text.NRSV: When you offer your gifts and make your children pass through the fire, you defile yourselves with all your idols to this day. And shall I be consulted by you, O house of Israel? As I live, says the Lord God, I will not be consulted by you.
text.Rotherham: Yea when ye lift up your gifts When ye cause your children to pass through the fire, Ye are defiling yourselves unto all your manufactured gods unto this day, And shall, I, be enquired of by you O house of Israel? As I live, Declareth My Lord Yahweh, Surely I will not be enquired of by you.
text.KJV: For when ye offer your gifts, when ye make your sons to pass through the fire, ye pollute yourselves with all your idols, even unto this day: and shall I be enquired of by you, O house of Israel? As I live, saith the Lord God, I will not be enquired of by you.
text.NABRE: By offering your gifts, by making your children pass through the fire, you defile yourselves with all your idols even to this day. Shall I let myself be consulted by you, house of Israel? As I live—oracle of the Lord GOD—I swear I will not let myself be consulted by you!
text.DRB: And in the oblation of your gifts, when you make your children pass through the fire, you are polluted in all your idols unto this day: and shall I answer you, o house of Israel? Live I, saith our Lord God, that I will not answer you.

text.NRSV: What is in your mind shall never happen—the thought, “Let us be like the nations, like the tribes of the countries, and worship wood and stone.”
text.Rotherham: And as for that which is rising up on your spirit, it shall, not at all come to pass, In that ye are saying Let us be like the nations Like the families of the lands, By ministering unto Wood and Stone!
text.KJV: And that which cometh into your mind shall not be at all, that ye say, We will be as the heathen, as the families of the countries, to serve wood and stone.
text.NABRE: What has entered your mind shall never happen: You are thinking, “We shall be like the nations, like the peoples of foreign lands, serving wood and stone.”
text.DRB: Neither shall the cogitation of your mind come to pass, saying: We will be as the Gentiles, and as the kindreds of the earth, that we may worship wood, and stones.

text.NRSV: As I live, says the Lord God, surely with a mighty hand and an outstretched arm, and with wrath poured out, I will be king over you.
text.Rotherham: As I live, Declareth My Lord Yahweh, Surely, with a strong hand and With an outstretched arm and With outpoured indignation, Will I be king over you;
text.KJV: As I live, saith the Lord God, surely with a mighty hand, and with a stretched out arm, and with fury poured out, will I rule over you:
text.NABRE: As I live—oracle of the Lord GOD—with mighty hand and outstretched arm, with wrath poured out, I swear I will be king over you!
text.DRB: Live I, saith our Lord God, that in a strong hand, & in a stretched out arm, & in fury poured forth will I reign over you.

text.NRSV: I will bring you out from the peoples and gather you out of the countries where you are scattered, with a mighty hand and an outstretched arm, and with wrath poured out;
text.Rotherham: And I will bring you forth from among the peoples, And gather you together out of the lands, throughout which ye have been dispersed, With a strong hand and With an outstretched arm, and With outpoured indignation;
text.KJV: And I will bring you out from the people, and will gather you out of the countries wherein ye are scattered, with a mighty hand, and with a stretched out arm, and with fury poured out.
text.NABRE: With mighty hand and outstretched arm, with wrath poured out, I will bring you out from the nations and gather you from the countries over which you are scattered;
text.DRB: And I will bring you out of the peoples, and I will gather you out of the lands, in which you are dispersed, in a strong hand, & in a stretched out arm, & in fury poured forth will I reign over you.

text.NRSV: and I will bring you into the wilderness of the peoples, and there I will enter into judgment with you face to face.
text.Rotherham: And I will bring you into the desert of the peoples,—and will contend with you there, face to face.
text.KJV: And I will bring you into the wilderness of the people, and there will I plead with you face to face.
text.NABRE: I will lead you to the wilderness of the peoples and enter into judgment with you face to face.
text.DRB: And I will bring you into the desert of peoples, and will be judged there with you face to face.

text.NRSV: As I entered into judgment with your ancestors in the wilderness of the land of Egypt, so I will enter into judgment with you, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: Just as I contended with your fathers in the desert of the land of Egypt, So will I contend with you, Declareth My Lord Yahweh;
text.KJV: Like as I pleaded with your fathers in the wilderness of the land of Egypt, so will I plead with you, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: Just as I entered into judgment with your ancestors in the wilderness of the land of Egypt, so will I enter into judgment with you—oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: As I contended in judgement against your fathers in the desert of the Land of Ægypt: so will I judge you, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: I will make you pass under the staff, and will bring you within the bond of the covenant.
text.Rotherham: And I will cause you to pass under the rod And will bring you into the bond of the covenant;
text.KJV: And I will cause you to pass under the rod, and I will bring you into the bond of the covenant:
text.NABRE: Thus I will make you pass under the staff and will impose on you the terms of the covenant.
text.DRB: And I will subdue you to my sceptre, & will bring you into the bands of covenant.

text.NRSV: I will purge out the rebels among you, and those who transgress against me; I will bring them out of the land where they reside as aliens, but they shall not enter the land of Israel. Then you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And will sever from you those who are rebelling and those who are transgressing against me, Out of the land of their sojournings, will I bring them forth, Yet upon the soil of Israel, shall they not enter, So shall ye know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: And I will purge out from among you the rebels, and them that transgress against me: I will bring them forth out of the country where they sojourn, and they shall not enter into the land of Israel: and ye shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: I will sort out from you those who defied me and rebelled against me; from the land where they resided as aliens I will bring them out, but they shall not return to the land of Israel. Thus you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.DRB: And I will choose out of you the transgressors, and impious, & will bring them out of the land of their sojourning, and into the land of Israel they shall not enter: and you shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: As for you, O house of Israel, thus says the Lord God: Go serve your idols, everyone of you now and hereafter, if you will not listen to me; but my holy name you shall no more profane with your gifts and your idols.
text.Rotherham: But as for you, O house of Israel Thus, saith my Lord. Yahweh, Go. serve every man his own manufactured gods, Yet afterwards, surely Ye shall be ready to hearken unto me,— And my holy name, shall ye profane no more, With your gifts, and With your manufactured gods.
text.KJV: As for you, O house of Israel, thus saith the Lord God; Go ye, serve ye every one his idols, and hereafter also, if ye will not hearken unto me: but pollute ye my holy name no more with your gifts, and with your idols.
text.NABRE: As for you, house of Israel, thus says the Lord God: Go! each of you, and worship your idols. Listen to me! You shall never again desecrate my holy name with your offerings and your idols!
text.DRB: And you o house of Israel, Thus saith our Lord God: Walk you every one after your idols, & serve them. But and if in this also you hear me not, and shall pollute my holy name any more in your gifts, & in your idols:

text.NRSV: For on my holy mountain, the mountain height of Israel, says the Lord God, there all the house of Israel, all of them, shall serve me in the land; there I will accept them, and there I will require your contributions and the choicest of your gifts, with all your sacred things.
text.Rotherham: For in my holy mountain. In the mountain of the height of Israel, Declareth, My Lord Yahweh, There shall all the house of Israel serve me, all of it, in the land, There will I accept them, and There, will I seek your heave-offerings and the firstfruits of your gifts, in all your holy things:
text.KJV: For in mine holy mountain, in the mountain of the height of Israel, saith the Lord God, there shall all the house of Israel, all of them in the land, serve me: there will I accept them, and there will I require your offerings, and the firstfruits of your oblations, with all your holy things.
text.NABRE: For on my holy mountain, on the highest mountain in Israel—oracle of the Lord GOD—there the whole house of Israel shall worship me; there in the land I will accept them all, there I will claim your tributes, the best of your offerings, from all your holy things.
text.DRB: in my holy mount, in the high mount of Israel, saith our Lord God, there shall all the house of Israel serve me; all I say in the land, wherein they shall please me, & there will I require your first fruits, and the beginning of your tithes in all your sanctifications.

text.NRSV: As a pleasing odor I will accept you, when I bring you out from the peoples, and gather you out of the countries where you have been scattered; and I will manifest my holiness among you in the sight of the nations.
text.Rotherham: As a satisfying odour, will I accept you, Through my bringing you forth from among the peoples, And gathering you out of the lands, throughout which ye have been dispersed; Thus will I hallow myself in you before the eyes of the nations.
text.KJV: I will accept you with your sweet savour, when I bring you out from the people, and gather you out of the countries wherein ye have been scattered; and I will be sanctified in you before the heathen.
text.NABRE: As a sweet-smelling oblation I will accept you, when I bring you from among the nations and gather you out of the lands over which you were scattered; and through you I will manifest my holiness in the sight of the nations.
text.DRB: I will receive you for an odour of sweetness, when Ishall have brought you out of the peoples, & shall have gathered you out of the lands, into which you are dispersed, & I will be sanctified in you in the eyes of the nations.

text.NRSV: You shall know that I am the Lord, when I bring you into the land of Israel, the country that I swore to give to your ancestors.
text.Rotherham: So shall ye know that I am Yahweh, Through my bringing you in unto the soil of Israel, Unto the land as to which I lifted up my hand, to give it unto your fathers.
text.KJV: And ye shall know that I am the Lord, when I shall bring you into the land of Israel, into the country for the which I lifted up mine hand to give it to your fathers.
text.NABRE: Thus you shall know that I am the Lord, when I bring you back to the soil of Israel, the land I swore to give your ancestors.
text.DRB: And you shall know that I am the Lord, when I shall have brought you into the land of Israel, into the land for which I lifted up my hand to give it to your fathers.

text.NRSV: There you shall remember your ways and all the deeds by which you have polluted yourselves; and you shall loathe yourselves for all the evils that you have committed.
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye call to mind. there, your own ways and all your own doings, wherewith ye had defiled yourselves,- And ye shall become loathsome in your own sight, for all your wickednesses, which ye have done.
text.KJV: And there shall ye remember your ways, and all your doings, wherein ye have been defiled; and ye shall lothe yourselves in your own sight for all your evils that ye have committed.
text.NABRE: There you shall remember your ways, all the deeds by which you defiled yourselves; and you shall loathe yourselves because of all the evil you did.
text.DRB: And there you shall remember your ways, and all your wicked deeds, in which you were polluted: & you shall mislike yourselves in your own sight, in all your malices, which you have done.

text.NRSV: And you shall know that I am the Lord, when I deal with you for my name’s sake, not according to your evil ways, or corrupt deeds, O house of Israel, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: So shall ye know that, I, am Yahweh, Through my dealing effectively with you, for the sake of mine own Name,- Not according to your own wicked ways. Nor according to your corrupted doings. O house of Israel, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: And ye shall know that I am the Lord, when I have wrought with you for my name’s sake, not according to your wicked ways, nor according to your corrupt doings, O ye house of Israel, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: And you shall know that I am the Lord when I deal with you thus, for the sake of my name, not according to your evil ways and wanton deeds, house of Israel—oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: And you shall know that I am the Lord, when I shall have done you good for my name sake, and not according to your evil ways, nor according to your most wicked deeds, o house of Israel, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me. saying:
text.KJV: Moreover the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, set your face toward the south, preach against the south, and prophesy against the forest land in the Negeb;
text.Rotherham: Son of man Set thy face the way towards Teman, And drop thy word against the land of the sun, And prophesy unto the forest of the field of the South;
text.KJV: Son of man, set thy face toward the south, and drop thy word toward the south, and prophesy against the forest of the south field;
text.NABRE: Son of man, turn your face to the south: preach against the south, prophesy against the forest land in the south.
text.DRB: Son of man, set thy face against the way of the south, and drop toward the southern wind, & prophesy to the forest of the southern field.

text.NRSV: say to the forest of the Negeb, Hear the word of the Lord: Thus says the Lord God, I will kindle a fire in you, and it shall devour every green tree in you and every dry tree; the blazing flame shall not be quenched, and all faces from south to north shall be scorched by it.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt say to the forest of the South Hear the word of Yahweh,— Thus saith My Lord Yahweh-Behold me! kindling in thee a fire. And it shall devour in thee every moist tree and every dry tree. The spreading flame shall not be quenched, Then shall be scorched thereby all faces from south to north;
text.KJV: And say to the forest of the south, Hear the word of the Lord; Thus saith the Lord God; Behold, I will kindle a fire in thee, and it shall devour every green tree in thee, and every dry tree: the flaming flame shall not be quenched, and all faces from the south to the north shall be burned therein.
