text.NRSV: For when they had slaughtered their children for their idols, on the same day they came into my sanctuary to profane it. This is what they did in my house.
text.Rotherham: Yea when they had slaughtered their children to their manufactured gods, then entered they into my sanctuary, on the same day, to profane it,- And lo! thus have they done in the midst of mine own house.
text.KJV: For when they had slain their children to their idols, then they came the same day into my sanctuary to profane it; and, lo, thus have they done in the midst of mine house.
text.NABRE: On the very day they slaughtered their children for their idols, they entered my sanctuary to desecrate it. Thus they acted within my house!
text.DRB: And when they immolated their children to their idols, and went into my sanctuary in that day to pollute it: these things also they did in the midst of my house.

text.NRSV: They even sent for men to come from far away, to whom a messenger was sent, and they came. For them you bathed yourself, painted your eyes, and decked yourself with ornaments;
text.Rotherham: Yea verily, that ye must needs send for men, ready to come in from afar, in that, a messenger, was sent unto them and lo! they came, For whom thou didst bathe thyself Paint thine eyes And deck thyself with ornaments.
text.KJV: And furthermore, that ye have sent for men to come from far, unto whom a messenger was sent; and, lo, they came: for whom thou didst wash thyself, paintedst thy eyes, and deckedst thyself with ornaments,
text.NABRE: Moreover, they sent for men who had to come from afar; when a messenger was sent to them, they came. On their account you bathed, painted your eyes, and put on jewelry.
text.DRB: They sent to men coming from far, to whom they had sent a messenger: therefore lo they came: to whom thou didst wash thyself, and didst anoint thine eyes about with stibic stone, and wast adorned with women's ornaments.

text.NRSV: you sat on a stately couch, with a table spread before it on which you had placed my incense and my oil.
text.Rotherham: Then satest thou upon a glorious couch With a table prepared before it,— And mine incense and mine oil, didst thou set thereon.
text.KJV: And satest upon a stately bed, and a table prepared before it, whereupon thou hast set mine incense and mine oil.
text.NABRE: You sat on a magnificent couch, set before it a table, on which to lay my incense and oil.
text.DRB: Thou satest in a very fair bed, and a table was decked before thee: mine incense, and mine ointment thou didst set upon it.

text.NRSV: The sound of a raucous multitude was around her, with many of the rabble brought in drunken from the wilderness; and they put bracelets on the arms of the women, and beautiful crowns upon their heads.
text.Rotherham: The noise of careless throng also, was with her, And besides men out of the mass of mankind, there were brought in drunkards, out of the desert; And they put bracelets upon the hands of both women, And crowns of adorning upon their heads.
text.KJV: And a voice of a multitude being at ease was with her: and with the men of the common sort were brought Sabeans from the wilderness, which put bracelets upon their hands, and beautiful crowns upon their heads.
text.NABRE: The cries of a mob! The shouts of men coming in from the wilderness! They put bracelets on the women’s arms and splendid crowns on their heads.
text.DRB: And the voice of a multitude rejoicing was on it: and on the men, that were brought of the multitude of men, and came from the desert, they did put bracelets on their hands, and beautiful crowns on their heads.

text.NRSV: Then I said, Ah, she is worn out with adulteries, but they carry on their sexual acts with her.
text.Rotherham: Then said I of her that was worn out with adulteries, Will they now join in her unchaste doings even hers?
text.KJV: Then said I unto her that was old in adulteries, Will they now commit whoredoms with her, and she with them?
text.NABRE: I said: “That worn-out one still has adulteries in her! Now they engage her as a prostitute and she…”
text.DRB: And I said to her, that was worn in adulteries: Now will this woman also fornicate in her fornication.

text.NRSV: For they have gone in to her, as one goes in to a whore. Thus they went in to Oholah and to Oholibah, wanton women.
text.Rotherham: Yet came they in unto her, as men go in unto a woman that is a harlot,— So, came they in unto Oholah, and unto Oholibah—the lewd women!
text.KJV: Yet they went in unto her, as they go in unto a woman that playeth the harlot: so went they in unto Aholah and unto Aholibah, the lewd women.
text.NABRE: And indeed they did come in to her as men come in to a prostitute. Thus they came to Oholah and Oholibah, the depraved women.
text.DRB: And they went to her as to an harlot woman: so went they unto Oolla, and Ooliba wicked women.

text.NRSV: But righteous judges shall declare them guilty of adultery and of bloodshed; because they are adulteresses and blood is on their hands.
text.Rotherham: But righteous men, they, shall judge them, With the judgment of adulteresses, and With the judgment of women that shed blood,— Because adulteresses, they are And blood, is on their hands.
text.KJV: And the righteous men, they shall judge them after the manner of adulteresses, and after the manner of women that shed blood; because they are adulteresses, and blood is in their hands.
text.NABRE: The righteous shall certainly punish them with sentences given to adulterers and murderers, for they committed adultery, and blood is on their hands.
text.DRB: They therefore are just men: these shall judge them with the judgement of adulteresses, and with the judgement of blood-shedders: because they are adulteresses, and blood is in their hands.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord God: Bring up an assembly against them, and make them an object of terror and of plunder.
text.Rotherham: For thus, saith My Lord. Yahweh,- There shall be brought up against them a gathered host, Then shall they be delivered up. for a terror and for a prey,
text.KJV: For thus saith the Lord God; I will bring up a company upon them, and will give them to be removed and spoiled.
text.NABRE: Indeed, thus says the Lord God: Raise up an army against them and hand them over to terror and plunder.
text.DRB: For thus saith our Lord God: Bring a multitude to them, and deliver them into tumult, and into spoil:

text.NRSV: The assembly shall stone them and with their swords they shall cut them down; they shall kill their sons and their daughters, and burn up their houses.
text.Rotherham: Yea a gathered host shall stone them with stones, and dispatch them with their swords, Their sons and their daughters, shall they slay, And their houses—in the fire, shall they consume.
text.KJV: And the company shall stone them with stones, and dispatch them with their swords; they shall slay their sons and their daughters, and burn up their houses with fire.
text.NABRE: The army will stone them and hack them to pieces with their swords. They will kill their sons and daughters and set fire to their houses.
text.DRB: and let them be stoned with the stones of peoples, and let them be thrust through with their swords: they shall kill their sons, and daughters, and their houses they shall burn with fire.

text.NRSV: Thus will I put an end to lewdness in the land, so that all women may take warning and not commit lewdness as you have done.
text.Rotherham: So will I make an end of lewdness out of the land,- And all women shall be disciplined, and shall not do according to your lewdness.
text.KJV: Thus will I cause lewdness to cease out of the land, that all women may be taught not to do after your lewdness.
text.NABRE: Thus I will put an end to depravity in the land, and all women will be warned not to imitate your depravity.
text.DRB: And I will take away the wickedness out of the land, and all women shall learn, not to do according to the wickedness of them.

text.NRSV: They shall repay you for your lewdness, and you shall bear the penalty for your sinful idolatry; and you shall know that I am the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: Yea the judges Shall set your lewdness upon you, And your sins with manufactured gods, shall ye bear; So shall ye know that, I, am The Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: And they shall recompense your lewdness upon you, and ye shall bear the sins of your idols: and ye shall know that I am the Lord God.
text.NABRE: They shall inflict on you the penalty of your depravity, and you shall pay for your sins of idolatry. Then you shall know that I am the Lord God.
text.DRB: And they shall give your wickedness upon you, and the sins of your idols you shall carry: and you shall know that I am the Lord God.

text.NRSV: In the ninth year, in the tenth month, on the tenth day of the month, the word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me. in the ninth year in the tenth month, on the tenth of the month, saying:
text.KJV: Again in the ninth year, in the tenth month, in the tenth day of the month, the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: On the tenth day of the tenth month, in the ninth year, the word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me in the ninth year, in the tenth month, the tenth day of the month, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, write down the name of this day, this very day. The king of Babylon has laid siege to Jerusalem this very day.
text.Rotherham: Son of man. Write thee the name of the day, this selfsame day,—the king of Babylon cast himself against Jerusalem, on this selfsame day.
text.KJV: Son of man, write thee the name of the day, even of this same day: the king of Babylon set himself against Jerusalem this same day.
text.NABRE: Son of man, write down today’s date this very day, for on this very day the king of Babylon lays siege to Jerusalem.
text.DRB: Son of man, write thee the name of this day, wherein the king of Babylon is confirmed against Jerusalem today.

text.NRSV: And utter an allegory to the rebellious house and say to them, Thus says the Lord God: Set on the pot, set it on, pour in water also;
text.Rotherham: Put thou forth therefore, against the perverse house a parable, and say unto them Thus saith My Lord, Yahweh: Set on the caldron set it on, Moreover also pour into it water:
text.KJV: And utter a parable unto the rebellious house, and say unto them, Thus saith the Lord God; Set on a pot, set it on, and also pour water into it:
text.NABRE: Propose this parable to the rebellious house and say to them: Thus says the Lord God: Put the pot on, put it on! Pour in some water;
text.DRB: And thou shalt speak by a proverb to the exasperating house a parable, & shalt say to them: Thus saith our Lord God: Set thou a pot, set it, I say, and put water into it.

text.NRSV: put in it the pieces, all the good pieces, the thigh and the shoulder; fill it with choice bones.
text.Rotherham: Gather the pieces thereof into it, Every good piece thigh and shoulder, With the choice of the bones, fill it:
text.KJV: Gather the pieces thereof into it, even every good piece, the thigh, and the shoulder; fill it with the choice bones.
text.NABRE: Add to it pieces of meat, all choice pieces; With thigh and shoulder, with choice cuts fill it.
text.DRB: Heap together the pieces thereof into it, every good part, the thigh and the shoulder, the chosen things and full of bones.

text.NRSV: Take the choicest one of the flock, pile the logs under it; boil its pieces, seethe also its bones in it.
text.Rotherham: Take of the choice of the rock, Moreover also pile of bones place under it,- Boil the pieces thereof, Yea let the bones of it seethe in the midst thereof.
text.KJV: Take the choice of the flock, and burn also the bones under it, and make it boil well, and let them seethe the bones of it therein.
text.NABRE: Choose the pick of the flock, then pile logs beneath it; Bring it to a boil, cook all the pieces in it.
text.DRB: Take the fattest beast, and lay together piles of bones also under it: the seething thereof is boiling hot, and the bones thereof are throughly sodden in the midst thereof.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God: Woe to the bloody city, the pot whose rust is in it, whose rust has not gone out of it! Empty it piece by piece, making no choice at all.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, Woe! city of bloodshed. The caldron whose scum is in it, and whose scum, hath not gone out of it: Piece by piece, bring it out, There hath fallen thereon no lot.
text.KJV: Wherefore thus saith the Lord God; Woe to the bloody city, to the pot whose scum is therein, and whose scum is not gone out of it! bring it out piece by piece; let no lot fall upon it.
text.NABRE: Therefore thus says the Lord God: Woe to the city full of blood! A pot containing filth, whose filth cannot be removed! Take out its pieces one by one, for no lot has fallen on their behalf.
text.DRB: Therefore thus saith our Lord God: Woe to the city of blood, to the pot, whose rustiness is in it, and the rustiness thereof is not gone out of it: by her parts and by her parts cast her out, there hath no lot fallen upon her.

text.NRSV: For the blood she shed is inside it; she placed it on a bare rock; she did not pour it out on the ground, to cover it with earth.
text.Rotherham: For, her own blood, hath come to be in her midst, Upon the smooth face of the cliff, hath she set it,- She hath not poured it out on the earth, that she might cover it with dust.
text.KJV: For her blood is in the midst of her; she set it upon the top of a rock; she poured it not upon the ground, to cover it with dust;
text.NABRE: For her blood is still in her midst; on a bare rock she left it; She did not pour it on the ground to be covered with dirt.
text.DRB: For her blood is in the midst of her, she hath shed it upon the most clear rock: she shed it not upon the ground, that it might be covered with dust.

text.NRSV: To rouse my wrath, to take vengeance, I have placed the blood she shed on a bare rock, so that it may not be covered.
text.Rotherham: To bring up indignation to execute an avenging, have I set her blood upon the smooth face of the cliff,—that it may not be covered.
text.KJV: That it might cause fury to come up to take vengeance; I have set her blood upon the top of a rock, that it should not be covered.
text.NABRE: To arouse wrath, to exact vengeance, I have left her blood on bare rock not to be covered.
text.DRB: That I might bring mine indignation in upon her, and might revenge with vengeance: I gave her blood upon the most clear rock, that it might not be covered.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God: Woe to the bloody city! I will even make the pile great.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Woe! city of bloodshed,—Even I myself, will make large the pile.
text.KJV: Therefore thus saith the Lord God; Woe to the bloody city! I will even make the pile for fire great.
text.NABRE: Therefore, thus says the Lord God: Woe to the city full of blood! I will make the pyre great!
text.DRB: Therefore thus saith our Lord God: Woe to the city of blood, whose bonefire I will make great.

text.NRSV: Heap up the logs, kindle the fire; boil the meat well, mix in the spices, let the bones be burned.
text.Rotherham: Heap on the wood Kindle the fire, Consume the flesh, Yea season with spice, And let the bones be scorched.
text.KJV: Heap on wood, kindle the fire, consume the flesh, and spice it well, and let the bones be burned.
text.NABRE: Pile on the wood, kindle the fire. Cook the meat, stir the spicy mixture, char the bones!
text.DRB: Heap together the bones, which I will burn with fire: the flesh shall be consumed, and all the composition shall be sod, and the bones shall dry away.

text.NRSV: Stand it empty upon the coals, so that it may become hot, its copper glow, its filth melt in it, its rust be consumed.
text.Rotherham: Then set it upon the live coals thereof empty,—that the copper of it may be scorched and burn. So that the impurity thereof may be annihilated within it The scum thereof be consumed,
text.KJV: Then set it empty upon the coals thereof, that the brass of it may be hot, and may burn, and that the filthiness of it may be molten in it, that the scum of it may be consumed.
text.NABRE: Then set it empty on the coals, to heat up until its copper glows, So its impurities melt, its filth disappears.
text.DRB: Set it also upon hot burning coals empty, that the brass thereof may wax hot, and be melted: and let the filth of it be melted in the midst thereof, and let the rust thereof be consumed.

text.NRSV: In vain I have wearied myself; its thick rust does not depart. To the fire with its rust!
text.Rotherham: With toils, hath she wearied herself,—Since her abundant scum will not go out of her, into the fire, with her scum!
text.KJV: She hath wearied herself with lies, and her great scum went not forth out of her: her scum shall be in the fire.
text.NABRE: The toil is exhausting, but the great filth will not come out— Filth, even with fire.
text.DRB: There hath been sweating with much labour, & the exceeding rust thereof is not gone out, no not by fire.

text.NRSV: Yet, when I cleansed you in your filthy lewdness, you did not become clean from your filth; you shall not again be cleansed until I have satisfied my fury upon you.
text.Rotherham: In thine uncleanness, is lewdness; be- cause I purified thee and thou wast not purified, from thine uncleanness, thou shalt not be purified any more, until I have let mine indignation rest upon thee.
text.KJV: In thy filthiness is lewdness: because I have purged thee, and thou wast not purged, thou shalt not be purged from thy filthiness any more, till I have caused my fury to rest upon thee.
text.NABRE: Even in defiling yourself with depravity I would still have cleansed you, but you would not have your impurity cleansed. You will not be cleansed now until I wreak my fury on you.
text.DRB: Thine uncleanness is execrable: because I would cleanse thee, and thou art not cleansed from thy filthiness: yea neither shalt thou be cleansed, before I make mine indignation to cease in thee.

text.NRSV: I the Lord have spoken; the time is coming, I will act. I will not refrain, I will not spare, I will not relent. According to your ways and your doings I will judge you, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: I Yahweh, have spoken It cometh and I will perform I will not let alone, neither will I shield, Nor will I repent,- According to thy ways and according to thy doings, have they judged thee, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: I the Lord have spoken it: it shall come to pass, and I will do it; I will not go back, neither will I spare, neither will I repent; according to thy ways, and according to thy doings, shall they judge thee, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: I, the Lord, have spoken; it will happen! I will do it and not hold back! I will not have pity or relent. By your conduct and deeds you shall be judged— oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: I the Lord have spoken: It shall come, and I will do it: I will not pass, nor spare, nor be pacified: according to thy ways, and according to thine inventions will I judge thee, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me saying:
text.KJV: Also the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: Ezekiel as a Sign for the Exiles. The word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, with one blow I am about to take away from you the delight of your eyes; yet you shall not mourn or weep, nor shall your tears run down.
text.Rotherham: Son of Man Behold me! taking away from thee the delight of thine eyes, with a stroke,- But thou shall not lament Neither shalt thou weep, Neither shall come—thy tears:
text.KJV: Son of man, behold, I take away from thee the desire of thine eyes with a stroke: yet neither shalt thou mourn nor weep, neither shall thy tears run down.
text.NABRE: Son of man, with a sudden blow I am taking away from you the delight of your eyes, but do not mourn or weep or shed any tears.
text.DRB: Son of man, behold I take from thee the thing that thine eyes desire in a plague: and thou shalt not lament, nor weep: neither shall thy tears run.

text.NRSV: Sigh, but not aloud; make no mourning for the dead. Bind on your turban, and put your sandals on your feet; do not cover your upper lip or eat the bread of mourners.
text.Rotherham: To groan, forbear, Over the dead—no mourning, shalt thou make Thy chaplet, bind thou on thee And thy sandals:, put thou on thy feet, And thou shalt not cover thy beard, And the bread of other men, shalt thou not eat.
text.KJV: Forbear to cry, make no mourning for the dead, bind the tire of thine head upon thee, and put on thy shoes upon thy feet, and cover not thy lips, and eat not the bread of men.
text.NABRE: Groan, moan for the dead, but make no public lament; bind on your turban, put your sandals on your feet, but do not cover your beard or eat the bread of mourners.
text.DRB: Sigh holding thy peace, thou shalt not make the mourning of the dead: let thy crown be tied round about thee, and thy shoes shall be on thy feet, neither shalt thou cover thy face with a cloth, neither shalt thou eat the meats of mourners.

text.NRSV: So I spoke to the people in the morning, and at evening my wife died. And on the next morning I did as I was commanded.
text.Rotherham: So I spake unto the people in the morning, and my wife died in the evening,—and I did in the morning, as I had been commanded.
text.KJV: So I spake unto the people in the morning: and at even my wife died; and I did in the morning as I was commanded.
text.NABRE: I spoke to the people in the morning. In the evening my wife died. The next morning I did as I had been commanded.
text.DRB: Ispake therefore to the people in the morning, and my wife died at even: and I did in the morning as he had commanded me.

text.NRSV: Then the people said to me, “Will you not tell us what these things mean for us, that you are acting this way?”
text.Rotherham: Then said the people unto me: Wilt thou not tell us what these things are to us, that thou shouldst be acting thus?
text.KJV: And the people said unto me, Wilt thou not tell us what these things are to us, that thou doest so?
text.NABRE: Then the people asked me, “Will you not tell us what all these things you are doing mean for us?”
text.DRB: And the people said to me: Why doest thou not tell us what these things signify, that thou doest?

text.NRSV: Then I said to them: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: So I said unto them: The word of Yahweh hath come unto me, saying:
text.KJV: Then I answered them, The word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: I said to them, The word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And I said to them: The word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: Say to the house of Israel, Thus says the Lord God: I will profane my sanctuary, the pride of your power, the delight of your eyes, and your heart’s desire; and your sons and your daughters whom you left behind shall fall by the sword.
text.Rotherham: Say to the house of Israel thus, saith My Lord. Yahweh, Behold me! profaning my sanctuary. The pride of your strength, The delight of your eyes And the object of the tender regard of your soul,- And your sons and your daughters whom ye have left behind, by the sword, shall fall.
text.KJV: Speak unto the house of Israel, Thus saith the Lord God; Behold, I will profane my sanctuary, the excellency of your strength, the desire of your eyes, and that which your soul pitieth; and your sons and your daughters whom ye have left shall fall by the sword.
text.NABRE: Say to the house of Israel: Thus says the Lord God: I will now desecrate my sanctuary, the pride of your strength, the delight of your eyes, the concern of your soul. The sons and daughters you left behind shall fall by the sword.
text.DRB: Speak to the house of Israel: Thus saith our Lord God: Behold I will pollute my sanctuary, the pride of your empire, and the thing that your eyes desire, and upon which your soul quaketh for fear: your sons, and your daughters, which you have left, shall fall by the sword.

text.NRSV: And you shall do as I have done; you shall not cover your upper lip or eat the bread of mourners.
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye do, as I have done.— The beard, shall ye not cover, And the bread of other men:, shall ye not eat;
text.KJV: And ye shall do as I have done: ye shall not cover your lips, nor eat the bread of men.
text.NABRE: Then you shall do as I have done, not covering your beards nor eating the bread of mourning.
text.DRB: And you shall do as I have done: your faces with a cloth you shall not cover, and the meats of mourners you shall not eat.

text.NRSV: Your turbans shall be on your heads and your sandals on your feet; you shall not mourn or weep, but you shall pine away in your iniquities and groan to one another.
text.Rotherham: And your chaplets shall be on your heads. And your sandals on your feet, Ye shall neither lament, nor weep,- Yet shall ye pine away in your iniquities, and shall groan one to another.
text.KJV: And your tires shall be upon your heads, and your shoes upon your feet: ye shall not mourn nor weep; but ye shall pine away for your iniquities, and mourn one toward another.
text.NABRE: Your turbans shall remain on your heads, your sandals on your feet. You shall not mourn or weep, but you shall waste away because of your sins and groan to one another.
text.DRB: You shall have crowns on your heads, & shoes on your feet: you shall not lament nor weep, but you shall pine away in your iniquities, and every one shall groan toward his brother.

text.NRSV: Thus Ezekiel shall be a sign to you; you shall do just as he has done. When this comes, then you shall know that I am the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: So shall Ezekiel become to you a wonder, According to all that he hath done, shall ye do: When it cometh, then shall ye know that I am The Lord, Yahweh.
text.KJV: Thus Ezekiel is unto you a sign: according to all that he hath done shall ye do: and when this cometh, ye shall know that I am the Lord God.
text.NABRE: Ezekiel shall be a sign for you: everything he did, you shall do. When it happens, you shall know that I am the Lord God.
text.DRB: And Ezechiel shall be unto you for a portending sign: according to all things, that he hath done, shall you do when this shall come: & you shall know that I am the Lord God.

text.NRSV: And you, mortal, on the day when I take from them their stronghold, their joy and glory, the delight of their eyes and their heart’s affection, and also their sons and their daughters,
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore Son of man, Shall it not be— In the day when I take away from them Their strength, The joy of their adorning,— The delight of their eyes And the desire of their soul, Their sons and their daughters
text.KJV: Also, thou son of man, shall it not be in the day when I take from them their strength, the joy of their glory, the desire of their eyes, and that whereupon they set their minds, their sons and their daughters,
text.NABRE: End of Ezekiel’s Muteness. As for you, son of man, truly, on the very day I take away from them their strength, their glorious joy, the delight of their eyes, the desire of their soul, the pride of their hearts, their sons and daughters,
text.DRB: And thou son of man, behold in the day, wherein I will take away from them their strength, and the joy of dignity, and the desire of their eyes, whereupon their souls rest, their sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: on that day, one who has escaped will come to you to report to you the news.
text.Rotherham: That in that day, one that hath escaped shall come in unto thee,—to cause thee to hear it with thine own ears?
text.KJV: That he that escapeth in that day shall come unto thee, to cause thee to hear it with thine ears?
text.NABRE: on that day a survivor will come to you so that you may hear it with your own ears.
text.DRB: In that day when one flying shall come to thee, to tell thee:

text.NRSV: On that day your mouth shall be opened to the one who has escaped, and you shall speak and no longer be silent. So you shall be a sign to them; and they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: That in that day, thy mouth shall be opened unto him that hath escaped, And thou shalt speak and not be dumb any longer? So shalt thou become to them a wonder, And they shall know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: In that day shall thy mouth be opened to him which is escaped, and thou shalt speak, and be no more dumb: and thou shalt be a sign unto them; and they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: On that day, with the survivor, your mouth shall be opened; you shall speak and be mute no longer. You shall be a sign to them, and they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.DRB: in that day, I say, shall thy mouth be opened with him that flyeth, and thou shalt speak, and shalt be silent no more: and thou shalt be unto them for a portending sign, and you shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying:
text.KJV: The word of the Lord came again unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, set your face toward the Ammonites and prophesy against them.
text.Rotherham: Son of man Set thy face against the sons of Ammon,— and prophesy against them.
text.KJV: Son of man, set thy face against the Ammonites, and prophesy against them;
text.NABRE: Son of man, turn toward the Ammonites and prophesy against them.
text.DRB: Son of man, set thy face against the children of Ammon, & thou shalt prophesy of them.

text.NRSV: Say to the Ammonites, Hear the word of the Lord God: Thus says the Lord God, Because you said, “Aha!” over my sanctuary when it was profaned, and over the land of Israel when it was made desolate, and over the house of Judah when it went into exile;
text.Rotherham: So then thou shalt say to the sons of Ammon, Hear ye the word of My Lord Yahweh: Thus, saith My Lord. Yahweh: Because thou saidst Aha! Against my sanctuary when it was profaned. And against the soil of Israel when it was laid waste, And against the house of Judah when they went into exile.
text.KJV: And say unto the Ammonites, Hear the word of the Lord God; Thus saith the Lord God; Because thou saidst, Aha, against my sanctuary, when it was profaned; and against the land of Israel, when it was desolate; and against the house of Judah, when they went into captivity;
text.NABRE: Say to the Ammonites: Hear the word of the Lord! Thus says the Lord God: Because you jeered at my sanctuary when it was desecrated, at the land of Israel when it was destroyed, and at the house of Judah when they went into exile,
text.DRB: And thou shalt say to the children of Ammon: Hear ye the word of our Lord God: Thus saith our Lord God: For that thou hast said: Ha, ha, upon my sanctuary, because it is polluted: and upon the Land of Israel, because it is made desolate: and upon the house of Juda, because they are led into captivity:

text.NRSV: therefore I am handing you over to the people of the east for a possession. They shall set their encampments among you and pitch their tents in your midst; they shall eat your fruit, and they shall drink your milk.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, behold me! delivering thee up to the Sons of the East for a possession And they shall set their encampments in thee, And place in thee their habitations, They, shall eat thy fruits, And they, shall drink thy milk;
text.KJV: Behold, therefore I will deliver thee to the men of the east for a possession, and they shall set their palaces in thee, and make their dwellings in thee: they shall eat thy fruit, and they shall drink thy milk.
text.NABRE: therefore I am giving you to people from the east as a possession. They shall set up their encampments among you and pitch their tents in your midst; they shall eat your produce and drink your milk.
text.DRB: Therefore will I deliver thee to the children of the east for an inheritance, and they shall place their sheepcotes in thee, and shall set their tents in thee: they shall eat thy fruits: and they shall drink thy milk.

text.NRSV: I will make Rabbah a pasture for camels and Ammon a fold for flocks. Then you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And I will make of Rabbah a home for camels, And the sons of Ammon a couching-place for flocks, So shall ye know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: And I will make Rabbah a stable for camels, and the Ammonites a couchingplace for flocks: and ye shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: And I will turn Rabbah into a pasture for camels and all of Ammon into a grazing place for flocks. Then you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.DRB: And I will give Rabbath to be an habitation of camels, and the children of Ammon to be a lying place for beasts: and you shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord God: Because you have clapped your hands and stamped your feet and rejoiced with all the malice within you against the land of Israel,
text.Rotherham: For, thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Because thou didst clap thy hands and stamp with thy foot,—and didst rejoice with all thy contempt in thy soul, against the soil of Israel.
text.KJV: For thus saith the Lord God; Because thou hast clapped thine hands, and stamped with the feet, and rejoiced in heart with all thy despite against the land of Israel;
text.NABRE: For thus says the Lord God: Because you rejoiced over the land of Israel with scorn in your heart, clapping your hands and stamping your feet,
text.DRB: Because thus saith our Lord God: For that thou hast clapped with the hand, and strucken with the foot, and hast been glad with all thy affection upon the land of Israel:

text.NRSV: therefore I have stretched out my hand against you, and will hand you over as plunder to the nations. I will cut you off from the peoples and will make you perish out of the countries; I will destroy you. Then you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Therefore behold me! I have stretched out my hand over thee And will deliver thee for a prey to the nations, And will cut thee off from among the peoples, And will cause thee to perish from among the lands,— I will destroy thee, So shalt thou know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: Behold, therefore I will stretch out mine hand upon thee, and will deliver thee for a spoil to the heathen; and I will cut thee off from the people, and I will cause thee to perish out of the countries: I will destroy thee; and thou shalt know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: therefore, see, I am stretching out my hand against you and giving you up as plunder to the nations. I will cut you off from the peoples and wipe you out of the lands. I will destroy you, and you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.DRB: therefore behold I will stretch forth my hand upon thee, and will deliver thee into the spoil of the Gentiles, and will kill thee out of the peoples, and destroy thee out of the lands, and break thee: and thou shalt know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: Because Moab said, The house of Judah is like all the other nations,
text.Rotherham: Thus saith My Lord Yahweh: Because Moab and Seir say, Lo! like all the nations, is the house of Judah.
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord God; Because that Moab and Seir do say, Behold, the house of Judah is like unto all the heathen;
text.NABRE: Against Moab. Thus says the Lord God: Because Moab said, “See! the house of Judah is like all the other nations,”
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord God: For that Moab, and Seir have said: Behold as all the Gentiles, so is the house of Juda:

text.NRSV: therefore I will lay open the flank of Moab from the towns on its frontier, the glory of the country, Beth-jeshimoth, Baal-meon, and Kiriathaim.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, behold me! laying open the side of Moab out of the cities, Out of his cities on his frontiers, The beauty of the land of Beth-jeshimoth, Baal-meon, and Kiriathaim:
text.KJV: Therefore, behold, I will open the side of Moab from the cities, from his cities which are on his frontiers, the glory of the country, Beth–jeshimoth, Baal–meon, and Kiriathaim,
text.NABRE: therefore, I am exposing the whole flank of Moab with its cities, the jewels of its land: Beth-jesimoth, Baalmeon, and Kiriathaim.
text.DRB: therefore behold I will open the shoulder of Moab of his cities, of his cities I say, and of his borders the noble cities of the land Bethiesimoth, and Beelmeon, and Cariathaim,

text.NRSV: I will give it along with Ammon to the people of the east as a possession. Thus Ammon shall be remembered no more among the nations,
text.Rotherham: To the Sons of the East [when they come] against the sons of Ammon, and I will deliver it up for possession,-to the end the sons of Ammon may not be remembered among the nations:
text.KJV: Unto the men of the east with the Ammonites, and will give them in possession, that the Ammonites may not be remembered among the nations.
text.NABRE: I will hand it over, along with the Ammonites, to the people from the east that it may not be remembered among the nations.
text.DRB: to the children of the East with the children of Ammon, & I will give it for an inheritance: that there may be memory no more of the children of Ammon among the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: and I will execute judgments upon Moab. Then they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Also upon Moab, will I execute judgments,- So shall they know that I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: And I will execute judgments upon Moab; and they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: I will execute judgment upon Moab that they may know that I am the Lord.
text.DRB: And in Moab I will do judgements: and they shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: Because Edom acted revengefully against the house of Judah and has grievously offended in taking vengeance upon them,
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith My Lord. Yahweh, Because of what Edom hath done, in taking vengeance on the house of Judah, so that they have become guilty again and again and have taken vengeance upon them,
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord God; Because that Edom hath dealt against the house of Judah by taking vengeance, and hath greatly offended, and revenged himself upon them;
text.NABRE: Against Edom. Thus says the Lord God: Because Edom took vengeance on the house of Judah and incurred terrible guilt by taking vengeance on them,
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord God: For that Idumea hath made revenge to revenge herself of the children of Juda, and hath sinned offending, & hath sought revenge of them,

text.NRSV: therefore thus says the Lord God, I will stretch out my hand against Edom, and cut off from it humans and animals, and I will make it desolate; from Teman even to Dedan they shall fall by the sword.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, I will therefore stretch forth my hand over Edom, and will cut off therefrom man and beast, And will deliver it up as a desolation, from Teman, And they of Dedan, by the sword, shall fall,
text.KJV: Therefore thus saith the Lord God; I will also stretch out mine hand upon Edom, and will cut off man and beast from it; and I will make it desolate from Teman; and they of Dedan shall fall by the sword.
text.NABRE: therefore thus says the Lord God: I will stretch out my hand against Edom and cut off from it human being and beast alike. I will turn it into ruins from Teman to Dedan; they shall fall by the sword.
text.DRB: therefore thus saith our Lord God: I will stretch forth my hand upon Idumea, and will take away out of it man, and beast, and will make it desert from the South: and they that are in Dedan, shall fall by the sword.

text.NRSV: I will lay my vengeance upon Edom by the hand of my people Israel; and they shall act in Edom according to my anger and according to my wrath; and they shall know my vengeance, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: And I will put forth mine avenging against Edom, by the hand of my people Israel, And they shall deal with Edom, according to mine anger and according to mine indignation,- So shall they know mine avenging, Declareth My Lord. Yahweh,
text.KJV: And I will lay my vengeance upon Edom by the hand of my people Israel: and they shall do in Edom according to mine anger and according to my fury; and they shall know my vengeance, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: I will put my vengeance against Edom into the hands of my people Israel; they will deal with Edom in accord with my furious anger. Thus they shall know my vengeance!—oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: And I will give my revenge upon Idumea by the hand of my people Israel: and they shall do in Edom according to my wrath, and my fury: and they shall know my vengeance, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: Because with unending hostilities the Philistines acted in vengeance, and with malice of heart took revenge in destruction;
text.Rotherham: Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, Because of what the Philistines have done by way of vengeance,- In that they have taken vengeance with contempt in the soul, to destroy, with the enmity of age-past times.
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord God; Because the Philistines have dealt by revenge, and have taken vengeance with a despiteful heart, to destroy it for the old hatred;
text.NABRE: Against the Philistines. Thus says the Lord God: Because the Philistines acted vengefully and exacted vengeance with intentional malice, destroying with undying hostility,
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord God: For that the Palesthines have made revenge, and have revenged themselves with all their mind, killing, and accomplishing old enmities:

text.NRSV: therefore thus says the Lord God, I will stretch out my hand against the Philistines, cut off the Cherethites, and destroy the rest of the seacoast.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus, saith My Lord. Yahweh, Behold me! stretching forth my hand over the Philistines, And I will cut off the Cherethim,— And will cause to perish the remnant of the coast of the sea;
text.KJV: Therefore thus saith the Lord God; Behold, I will stretch out mine hand upon the Philistines, and I will cut off the Cherethims, and destroy the remnant of the sea coast.
text.NABRE: therefore thus says the Lord God: See! I am stretching out my hand against the Philistines, and I will cut off the Cherethites and wipe out the remnant on the seacoast.
text.DRB: therefore thus saith our Lord God: Behold I will stretch forth my hand upon the Palesthines, and will kill the killers, and will destroy the remnant of the sea coast,

text.NRSV: I will execute great vengeance on them with wrathful punishments. Then they shall know that I am the Lord, when I lay my vengeance on them.
text.Rotherham: And I will execute upon them great vengeance, with rebukes of indignation: shall they know that I am Yahweh, when I put forth mine avenging against them.
text.KJV: And I will execute great vengeance upon them with furious rebukes; and they shall know that I am the Lord, when I shall lay my vengeance upon them.
text.NABRE: Thus I will execute great acts of vengeance on them, punishing them furiously. Then they shall know that I am the Lord, when I wreak my vengeance on them.
text.DRB: and I will make in them great revengements arguing in fury: and they shall know that I am the Lord, when I shall have given my vengeance upon them.

text.NRSV: In the eleventh year, on the first day of the month, the word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass in the eleventh year, on the first of the month, that the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying:
text.KJV: And it came to pass in the eleventh year, in the first day of the month, that the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: On the first day of the eleventh month of the eleventh year, the word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And it came to pass in the eleventh year, the first of the month, the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, because Tyre said concerning Jerusalem, “Aha, broken is the gateway of the peoples; it has swung open to me; I shall be replenished, now that it is wasted.”
text.Rotherham: Son of man, Because Tyre hath said concerning Jerusalem. Aha! She is broken that was the doors of the peoples. She is turned unto me,— I shall be filled, She is laid waste,
text.KJV: Son of man, because that Tyrus hath said against Jerusalem, Aha, she is broken that was the gates of the people: she is turned unto me: I shall be replenished, now she is laid waste:
text.NABRE: Son of man, because Tyre said of Jerusalem: “Aha! The gateway of the peoples is smashed! It has been turned over to me; I will be enriched by its ruin!”
text.DRB: Son of man for that Tyre hath said of Jerusalem: Ah the gates of the peoples are broken, she is turned to me: I shall be filled, she is desert.

text.NRSV: Therefore, thus says the Lord God: See, I am against you, O Tyre! I will hurl many nations against you, as the sea hurls its waves.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Behold me! against thee O Tyre,— So then I will bring up against thee many nations, As the sea bringeth up its rolling waves;
text.KJV: Therefore thus saith the Lord God; Behold, I am against thee, O Tyrus, and will cause many nations to come up against thee, as the sea causeth his waves to come up.
text.NABRE: therefore thus says the Lord God: See! I am coming against you, Tyre; I will churn up against you many nations, just as the sea churns up its waves.
text.DRB: Therefore thus saith our Lord God: Behold I upon thee o Tyre, and I will make many nations come up to thee, as the sea riseth up swelling.

text.NRSV: They shall destroy the walls of Tyre and break down its towers. I will scrape its soil from it and make it a bare rock.
text.Rotherham: And they shall destroy the walls of Tyre. And break down her towers, And I will scrape clean her dust from off her, —And make of her the glaring face of a cliff:
text.KJV: And they shall destroy the walls of Tyrus, and break down her towers: I will also scrape her dust from her, and make her like the top of a rock.
text.NABRE: They will destroy the walls of Tyre and tear down its towers; I will scrape off its debris and leave it a bare rock.
text.DRB: And they shall dissipate the walls of Tyre, and shall destroy the towers thereof: and I will scrape her dust from her, and will make her as a most clear rock.

text.NRSV: It shall become, in the midst of the sea, a place for spreading nets. I have spoken, says the Lord God. It shall become plunder for the nations,
text.Rotherham: A place for the spreading of nets, shall she become, in the midst of the sea, For, I, have spoken Declareth My Lord. Yahweh,-And she shall become a prey to the nations.
text.KJV: It shall be a place for the spreading of nets in the midst of the sea: for I have spoken it, saith the Lord God: and it shall become a spoil to the nations.
text.NABRE: It will become a place for drying nets in the midst of the sea. For I have spoken—oracle of the Lord God: she will become plunder for the nations.
text.DRB: The drying of nets shall be in the midst of the sea, because I have spoken, saith our Lord God: and she shall be for a spoil to the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: and its daughter-towns in the country shall be killed by the sword. Then they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And her daughters that are in the field: with the sword shall be slain: So shall they know that I am Yahweh.
text.KJV: And her daughters which are in the field shall be slain by the sword; and they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: Her daughter cities on the mainland will be slaughtered by the sword; then they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.DRB: Her daughters also that be in the field, shall be slain by the sword: and they shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord God: I will bring against Tyre from the north King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon, king of kings, together with horses, chariots, cavalry, and a great and powerful army.
text.Rotherham: For thus saith My Lord. Yahweh, Behold me! bringing against Tyre. Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon out of the North. a king of kings,—with horses and with chariots and with horsemen, and a gathered host, and much people.
text.KJV: For thus saith the Lord God; Behold, I will bring upon Tyrus Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon, a king of kings, from the north, with horses, and with chariots, and with horsemen, and companies, and much people.
text.NABRE: Indeed thus says the Lord God: I am bringing up against Tyre from the north, Nebuchadnezzar, King of Babylon, king of kings, with horses and chariots, with cavalry, and a mighty horde of troops.
text.DRB: Because thus saith our Lord God: Behold I will bring to Tyre Nabuchodonosor the king of Babylon from the North, the king of kings with horses, & chariots, and horsemen, and multitude, & a great people.

text.NRSV: Your daughter-towns in the country he shall put to the sword. He shall set up a siege wall against you, cast up a ramp against you, and raise a roof of shields against you.
text.Rotherham: Thy daughters in the field—with the sword, shall he slay,- And shall set against thee a siege-wall And cast up against thee an earthwork, And set up against thee a large shield;
text.KJV: He shall slay with the sword thy daughters in the field: and he shall make a fort against thee, and cast a mount against thee, and lift up the buckler against thee.
text.NABRE: Your daughter cities on the mainland he shall slay with the sword. He shall build a siege wall around you, throw up a ramp against you, and raise his shields about you.
text.DRB: Thy daughters that are in the field, he shall kill with the sword: & he shall compass thee with munitions, and shall cast up a mount round about: and he shall lift up the buckler against thee.

text.NRSV: He shall direct the shock of his battering rams against your walls and break down your towers with his axes.
text.Rotherham: And the stroke of his attacking-engine, will he direct against thy walls,— And thy towers, will he break down with his axes.
text.KJV: And he shall set engines of war against thy walls, and with his axes he shall break down thy towers.
text.NABRE: He shall pound your walls with battering-rams and break down your towers with his axes.
text.DRB: And he shall dispose ordinances, and engines against thy walls, and shall destroy thy towers with his armour.

text.NRSV: His horses shall be so many that their dust shall cover you. At the noise of cavalry, wheels, and chariots your very walls shall shake, when he enters your gates like those entering a breached city.
text.Rotherham: By reason of the multitude of his horses, their dust shall cover thee,— At the noise of horseman and wheel and chariot, shall thy walls tremble, when he entereth into thy gates Just as they do who enter a city broken open.
text.KJV: By reason of the abundance of his horses their dust shall cover thee: thy walls shall shake at the noise of the horsemen, and of the wheels, and of the chariots, when he shall enter into thy gates, as men enter into a city wherein is made a breach.
text.NABRE: From the surging of his horses he will cover you with dust; from the noise of warhorses, wheels and chariots. Your walls will shake when he enters your gates, even as one enters a city that is breached.
text.DRB: With the inundation of his horses, the dust of them shall cover thee: at the sound of the horsemen, and wheels, and chariots thy walls shall be moved, when he shall go in at thy gates, as by the entrance of a city destroyed.

text.NRSV: With the hoofs of his horses he shall trample all your streets. He shall put your people to the sword, and your strong pillars shall fall to the ground.
text.Rotherham: With the hoofs of his horses, shall he tread down all thy streets,— Thy people-with the sword, shall he slay, And thy pillars of strength to the earth, shall go down.
text.KJV: With the hoofs of his horses shall he tread down all thy streets: he shall slay thy people by the sword, and thy strong garrisons shall go down to the ground.
text.NABRE: With the hooves of his horses he will trample all your streets; Your people he will slay by the sword; your mighty pillars will collapse.
text.DRB: With the hoofs of his horses he shall tread down all thy streets: thy people he shall kill with the sword, and thy noble statues shall fall to the ground.

text.NRSV: They will plunder your riches and loot your merchandise; they shall break down your walls and destroy your fine houses. Your stones and timber and soil they shall cast into the water.
text.Rotherham: And they shall spoil thy wealth And make a prey of thy merchandise, And break down thy walls And thy pleasant houses, shall they lay in ruins; And thy stones and thy timber and thy dust—in the midst of the waters, shall they lay.
text.KJV: And they shall make a spoil of thy riches, and make a prey of thy merchandise: and they shall break down thy walls, and destroy thy pleasant houses: and they shall lay thy stones and thy timber and thy dust in the midst of the water.
text.NABRE: They shall plunder your wealth and pillage your goods; They will tear down your walls and demolish your splendid houses. Your stones, timbers, and debris they will cast into the sea.
text.DRB: They shall waste thy riches, they shall spoil thy merchandise: and they shall destroy thy walls, and shall overthrow thy goodly houses: and thy stones, and thy timber, and thy dust they shall put in the midst of the waters.

text.NRSV: I will silence the music of your songs; the sound of your lyres shall be heard no more.
text.Rotherham: And I will cause to cease the hum of thy songs; And the sound of thy lyres, shall not be heard any more.
text.KJV: And I will cause the noise of thy songs to cease; and the sound of thy harps shall be no more heard.
text.NABRE: I will bring an end to the noise of your songs; the music of your lyres will be heard no more.
text.DRB: And I will make the multitude of thy songs to cease, and the sound of thy harps shall be heard no more.

text.NRSV: I will make you a bare rock; you shall be a place for spreading nets. You shall never again be rebuilt, for I the Lord have spoken, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: And I will make of thee the glaring face of a cliff. A place for the spreading of nets, shalt thou become, Thou shalt not be but any more, For I. Yahweh have spoken it, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: And I will make thee like the top of a rock: thou shalt be a place to spread nets upon; thou shalt be built no more: for I the Lord have spoken it, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: I will turn you into bare rock, you will become a place for drying nets. You shall never be rebuilt, for I the Lord have spoken— oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: And I will make thee as a most clear rock, drying of nets shall thou be, neither shalt thou be built any more: because I have spoken, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God to Tyre: Shall not the coastlands shake at the sound of your fall, when the wounded groan, when slaughter goes on within you?
text.Rotherham: Thus saith My Lord. Yahweh, to Tyre: Shall it not be that. At the sound of thy fall. With the groan of thy pierced one. With the slaughter made in thy midst, The Coastlands shall tremble?
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord God to Tyrus; Shall not the isles shake at the sound of thy fall, when the wounded cry, when the slaughter is made in the midst of thee?
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord God to Tyre: At the sound of your downfall, at the groaning of the wounded, When victims are slain within you, will the islands not quake?
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord God to Tyre: Why, shall not the islands be moved at the sound of thy ruin, and the groaning of thy slain when they shall be killed in the midst of thee?

text.NRSV: Then all the princes of the sea shall step down from their thrones; they shall remove their robes and strip off their embroidered garments. They shall clothe themselves with trembling, and shall sit on the ground; they shall tremble every moment, and be appalled at you.
text.Rotherham: Then shall come down from off their thrones all the princes of the sea, And shall lay aside their robes. And their embroidered garments, shall strip off,— With tremblings, shall they clothe them. selves Upon the ground, shall they sit, And shall tremble every moment and be astonished over thee.
text.KJV: Then all the princes of the sea shall come down from their thrones, and lay away their robes, and put off their broidered garments: they shall clothe themselves with trembling; they shall sit upon the ground, and shall tremble at every moment, and be astonished at thee.
text.NABRE: All the princes of the sea will step down from their thrones, Lay aside their robes, and strip off their embroidered garments. Clothed in mourning, they will sit on the ground And tremble, horror-struck and appalled at you.
text.DRB: And all the Princes of the sea shall go down from their seats: and take off their robes, and cast away their broidered garments, and be clothed with astonishment, sit on the earth, and being astonished marvel at thy sudden fall.

text.NRSV: And they shall raise a lamentation over you, and say to you: How you have vanished from the seas, O city renowned, once mighty on the sea, you and your inhabitants, who imposed your terror on all the mainland!
text.Rotherham: Then shall they take up over thee a dirge, And say to thee,- How hast thou perished, And ceased from the seas,- The city renowned which was strong in the sea She and they who dwelt in her, Who imparted their terror To all who dwelt in her.
text.KJV: And they shall take up a lamentation for thee, and say to thee, How art thou destroyed, that wast inhabited of seafaring men, the renowned city, which wast strong in the sea, she and her inhabitants, which cause their terror to be on all that haunt it!
text.NABRE: They will raise lament over you and say to you: How you have perished, gone from the seas, Renowned City! Once she was mighty on the sea, she and her inhabitants, Those who spread their terror to all who dwelt nearby.
text.DRB: And taking upon thee a lamentation, they shall say to thee: How hast thou perished that dwellest in the sea, o noble city, which hast been strong in the sea with thine inhabitants, whom all did fear?

text.NRSV: Now the coastlands tremble on the day of your fall; the coastlands by the sea are dismayed at your passing.
text.Rotherham: Now, shall tremble the Coastlands, In the day of thy fall,- And shall shudder the isles that are in the sea. At thine exit!
text.KJV: Now shall the isles tremble in the day of thy fall; yea, the isles that are in the sea shall be troubled at thy departure.
text.NABRE: On this, the day of your fall, the islands quake! The islands in the sea are terrified at your passing.
text.DRB: Now shall the ships be astonished in the day of thy fear: and the islands in the sea shall be troubled, for that none cometh forth out of thee.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord God: When I make you a city laid waste, like cities that are not inhabited, when I bring up the deep over you, and the great waters cover you,
text.Rotherham: For thus saith My Lord Yahweh, When I make thee a desolate city, Like cities which cannot be dwelt in,- When I bring up over thee the roaring deep, And the mighty waters cover thee,
text.KJV: For thus saith the Lord God; When I shall make thee a desolate city, like the cities that are not inhabited; when I shall bring up the deep upon thee, and great waters shall cover thee;
text.NABRE: Indeed thus says the Lord God: When I make you a ruined city like cities no longer inhabited, When I churn up the deep and its mighty waters cover you,
text.DRB: Because thus saith our Lord God: When I shall make thee a desolate city as the cities that are not inhabited: and shall bring upon thee the depth, & many waters shall cover thee:

text.NRSV: then I will thrust you down with those who descend into the Pit, to the people of long ago, and I will make you live in the world below, among primeval ruins, with those who go down to the Pit, so that you will not be inhabited or have a place in the land of the living.
text.Rotherham: Then will I bring thee down with them that go down into the pit. Unto the people of age-past times And cause thee to dwell in the earth below Among the desolations from age-past times With them that go down into the pit That thou mayest not be dwelt in, Nor yet present thyself in the land of the living.
text.KJV: When I shall bring thee down with them that descend into the pit, with the people of old time, and shall set thee in the low parts of the earth, in places desolate of old, with them that go down to the pit, that thou be not inhabited; and I shall set glory in the land of the living;
text.NABRE: Then I will thrust you down with those who go down to the pit, to those of the bygone age; I will make you dwell in the netherworld, in the everlasting ruins, with those who have gone down to the pit, So you will never return or have a place in the land of the living.
text.DRB: and shall pluck thee down with those, that descend into the lake to the everlasting people, and shall place thee in the lowest ground as old desolations, with them that are brought down into the lake, that thou be not inhabited: moreover when I shall give glory in the land of the living,

text.NRSV: I will bring you to a dreadful end, and you shall be no more; though sought for, you will never be found again, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: A terror, will I make thee And thou shalt not be: Though thou be sought for, Thou shalt not be found any more To times age-abiding, Declareth My Lord, Yahweh.
text.KJV: I will make thee a terror, and thou shalt be no more: though thou be sought for, yet shalt thou never be found again, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: I will make you a horror, and you shall be no more; You shall be sought for, but never found again— oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: I will bring thee to nothing, & thou shalt not be, and being sought for, thou shalt not be found any more for ever, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying:
text.KJV: The word of the Lord came again unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: Now you, mortal, raise a lamentation over Tyre,
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, son of man, Take up over Tyre a dirge;
text.KJV: Now, thou son of man, take up a lamentation for Tyrus;
text.NABRE: You, son of man, raise a lament over Tyre,
text.DRB: Thou therefore o son of man, take up a lamentation upon Tyre:

text.NRSV: and say to Tyre, which sits at the entrance to the sea, merchant of the peoples on many coastlands, Thus says the Lord God: O Tyre, you have said, “I am perfect in beauty.”
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt say unto Tyre, O thou that dwellest by the entrances of the sea, Thou merchant of the peoples unto many shores,— Thus, saith My Lord, Yahweh, O Tyre, Thou saidst, I, am the perfection of beauty!
text.KJV: And say unto Tyrus, O thou that art situate at the entry of the sea, which art a merchant of the people for many isles, Thus saith the Lord God; O Tyrus, thou hast said, I am of perfect beauty.
text.NABRE: and say to Tyre, who sits at the entrance to the sea, trader to peoples on many coastlands, Thus says the Lord God: Tyre, you said, “I am a ship, perfect in beauty”;
text.DRB: And thou shalt say to Tyre, which dwelleth in the entrance of the sea, being the traffic of peoples to many islands: Thus saith our Lord God: o Tyre, thou hast said: I am of perfect beauty,

text.NRSV: Your borders are in the heart of the seas; your builders made perfect your beauty.
text.Rotherham: In the heart of the seas, were thy bounds, —Thy builders perfected thy beauty:
text.KJV: Thy borders are in the midst of the seas, thy builders have perfected thy beauty.
text.NABRE: In the heart of the sea was your territory; your builders perfected your beauty.
text.DRB: & situated in the heart of the sea. Thy borderers, which builded thee have accomplished thy beauty:

text.NRSV: They made all your planks of fir trees from Senir; they took a cedar from Lebanon to make a mast for you.
text.Rotherham: With fir-trees from Senir, but they for thee all the two-decked vessels,— Cedar from Lebanon, fetched they, to make masts for thee:
text.KJV: They have made all thy ship boards of fir trees of Senir: they have taken cedars from Lebanon to make masts for thee.
text.NABRE: With juniper wood from Senir they built all your decks; A cedar from Lebanon they took to make you a mast.
text.DRB: with the fir trees of Sanir they builded thee, with all the boards of the sea: they took the cedar from Libanus, to make thee a mast.

text.NRSV: From oaks of Bashan they made your oars; they made your deck of pines from the coasts of Cyprus, inlaid with ivory.
text.Rotherham: With oaks from Bashan, made they thine oars,— Thy benches, made they of ivory, inlaid with boxwood, From the shores of the West:
text.KJV: Of the oaks of Bashan have they made thine oars; the company of the Ashurites have made thy benches of ivory, brought out of the isles of Chittim.
text.NABRE: With oaks of Bashan they fashioned your oars, Your bridge, of ivory-inlaid cypress wood from the coasts of Kittim.
text.DRB: Oaks of Basan they have hewed for thine oars: & thy banks they have made thee of the ivory of India, and little cabins of the islands of Italy.

text.NRSV: Of fine embroidered linen from Egypt was your sail, serving as your ensign; blue and purple from the coasts of Elishah was your awning.
text.Rotherham: Of fine linen with embroidered work from Egypt, was thy sail, To serve thee for ensign,— Blue and purple from the shores of Aeolis became thine awning:
text.KJV: Fine linen with broidered work from Egypt was that which thou spreadest forth to be thy sail; blue and purple from the isles of Elishah was that which covered thee.
text.NABRE: Fine embroidered linen from Egypt became your sail; Your awnings were made of purple and scarlet from the coasts of Elishah.
text.DRB: Various silk of Ægypt was woven for thy sail, to be put on the mast: hyacinth, and purple out of the islands of Elisa, were made thy covering.

text.NRSV: The inhabitants of Sidon and Arvad were your rowers; skilled men of Zemer were within you, they were your pilots.
text.Rotherham: the inhabitants of Zidon and Arvad, Became rowers for thee,— Thine own skilled men O Tyre, were within thee, They were thy sailors:
text.KJV: The inhabitants of Zidon and Arvad were thy mariners: thy wise men, O Tyrus, that were in thee, were thy pilots.
text.NABRE: Inhabitants of Sidon and Arvad were your oarsmen; Your own sages, Tyre, were on board, serving as your sailors.
text.DRB: The inhabitants of Sidon, and the Aradians were thy rowers: thy wise men, o Tyre, were made thy governors.

text.NRSV: The elders of Gebal and its artisans were within you, caulking your seams; all the ships of the sea with their mariners were within you, to barter for your wares.
text.Rotherham: the elders of Byblus and the skilled men thereof, were within thee, Thy calkers,— All the ships of the sea and their mariners, were within thee, To carry on thy traffic,
text.KJV: The ancients of Gebal and the wise men thereof were in thee thy calkers: all the ships of the sea with their mariners were in thee to occupy thy merchandise.
text.NABRE: The elders and sages of Gebal were with you to caulk your seams. Every ship and sailor on the sea came to you to carry on trade.
text.DRB: The ancients of Gebal, & wisemen thereof had mariners to the service of thy diverse stuff: all the ships of the sea, and their mariners have been in the people of thy traffic.

text.NRSV: Paras and Lud and Put were in your army, your mighty warriors; they hung shield and helmet in you; they gave you splendor.
text.Rotherham: They of Persia and Lud and Phut were in thine army, Thy men of war,— Shield and helmet, hung they up within thee, They set forth thy splendour:
text.KJV: They of Persia and of Lud and of Phut were in thine army, thy men of war: they hanged the shield and helmet in thee; they set forth thy comeliness.
text.NABRE: Persia and Lud and Put were warriors in your army; Shield and helmet they hung on you to enhance your splendor.
text.DRB: The Persians, and Lydians, and the Libyans were in thine army thy men of war: the buckler, and helmet they did hang in thee for thine ornament.

text.NRSV: Men of Arvad and Helech were on your walls all around; men of Gamad were at your towers. They hung their quivers all around your walls; they made perfect your beauty.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Arvad,:with thine army, were upon thy walls round about, And valorous in thy towers,— Their shields, hung they up on thy walls round about, They perfected thy beauty:
text.KJV: The men of Arvad with thine army were upon thy walls round about, and the Gammadims were in thy towers: they hanged their shields upon thy walls round about; they have made thy beauty perfect.
text.NABRE: The men of Arvad and Helech were on your walls all around And Gamadites on your towers; they hung their shields around your walls, they made your beauty perfect.
text.DRB: The children of Arad were with thine army upon thy walls round about: yea and the Pygmeians, that were in thy towers, hung up their quivers on thy walls round about: they accomplished thy beauty.

text.NRSV: Tarshish did business with you out of the abundance of your great wealth; silver, iron, tin, and lead they exchanged for your wares.
text.Rotherham: Tarshish was a trader of thine. For the abundance of all wealth,— With silver iron. tin and lead, Took they part in thy traffic:
text.KJV: Tarshish was thy merchant by reason of the multitude of all kind of riches; with silver, iron, tin, and lead, they traded in thy fairs.
text.NABRE: Tarshish traded with you, so great was your wealth, Exchanging for your wares silver, iron, tin, and lead.
text.DRB: They of Carthage thy merchants, for the multitude of all riches, with silver, iron, tin, and lead did they replenish thy marts.

text.NRSV: Javan, Tubal, and Meshech traded with you; they exchanged human beings and vessels of bronze for your merchandise.
text.Rotherham: Javan. Tubal and Meshech, They were merchants of thine,— With the persons of men and vessels of bronze, Shared they in thy barter:
text.KJV: Javan, Tubal, and Meshech, they were thy merchants: they traded the persons of men and vessels of brass in thy market.
text.NABRE: Javan, Tubal, and Meshech also traded with you, Exchanging slaves and bronze vessels for your merchandise.
text.DRB: Greece, Thubal, and Mosoch, they were thy merchants: slaves, and vessels of brass they brought to thy people.

text.NRSV: Beth-togarmah exchanged for your wares horses, war horses, and mules.
text.Rotherham: Some of the house of Togarmah, with horses and horsemen and mules, Took part in thy traffic:
text.KJV: They of the house of Togarmah traded in thy fairs with horses and horsemen and mules.
text.NABRE: Horses, steeds, and mules from Beth-togarmah were exchanged for your wares.
text.DRB: From the house of Thogorma they brought horses, and horsemen, and mules to thy market.

text.NRSV: The Rhodians traded with you; many coastlands were your own special markets; they brought you in payment ivory tusks and ebony.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Dedan were merchants of thine, Many, isles, took the merchandise of thy hand, —Horns of ivory and ebony, gave they back to thee in exchange:
text.KJV: The men of Dedan were thy merchants; many isles were the merchandise of thine hand: they brought thee for a present horns of ivory and ebony.
text.NABRE: Men of Rhodes trafficked with you; many coastlands were your agents; Ivory tusks and ebony wood they brought back as your payment.
text.DRB: The children of Dedan were thy merchants: many islands the traffic of thy hand, teeth of ivory, and of Heben they exchanged for thy price.

text.NRSV: Edom did business with you because of your abundant goods; they exchanged for your wares turquoise, purple, embroidered work, fine linen, coral, and rubies.
text.Rotherham: Syria, was a trader of thine For the abundance of thy manufactures,- With carbuncle and purple and embroidery and fine linen and coral and rubies, Took they part in thy traffic:
text.KJV: Syria was thy merchant by reason of the multitude of the wares of thy making: they occupied in thy fairs with emeralds, purple, and broidered work, and fine linen, and coral, and agate.
text.NABRE: Edom traded with you for your many wares: garnets, purple dye, embroidered cloth, Fine linen, coral, and rubies they gave you as merchandise.
text.DRB: The Syrian was thy merchant for the multitude of thy works, the precious stone, and purple, and branched works, and fine linen, and silk, and chodchod they did set forth in thy market.

text.NRSV: Judah and the land of Israel traded with you; they exchanged for your merchandise wheat from Minnith, millet, honey, oil, and balm.
text.Rotherham: Judah and the land of Israel, They, were merchants of thine,— With the wheat of Minnith and the sweets of pannag and honey and oil and balsam, Shared they in thy barter:
text.KJV: Judah, and the land of Israel, they were thy merchants: they traded in thy market wheat of Minnith, and Pannag, and honey, and oil, and balm.
text.NABRE: Judah and the land of Israel trafficked with you: Minnith wheat, grain, honey, oil, and balm they gave you as merchandise.
text.DRB: Juda and the land of Israel they were thy merchants in the principal corn: balm, and honey, and oil, and resin they did set forth in thy marts.

text.NRSV: Damascus traded with you for your abundant goods—because of your great wealth of every kind—wine of Helbon, and white wool.
text.Rotherham: Damascus was a trader of thine For the multitude of thy manufactures For the multitude of all wealth,- With the wine of Helbon and white wool:
text.KJV: Damascus was thy merchant in the multitude of the wares of thy making, for the multitude of all riches; in the wine of Helbon, and white wool.
text.NABRE: Damascus traded with you for your many wares, so great was your wealth, exchanging Helbon wine and Zahar wool.
text.DRB: The Damacene was thy merchant in the multitude of thy works, in the multitude of diverse riches, in fat wine, in wools of the best colour.

text.NRSV: Vedan and Javan from Uzal entered into trade for your wares; wrought iron, cassia, and sweet cane were bartered for your merchandise.
text.Rotherham: Wedan and Javan, from Uzal, Brought into thy traffic,— Steel, cassia and calamus, Were, in thy merchandise:
text.KJV: Dan also and Javan going to and fro occupied in thy fairs: bright iron, cassia, and calamus, were in thy market.
text.NABRE: Javan exchanged wrought iron, cassia, and aromatic cane from Uzal for your wares.
text.DRB: Dan, and Greece, and Mosel in thy marts have set forth wrought iron: stacte, and calamus were in thy merchandise.

text.NRSV: Dedan traded with you in saddlecloths for riding.
text.Rotherham: Dedan was a merchant of thine,- With spreading wraps for riding:
text.KJV: Dedan was thy merchant in precious clothes for chariots.
text.NABRE: Dedan traded with you for riding gear.
text.DRB: Dedan thy merchants in tapestry for seats.

text.NRSV: Arabia and all the princes of Kedar were your favored dealers in lambs, rams, and goats; in these they did business with you.
text.Rotherham: Arabia and all the princes of Kedar, They, were traders at thy service,— With lambs and rams and he-goats, With these, were they traders of thine:
text.KJV: Arabia, and all the princes of Kedar, they occupied with thee in lambs, and rams, and goats: in these were they thy merchants.
text.NABRE: Arabia and the sheikhs of Kedar were your agents, dealing in lambs, rams, and goats.
text.DRB: Arabia, and all the Princes of Cedar, they were the merchants of thy hand: with lambs, and rams, and kids thy merchants came to thee.

text.NRSV: The merchants of Sheba and Raamah traded with you; they exchanged for your wares the best of all kinds of spices, and all precious stones, and gold.
text.Rotherham: The merchants of Sheba and Raamah, They were merchants of thine,— With the chief of all spices and with every kind of precious stone and gold, Took they part in thy traffic:
text.KJV: The merchants of Sheba and Raamah, they were thy merchants: they occupied in thy fairs with chief of all spices, and with all precious stones, and gold.
text.NABRE: The merchants of Sheba and Raamah also traded with you, exchanging for your wares the very best spices, all kinds of precious stones, and gold.
text.DRB: The sellers of Saba, and Reema, they were thy merchants: with principal spices, and precious stone, and gold, which they did set forth in thy market.

text.NRSV: Haran, Canneh, Eden, the merchants of Sheba, Asshur, and Chilmad traded with you.
text.Rotherham: Haran and Canneh and Eden, the merchants of Sheba,—Asshur. Chilmad, Were merchants of thine:
text.KJV: Haran, and Canneh, and Eden, the merchants of Sheba, Asshur, and Chilmad, were thy merchants.
text.NABRE: Haran, Canneh, and Eden, the merchants of Sheba, Asshur, and Chilmad,
text.DRB: Haran, and Chene, and Eden, thy merchants: Saba, Assur, and Chelmad thy sellers.

text.NRSV: These traded with you in choice garments, in clothes of blue and embroidered work, and in carpets of colored material, bound with cords and made secure; in these they traded with you.
text.Rotherham: Those were thy merchants for gorgeous garments, for wrappings of blue and embroidery, and for treasure-chests of variegated cloth,—for cords, twisted and strong, in thy market:
text.KJV: These were thy merchants in all sorts of things, in blue clothes, and broidered work, and in chests of rich apparel, bound with cords, and made of cedar, among thy merchandise.
text.NABRE: Traded with you, marketing rich garments, purple cloth, embroidered fabric, varicolored carpets, and braided cords.
text.DRB: They were thy merchants in diverse manner, with folded pieces of hyacinth, and of embroidered cloths, and of precious riches, which were wrapped up, and bound with cords: cedars also they had in thy merchandise.

text.NRSV: The ships of Tarshish traveled for you in your trade. So you were filled and heavily laden in the heart of the seas.
text.Rotherham: the ships of Tarshish, were thy masts Carrying on thy barter, So wast thou filled and becamest very glorious In the heart of the seas.
text.KJV: The ships of Tarshish did sing of thee in thy market: and thou wast replenished, and made very glorious in the midst of the seas.
text.NABRE: The ships of Tarshish sailed for you with your goods; You were full and heavily laden in the heart of the sea.
text.DRB: The ships of the sea, thy chief in thy merchandise: and thou was replenished, and glorified exceedingly in the heart of the sea.

text.NRSV: Your rowers have brought you into the high seas. The east wind has wrecked you in the heart of the seas.
text.Rotherham: Into mighty waters, have they brought thee, the rowers—even thee,— the east wind, hath broken thee In the heart of the seas:
text.KJV: Thy rowers have brought thee into great waters: the east wind hath broken thee in the midst of the seas.
text.NABRE: Out into deep waters your oarsmen brought you; The east wind shattered you in the heart of the sea.
