text.NABRE: Was it not enough for you to graze on the best pasture, that you had to trample the rest of your pastures with your hooves? Or to drink the clearest water, that you had to pollute the rest with your hooves?
text.DRB: Was it not enough for you to feed upon good pastures? You have besides also trodden down with your feet the residue of your pastures: and when you drank most pure water, the rest you troubled with your feet.

text.NRSV: And must my sheep eat what you have trodden with your feet, and drink what you have fouled with your feet?
text.Rotherham: And my flock On what hath been trampled down by your feet, may feed, And of what hath been fouled by your feet, may drink?
text.KJV: And as for my flock, they eat that which ye have trodden with your feet; and they drink that which ye have fouled with your feet.
text.NABRE: Thus my flock had to graze on what your hooves had trampled and drink what your hooves had polluted.
text.DRB: And my sheep were fed with those things, which were trodden with your feet: and what your feet had troubled, that did they drink.

text.NRSV: Therefore, thus says the Lord God to them: I myself will judge between the fat sheep and the lean sheep.
text.Rotherham: Therefore Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, unto them: Here am I myself. Therefore will I judge between fat sheep and lean sheep,
text.KJV: Therefore thus saith the Lord God unto them; Behold, I, even I, will judge between the fat cattle and between the lean cattle.
text.NABRE: Therefore thus says the Lord God: Now I will judge between the fat and the lean.
text.DRB: Therefore thus saith our Lord God to you: Behold, I myself do judge between the fat beast & the lean.

text.NRSV: Because you pushed with flank and shoulder, and butted at all the weak animals with your horns until you scattered them far and wide,
text.Rotherham: Because with the side and with the shoulder, ye do thrust, And with your horns, ye do push all the sick,— Until you have scattered them abroad
text.KJV: Because ye have thrust with side and with shoulder, and pushed all the diseased with your horns, till ye have scattered them abroad;
text.NABRE: Because you push with flank and shoulder, and butt all the weak sheep with your horns until you drive them off,
text.DRB: For that you with sides & shoulders did thrust, and with your horns struck all the weak beasts, till they were dispersed abroad:

text.NRSV: I will save my flock, and they shall no longer be ravaged; and I will judge between sheep and sheep.
text.Rotherham: Therefore will I bring salvation to my flock, and they shall be no longer a prey,—but I will judge between one sheep and another,
text.KJV: Therefore will I save my flock, and they shall no more be a prey; and I will judge between cattle and cattle.
text.NABRE: I will save my flock so they can no longer be plundered; I will judge between one sheep and another.
text.DRB: I will save my flock, & it shall be no more into spoil, & I will judge between beast and beast.

text.NRSV: I will set up over them one shepherd, my servant David, and he shall feed them: he shall feed them and be their shepherd.
text.Rotherham: And I will raise up over them one shepherd And he shall tend them, Even my servant David,— He, will tend them, And, he, will become to them a shepherd;
text.KJV: And I will set up one shepherd over them, and he shall feed them, even my servant David; he shall feed them, and he shall be their shepherd.
text.NABRE: I will appoint one shepherd over them to pasture them, my servant David; he shall pasture them and be their shepherd.
text.DRB: And I will Raise up Over Them One Pastor: who shall feed them, my servant David: he shall feed them, & he shall be their Pastor.

text.NRSV: And I, the Lord, will be their God, and my servant David shall be prince among them; I, the Lord, have spoken.
text.Rotherham: And, I Yahweh, will become to them a God. my servant David, being a prince in their midst,— I Yahweh, have spoken;
text.KJV: And I the Lord will be their God, and my servant David a prince among them; I the Lord have spoken it.
text.NABRE: I, the Lord, will be their God, and my servant David will be prince in their midst. I, the Lord, have spoken.
text.DRB: And I the Lord will be their God: & my servant David the Prince in the midst of them: I the Lord have spoken.

text.NRSV: I will make with them a covenant of peace and banish wild animals from the land, so that they may live in the wild and sleep in the woods securely.
text.Rotherham: And I will solemnise for them a covenant of prosperity, And will cause to cease the mischievous wild-beast out of the land, And they shall dwell in the wilderness, securely, And shall sleep in the forests;
text.KJV: And I will make with them a covenant of peace, and will cause the evil beasts to cease out of the land: and they shall dwell safely in the wilderness, and sleep in the woods.
text.NABRE: I will make a covenant of peace with them and rid the country of wild beasts so they will dwell securely in the wilderness and sleep in the forests.
text.DRB: And I will make a covenant of peace with them, and will make the most cruel beasts to cease out of the land: & they that dwell in the desert, shall sleep secure in the forests.

text.NRSV: I will make them and the region around my hill a blessing; and I will send down the showers in their season; they shall be showers of blessing.
text.Rotherham: And I, will make them and the places round about my hill a blessing,- And I will cause the abundant rain to come down in its season abundant rains of blessing, shall they be;
text.KJV: And I will make them and the places round about my hill a blessing; and I will cause the shower to come down in his season; there shall be showers of blessing.
text.NABRE: I will settle them around my hill and send rain in its season, the blessing of abundant rain.
text.DRB: And I will put them round about my hill a blessing: and I will bring down the showers in his time, there shall be rains of blessing.

text.NRSV: The trees of the field shall yield their fruit, and the earth shall yield its increase. They shall be secure on their soil; and they shall know that I am the Lord, when I break the bars of their yoke, and save them from the hands of those who enslaved them.
text.Rotherham: And the tree of the field shall yield his fruit And, the land, shall yield her increase, And they shall remain on their own soil in security,- So shall they know that I, am Yahweh, When I have broken the bars of their yoke, And shall deliver them out of the hand of them who have been using them as slaves.
text.KJV: And the tree of the field shall yield her fruit, and the earth shall yield her increase, and they shall be safe in their land, and shall know that I am the Lord, when I have broken the bands of their yoke, and delivered them out of the hand of those that served themselves of them.
text.NABRE: The trees of the field shall bear their fruits, and the land its crops, and they shall dwell securely on their own soil. They shall know that I am the Lord when I break the bars of their yoke and deliver them from the power of those who enslaved them.
text.DRB: And the tree of the field shall give his fruit, and the earth shall give her spring, and they shall be in their land without fear: and they shall know that I am the Lord, when I shall have broken the chains of their yoke, and shall have delivered them out of the hand of those that rule over them.

text.NRSV: They shall no more be plunder for the nations, nor shall the animals of the land devour them; they shall live in safety, and no one shall make them afraid.
text.Rotherham: And they shall be no longer a prey for the nations, Nor shall the wild beast of the earth! devour them,— But they shall dwell securely with none to put them in terror.
text.KJV: And they shall no more be a prey to the heathen, neither shall the beast of the land devour them; but they shall dwell safely, and none shall make them afraid.
text.NABRE: They shall no longer be plundered by the nations nor will wild beasts devour them, but they shall dwell securely, with no one to frighten them.
text.DRB: And they shall be no more for a spoil in the Gentiles, neither shall the beasts of the earth devour them: but they shall dwell confidently without any terror.

text.NRSV: I will provide for them a splendid vegetation so that they shall no more be consumed with hunger in the land, and no longer suffer the insults of the nations.
text.Rotherham: And I will raise up unto them a plantation for fame,- And there shall be no longer the destroyed of hunger in the land, Neither shall they bear any longer the reproach of the nations.
text.KJV: And I will raise up for them a plant of renown, and they shall be no more consumed with hunger in the land, neither bear the shame of the heathen any more.
text.NABRE: I will prepare for them peaceful fields for planting so they are never again swept away by famine in the land or bear taunts from the nations.
text.DRB: And I will raise up unto them a bud of name: and they shall be no more diminished for famine in the land, neither shall they bear any more the reproach of the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: They shall know that I, the Lord their God, am with them, and that they, the house of Israel, are my people, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: So shall they know that, I, Yahweh their God am with them,- And that they are my people the house of Israel, Declareth My Lord. Yahweh.
text.KJV: Thus shall they know that I the Lord their God am with them, and that they, even the house of Israel, are my people, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: Thus they shall know that I, the Lord, their God, am with them, and that they are my people, the house of Israel—oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: And they shall know that I the Lord their God with them, and they my people the house of Israel: saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: You are my sheep, the sheep of my pasture and I am your God, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: Ye, therefore, my flock the flock of my pasture, are, men,— I, am your God, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: And ye my flock, the flock of my pasture, are men, and I am your God, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: Yes, you are my flock: you people are the flock of my pasture, and I am your God—oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: And you my flocks, the flocks of my pasture are men: and I the Lord your God, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying,
text.KJV: Moreover the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, set your face against Mount Seir, and prophesy against it,
text.Rotherham: Son of man Set thy face against Mount Seir,—and prophesy against it;
text.KJV: Son of man, set thy face against mount Seir, and prophesy against it,
text.NABRE: Son of man, set your face against Mount Seir and prophesy against it.
text.DRB: Son of man set thy face against mount Seir, and thou shalt Prophesy of it, and shalt say to it:

text.NRSV: and say to it, Thus says the Lord God: I am against you, Mount Seir; I stretch out my hand against you to make you a desolation and a waste.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt say to it Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, Behold me! against thee Mount Seir,—Therefore will I stretch out my hand against thee, And I will make thee a desolation and an astonishment:
text.KJV: And say unto it, Thus saith the Lord God; Behold, O mount Seir, I am against thee, and I will stretch out mine hand against thee, and I will make thee most desolate.
text.NABRE: Say to it: Thus says the Lord God: Watch out! I am against you, Mount Seir. I will stretch out my hand against you and turn you into a desolate waste.
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord God: Behold I to thee mount Seir, and I will stretch forth my hand upon thee, & will make thee desolate and desert.

text.NRSV: I lay your towns in ruins; you shall become a desolation, and you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thy cities, will I lay waste, And thou—a desolation, shalt become, So shalt thou know that I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: I will lay thy cities waste, and thou shalt be desolate, and thou shalt know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: Your cities I will turn into ruins, and you shall be a desolation; then you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.DRB: Thy cities I will destroy, & thou shalt be desert: & thou shalt know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: Because you cherished an ancient enmity, and gave over the people of Israel to the power of the sword at the time of their calamity, at the time of their final punishment;
text.Rotherham: Because thou hast had an age-abiding enmity, And hast delivered up the sons of Israel unto the hands of the sword,- In the time of their misfortune, In the time of the final punishment for iniquity,
text.KJV: Because thou hast had a perpetual hatred, and hast shed the blood of the children of Israel by the force of the sword in the time of their calamity, in the time that their iniquity had an end:
text.NABRE: Because you nursed a long-standing hatred and handed the Israelites over to the sword at the time of their collapse, at the time of their final punishment,
text.DRB: For that thou hast been an everlasting enemy, and hast shut up the children of Israel into the hands of the sword in the time of their affliction, in the time of extreme iniquity.

text.NRSV: therefore, as I live, says the Lord God, I will prepare you for blood, and blood shall pursue you; since you did not hate bloodshed, bloodshed shall pursue you.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, as I live, Declareth My Lord Yahweh, Surely regarding blood, will I deal with thee, Blood therefore, shall pursue thee,-Since blood thou hast not hated, Therefore blood, shall pursue thee,
text.KJV: Therefore, as I live, saith the Lord God, I will prepare thee unto blood, and blood shall pursue thee: sith thou hast not hated blood, even blood shall pursue thee.
text.NABRE: therefore, as I live—oracle of the Lord GOD—you are guilty of blood, and blood, I swear, shall pursue you.
text.DRB: Therefore live I, saith our Lord God, that I will deliver thee unto blood, & blood shall persecute thee: & whereas thou hast hated blood, blood shall persecute thee.

text.NRSV: I will make Mount Seir a waste and a desolation; and I will cut off from it all who come and go.
text.Rotherham: So then I will deliver up Mount Seir to desolation after desolation,- And will cut off therefrom him that passeth by and him that returneth,
text.KJV: Thus will I make mount Seir most desolate, and cut off from it him that passeth out and him that returneth.
text.NABRE: I will make Mount Seir a desolate waste and cut off from it anyone who travels across it and back.
text.DRB: And I will make mount Seir desolate & desert: & I will take from it the comer & goer.

text.NRSV: I will fill its mountains with the slain; on your hills and in your valleys and in all your watercourses those killed with the sword shall fall.
text.Rotherham: And I will fill his mountains with his slain,— As for thy hills and thy hollows and all thy channels, they who are thrust through by the sword shall fall therein:
text.KJV: And I will fill his mountains with his slain men: in thy hills, and in thy valleys, and in all thy rivers, shall they fall that are slain with the sword.
text.NABRE: I will fill its mountains with the slain; those slain by the sword shall fall on your hills, into your valleys and all your ravines.
text.DRB: And I will fill his mountains with his slain: in thy hills, and in thy valleys, & in the torrents shall the slain with the sword fall.

text.NRSV: I will make you a perpetual desolation, and your cities shall never be inhabited. Then you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Desolations age-abiding, will I make thee, And thy cities, shall not be inhabited,- So shall ye know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: I will make thee perpetual desolations, and thy cities shall not return: and ye shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: I will make you a desolation forever, your cities will not be inhabited, and you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.DRB: Into everlasting desolations will I deliver thee, & thy cities shall not be inhabited: & thou shalt know that I am the Lord God.

text.NRSV: Because you said, “These two nations and these two countries shall be mine, and we will take possession of them,”—although the Lord was there—
text.Rotherham: Because thou hast said, the two nations, and the two lands, mine, shall become, that we may possess it Whereas Yahweh, had been there,
text.KJV: Because thou hast said, These two nations and these two countries shall be mine, and we will possess it; whereas the Lord was there:
text.NABRE: Because you said: The two nations and the two lands belong to me; let us take possession of them—although the Lord was there—
text.DRB: For that thou hast said: The two nations, & the two lands shall be mine, & I will possess them by inheritance: whereas our Lord was there.

text.NRSV: therefore, as I live, says the Lord God, I will deal with you according to the anger and envy that you showed because of your hatred against them; and I will make myself known among you, when I judge you.
text.Rotherham: Therefore as I live, Declareth My Lord Yahweh, I will even deal according to thine anger, and according to thine envy, wherewith thou hast dealt, out of thy hatred with them,- So will I make myself known among them as soon as I shall judge thee
text.KJV: Therefore, as I live, saith the Lord God, I will even do according to thine anger, and according to thine envy which thou hast used out of thy hatred against them; and I will make myself known among them, when I have judged thee.
text.NABRE: therefore, as I live—oracle of the Lord GOD—I will deal with you according to the anger and envy you dealt out to them in your hatred, and I will make myself known to them when I execute judgment on you,
text.DRB: Therefore live I, saith our Lord God, that I will do according to thy wrath, and according to thy zeal, which thou hast used hating them: & I will be made known by them, when I shall have judged thee.

text.NRSV: You shall know that I, the Lord, have heard all the abusive speech that you uttered against the mountains of Israel, saying, “They are laid desolate, they are given us to devour.”
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt know that, I Yahweh, have heard all thy revilings which thou hast uttered against the mountains of Israel saying, They have become desolate,— To us, have they been given for food;
text.KJV: And thou shalt know that I am the Lord, and that I have heard all thy blasphemies which thou hast spoken against the mountains of Israel, saying, They are laid desolate, they are given us to consume.
text.NABRE: then you shall know that I am the Lord. I have heard all the insults you spoke against the mountains of Israel, saying: They are desolate; they have been given to us to devour.
text.DRB: And thou shalt know that I the Lord have heard all thy reproaches, that thou hast spoken of the mountains of Israel, saying: They are desert, they are given unto us to devour.

text.NRSV: And you magnified yourselves against me with your mouth, and multiplied your words against me; I heard it.
text.Rotherham: And so ye have magnified yourselves against me with your mouth, And have caused to abound against me your words, —I, have heard!
text.KJV: Thus with your mouth ye have boasted against me, and have multiplied your words against me: I have heard them.
text.NABRE: You boasted against me with your mouths and used insolent words against me. I heard everything!
text.DRB: And you rose up upon me with your mouth, and have derogated your words against me: I heard it.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: As the whole earth rejoices, I will make you desolate.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh,— When all the earth is rejoicing, a desolation, will I make thee:
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord God; When the whole earth rejoiceth, I will make thee desolate.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord God: Because you rejoiced that the whole land was desolate, so I will do to you.
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord God: All the earth rejoicing, I will bring thee into a wilderness.

text.NRSV: As you rejoiced over the inheritance of the house of Israel, because it was desolate, so I will deal with you; you shall be desolate, Mount Seir, and all Edom, all of it. Then they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: As thou didst rejoice over the inheritance of the house of Israel because it had become a desolation, so, will, I, do unto thee,— A desolation, shalt thou become, O Mount Seir And all Edom all of it, So shall they know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: As thou didst rejoice at the inheritance of the house of Israel, because it was desolate, so will I do unto thee: thou shalt be desolate, O mount Seir, and all Idumea, even all of it: and they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: As you rejoiced over the devastation of the heritage of the house of Israel, the same I will do to you: you will become a ruin, Mount Seir, and the whole of Edom, all of it! Then they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.DRB: As thou hast rejoiced upon the inheritance of the house of Israel, because it was dissipated, so will I do to thee: Thou shalt be dissipated mount Seir, & all Idumea: and they shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: And you, mortal, prophesy to the mountains of Israel, and say: O mountains of Israel, hear the word of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thou therefore. Son of man, Prophesy unto the mountains of Israel,—and thou shalt say, Ye mountains of Israel Hear ye the word of Yahweh.
text.KJV: Also, thou son of man, prophesy unto the mountains of Israel, and say, Ye mountains of Israel, hear the word of the Lord:
text.NABRE: As for you, son of man, prophesy to the mountains of Israel and say: Mountains of Israel, hear the word of the Lord!
text.DRB: And thou son of man, Prophesy concerning the mountains of Israel, and thou shalt say: Mountains of Israel hear ye the word of our Lord:

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: Because the enemy said of you, “Aha!” and, “The ancient heights have become our possession,”
text.Rotherham: Thus saith My Lord, Yahweh, Because the enemy hath said concerning you Aha! Even the high places of age-past times, for a possession have become ours
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord God; Because the enemy hath said against you, Aha, even the ancient high places are ours in possession:
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord God: Because the enemy said about you, “Ha! the ancient heights have become our possession,”
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord God: For that the enemy hath said of you: Aha, the everlasting heights are given to us for an inheritance:

text.NRSV: therefore prophesy, and say: Thus says the Lord God: Because they made you desolate indeed, and crushed you from all sides, so that you became the possession of the rest of the nations, and you became an object of gossip and slander among the people;
text.Rotherham: Therefore, prophesy, and thou shalt say, Thus saith My Lord Yahweh-Because even because they have desolated and panted for you on every side That ye should become a possession to the rest of the nations, And ye have come up into the talk of the tongue and the slander of the people
text.KJV: Therefore prophesy and say, Thus saith the Lord God; Because they have made you desolate, and swallowed you up on every side, that ye might be a possession unto the residue of the heathen, and ye are taken up in the lips of talkers, and are an infamy of the people:
text.NABRE: therefore prophesy and say: Thus says the Lord God: because you have been ridiculed and hounded on all sides for becoming a possession for the remaining nations and have become a byword and a popular jeer,
text.DRB: therefore prophesy, & say: Thus saith our Lord God: For that you have been desolate, & trodden down round about, & made an inheritance to the rest of the Gentiles, and have ascended upon the lip of the tongue, & the reproach of the people:

text.NRSV: therefore, O mountains of Israel, hear the word of the Lord God: Thus says the Lord God to the mountains and the hills, the watercourses and the valleys, the desolate wastes and the deserted towns, which have become a source of plunder and an object of derision to the rest of the nations all around;
text.Rotherham: Therefore ye mountains of Israel, hear ye the word of My Lord Yahweh,— Thus saith My Lord, Yahweh, To the mountains and to the hills, to the channels and to the valleys, and to the desolate waste places and to the cities that are forsaken, which have become a prey and a derision, to the rest of the nations which are on every side:
text.KJV: Therefore, ye mountains of Israel, hear the word of the Lord God; Thus saith the Lord God to the mountains, and to the hills, to the rivers, and to the valleys, to the desolate wastes, and to the cities that are forsaken, which became a prey and derision to the residue of the heathen that are round about;
text.NABRE: therefore, mountains of Israel, hear the word of the Lord God: Thus says the Lord God to the mountains and hills, to the ravines and valleys, to the desolate ruins and abandoned cities, plundered and mocked by the nations remaining around you:
text.DRB: Therefore ye mountains of Israel hear the word of our Lord God: Thus saith our Lord God to the mountains, and hills, to the torrents, and valleys, & the deserts, and broken walls, and to the cities forsaken, which are spoiled, & scorned of the rest of the Nations round about.

text.NRSV: therefore thus says the Lord God: I am speaking in my hot jealousy against the rest of the nations, and against all Edom, who, with wholehearted joy and utter contempt, took my land as their possession, because of its pasture, to plunder it.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus, saith My Lord, Yahweh, Surely in the fire of my jealousy, have I spoken against the rest of the nations and against Edom all of it,— Who have given my land to themselves for a possession, In the rejoicing of all the heart In contempt of soul, To make of its produce a prey.
text.KJV: Therefore thus saith the Lord God; Surely in the fire of my jealousy have I spoken against the residue of the heathen, and against all Idumea, which have appointed my land into their possession with the joy of all their heart, with despiteful minds, to cast it out for a prey.
text.NABRE: therefore thus says the Lord God: Truly, with burning jealousy I speak against the remaining nations and against Edom; they all took possession of my land for plunder with wholehearted joy and utter contempt.
text.DRB: Therefore thus saith our Lord God: Because in fire of my zeal I have spoken of the rest of the nations, & of all Idumea, which have given my land to themselves for an inheritance with joy, and with all their heart, and with the mind: and have cast it forth to waste it:

text.NRSV: Therefore prophesy concerning the land of Israel, and say to the mountains and hills, to the watercourses and valleys, Thus says the Lord God: I am speaking in my jealous wrath, because you have suffered the insults of the nations;
text.Rotherham: Therefore prophesy, concerning the soil of Israel,—and say to the mountains and to the hills to the channels and to the valleys, Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Behold me! In my jealousy and in mine indignation, have I spoken, Because the insult of the nations, ye have borne;
text.KJV: Prophesy therefore concerning the land of Israel, and say unto the mountains, and to the hills, to the rivers, and to the valleys, Thus saith the Lord God; Behold, I have spoken in my jealousy and in my fury, because ye have borne the shame of the heathen:
text.NABRE: Therefore, prophesy concerning the land of Israel and say to the mountains and hills, to the ravines and valleys: Thus says the Lord God: See! in my jealous fury I speak, because you endured the reproach of the nations.
text.DRB: therefore prophesy concerning the ground of Israel, and thou shalt say to the mountains, and hills, to the hill-tops, & valleys: Thus saith our Lord God: Behold I have spoken in my zeal, & in my fury because you have sustained the confusion of the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: therefore thus says the Lord God: I swear that the nations that are all around you shall themselves suffer insults.
text.Rotherham: Therefore Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, I myself have lifted my hand,- Surely the nations which are yours round about, they their own shame, shall bear:
text.KJV: Therefore thus saith the Lord God; I have lifted up mine hand, Surely the heathen that are about you, they shall bear their shame.
text.NABRE: Therefore, thus says the Lord God: I raise my hand and swear: the nations around you shall bear their own reproach.
text.DRB: Therefore thus saith our Lord God: I have lifted my hand, that the Gentiles which are round about you, they may bear their confusion.

text.NRSV: But you, O mountains of Israel, shall shoot out your branches, and yield your fruit to my people Israel; for they shall soon come home.
text.Rotherham: But ye O mountains of Israel Your boughs, shall ye shoot forth, And your fruit, shall ye bear For my people Israel, For they have drawn near to enter.
text.KJV: But ye, O mountains of Israel, ye shall shoot forth your branches, and yield your fruit to my people of Israel; for they are at hand to come.
text.NABRE: But you, mountains of Israel, you will sprout branches and bear fruit for my people Israel, for they are coming soon.
text.DRB: But you o mountains of Israel may shoot forth your boughs, and bring forth your fruit to my people of Israel: for he is at hand to come.

text.NRSV: See now, I am for you; I will turn to you, and you shall be tilled and sown;
text.Rotherham: For lo! I am towards you, Therefore will I turn towards you, And ye shall be tilled and sown;
text.KJV: For, behold, I am for you, and I will turn unto you, and ye shall be tilled and sown:
text.NABRE: Look! I am for you! I will turn my face toward you; you will be plowed and planted.
text.DRB: Because lo I to you, and I will turn to you, and you shall be ploughed, and shall take seed.

text.NRSV: and I will multiply your population, the whole house of Israel, all of it; the towns shall be inhabited and the waste places rebuilt;
text.Rotherham: And I will multiply, upon you—men, All the house of Israel All of it, Then shall the cities be inhabited, And the waste places, shall be built,
text.KJV: And I will multiply men upon you, all the house of Israel, even all of it: and the cities shall be inhabited, and the wastes shall be builded:
text.NABRE: Upon you I will multiply the whole house of Israel; cities shall be resettled and ruins rebuilt.
text.DRB: And I will multiply in you men, and all the house of Israel. And the cities shall be inhabited, & the ruinous places shall be repaired.

text.NRSV: and I will multiply human beings and animals upon you. They shall increase and be fruitful; and I will cause you to be inhabited as in your former times, and will do more good to you than ever before. Then you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And I will multiply upon you—man and beast, And they shall multiply and be fruitful,— And I will cause you to be inhabited as in your former estates. And will do you more good than at your beginnings, So shall ye know that am Yahweh;
text.KJV: And I will multiply upon you man and beast; and they shall increase and bring fruit: and I will settle you after your old estates, and will do better unto you than at your beginnings: and ye shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: Upon you I will multiply people and animals so they can multiply and be fruitful. I will resettle you as in the past, and make you more prosperous than at your beginning; then you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.DRB: And I will replenish you with men, and with beasts: and they shall be multiplied, and increase: and I will make you dwell as from the beginning, and will endue you with greater gifts, than you have had from the beginning: and you shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: I will lead people upon you—my people Israel—and they shall possess you, and you shall be their inheritance. No longer shall you bereave them of children.
text.Rotherham: And I will bring upon you—men even my people Israel That they may possess thee, So shalt thou be theirs, as an inheritance, -And thou shalt no more again make them childless.
text.KJV: Yea, I will cause men to walk upon you, even my people Israel; and they shall possess thee, and thou shalt be their inheritance, and thou shalt no more henceforth bereave them of men.
text.NABRE: Upon you I will have them walk, my people Israel. They shall possess you, and you shall be their heritage. Never again shall you rob them of their children.
text.DRB: And I will bring men upon you, my people Israel, and they shall possess thee by inheritance: and thou shalt be for an inheritance to them, & thou shalt add no more to be without them.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: Because they say to you, “You devour people, and you bereave your nation of children,”
text.Rotherham: Thus saith My Lord, Yahweh, Because they are saying to you. A devourer of men, thou art, And one that didst make childless thy nations, hast thou been
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord God; Because they say unto you, Thou land devourest up men, and hast bereaved thy nations;
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord God: Because they say of you, “You devour your own people, you rob your nation of its children,”
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord God: For that they say of you: A devourer of men thou art, and one that doest suffocate thy nation.

text.NRSV: therefore you shall no longer devour people and no longer bereave your nation of children, says the Lord God;
text.Rotherham: Therefore, men, shalt thou not devour any more,- And thy nations, shalt thou make childless no more, Declareth My Lord Yahweh;
text.KJV: Therefore thou shalt devour men no more, neither bereave thy nations any more, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: therefore, you shall never again devour your people or rob your nation of its children—oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: Therefore thou shalt eat men no more, and thy nation thou shalt kill no more, saith our Lord God:

text.NRSV: and no longer will I let you hear the insults of the nations, no longer shall you bear the disgrace of the peoples; and no longer shall you cause your nation to stumble, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: And I will not suffer to be heard against thee any more, the insult of the nations, And the reproach of the peoples, shalt thou not bear any more,- And thy nations, shalt thou not cause to stumble any more, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: Neither will I cause men to hear in thee the shame of the heathen any more, neither shalt thou bear the reproach of the people any more, neither shalt thou cause thy nations to fall any more, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: I will no longer make you listen to the reproach of nations. You will never again endure insults from the peoples. Never again shall you rob your nation of its children—oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: neither will I make the confusion of the Gentiles, to be heard in thee any more, and the reproach of the people thou shalt not bear, & thy nation thou shalt lose no more, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me saying:
text.KJV: Moreover the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: Regeneration of the People. The word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, when the house of Israel lived on their own soil, they defiled it with their ways and their deeds; their conduct in my sight was like the uncleanness of a woman in her menstrual period.
text.Rotherham: Son of man The house of Israel were dwelling upon their own soil, But they defiled it by their way, and by their doings. Like the defilement of her that is removed, became their way before me.
text.KJV: Son of man, when the house of Israel dwelt in their own land, they defiled it by their own way and by their doings: their way was before me as the uncleanness of a removed woman.
text.NABRE: Son of man, when the house of Israel lived in its land, they defiled it with their behavior and their deeds. In my sight their behavior was like the impurity of a woman in menstruation.
text.DRB: Son of man, the house of Israel dwelt in their ground, and polluted it in their ways, & in their studies, according to the uncleanness of a menstruous woman was their way made before me.

text.NRSV: So I poured out my wrath upon them for the blood that they had shed upon the land, and for the idols with which they had defiled it.
text.Rotherham: So I poured out mine indignation upon them, Because of the blood which they had shed upon the land,- Yea with their manufactured gods, had they defiled it.
text.KJV: Wherefore I poured my fury upon them for the blood that they had shed upon the land, and for their idols wherewith they had polluted it:
text.NABRE: So I poured out my fury upon them for the blood they poured out on the ground and for the idols with which they defiled it.
text.DRB: And I have poured out mine indignation upon them for the blood, which they have shed upon the land, & in their idols have polluted it.

text.NRSV: I scattered them among the nations, and they were dispersed through the countries; in accordance with their conduct and their deeds I judged them.
text.Rotherham: So I dispersed them among the nations, And they were scattered throughout the lands,— According to their way and according to their doings, I judged them.
text.KJV: And I scattered them among the heathen, and they were dispersed through the countries: according to their way and according to their doings I judged them.
text.NABRE: I scattered them among the nations, and they were dispersed through other lands; according to their behavior and their deeds I carried out judgment against them.
text.DRB: And I have dispersed them into the Gentiles, & they are scattered into the lands: according to their ways, & their inventions have I judged them

text.NRSV: But when they came to the nations, wherever they came, they profaned my holy name, in that it was said of them, “These are the people of the Lord, and yet they had to go out of his land.”
text.Rotherham: But when they entered among the nations where they did enter, then profaned they my holy Name,—in that it was said of them The people of Yahweh, these! Yet from his land, have they come forth!
text.KJV: And when they entered unto the heathen, whither they went, they profaned my holy name, when they said to them, These are the people of the Lord, and are gone forth out of his land.
text.NABRE: But when they came to the nations, where they went, they desecrated my holy name, for people said of them: “These are the people of the Lord, yet they had to leave their land.”
text.DRB: And they went in to the Nations, unto which they entered, and have polluted my holy name, when it was said of them: This is the people of the Lord, and out of his land they are come forth.

text.NRSV: But I had concern for my holy name, which the house of Israel had profaned among the nations to which they came.
text.Rotherham: So then I had tender regard for my holy Name,- Which the house of Israel had profaned, among the nations, where they had entered.
text.KJV: But I had pity for mine holy name, which the house of Israel had profaned among the heathen, whither they went.
text.NABRE: So I relented because of my holy name which the house of Israel desecrated among the nations to which they came.
text.DRB: And I have spared my holy name, which the house of Israel polluted among the Gentiles, to which they entered in.

text.NRSV: Therefore say to the house of Israel, Thus says the Lord God: It is not for your sake, O house of Israel, that I am about to act, but for the sake of my holy name, which you have profaned among the nations to which you came.
text.Rotherham: Therefore say thou to the house of Israel Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, Not for your sakes, am I working. O house of Israel, But for my holy Name which ye have profaned, among the nations where ye have entered;
text.KJV: Therefore say unto the house of Israel, Thus saith the Lord God; I do not this for your sakes, O house of Israel, but for mine holy name’s sake, which ye have profaned among the heathen, whither ye went.
text.NABRE: Therefore say to the house of Israel: Thus says the Lord God: Not for your sake do I act, house of Israel, but for the sake of my holy name, which you desecrated among the nations to which you came.
text.DRB: Therefore thou shalt say to the house of Israel: Thus saith our Lord God, not for your sake will I do it, o house of Israel, but for my holy name which you have polluted in the Nations to which you entered.

text.NRSV: I will sanctify my great name, which has been profaned among the nations, and which you have profaned among them; and the nations shall know that I am the Lord, says the Lord God, when through you I display my holiness before their eyes.
text.Rotherham: Therefore will I hallow my great Name, that hath been profaned among the nations, which ye have profaned in their midst,-So shall the nations know that I am Yahweh Declareth My Lord, Yahweh, When I have hallowed myself in you, before their eyes.
text.KJV: And I will sanctify my great name, which was profaned among the heathen, which ye have profaned in the midst of them; and the heathen shall know that I am the Lord, saith the Lord God, when I shall be sanctified in you before their eyes.
text.NABRE: But I will show the holiness of my great name, desecrated among the nations, in whose midst you desecrated it. Then the nations shall know that I am the LORD—oracle of the Lord GOD—when through you I show my holiness before their very eyes.
text.DRB: And I will sanctify my great name, that is polluted among the Gentiles, which you have polluted in the midst of them: that the Gentiles may know that I am the Lord, saith our Lord of hosts, when I shall be sanctified in you before them.

text.NRSV: I will take you from the nations, and gather you from all the countries, and bring you into your own land.
text.Rotherham: Therefore will I take you from among the nations, And gather you out of all the lands,- And will bring you upon your own soil;
text.KJV: For I will take you from among the heathen, and gather you out of all countries, and will bring you into your own land.
text.NABRE: I will take you away from among the nations, gather you from all the lands, and bring you back to your own soil.
text.DRB: For I will take you out of the Gentiles, & will gather you together out of all the lands, & will bring you into your land.

text.NRSV: I will sprinkle clean water upon you, and you shall be clean from all your uncleannesses, and from all your idols I will cleanse you.
text.Rotherham: And I will throw upon you clean water and ye shall be clean,— From all your uncleannesses and from all your manufactured gods, will I cleanse you;
text.KJV: Then will I sprinkle clean water upon you, and ye shall be clean: from all your filthiness, and from all your idols, will I cleanse you.
text.NABRE: I will sprinkle clean water over you to make you clean; from all your impurities and from all your idols I will cleanse you.
text.DRB: And I will pour out upon you clean water, and you shall be cleansed from all your contaminations, and from all your idols will I cleanse you.

text.NRSV: A new heart I will give you, and a new spirit I will put within you; and I will remove from your body the heart of stone and give you a heart of flesh.
text.Rotherham: And I will give you a new heart, And a new spirit, will I put within you,-And I will take away the heart of stone out of your flesh, And will give you a heart of flesh;
text.KJV: A new heart also will I give you, and a new spirit will I put within you: and I will take away the stony heart out of your flesh, and I will give you an heart of flesh.
text.NABRE: I will give you a new heart, and a new spirit I will put within you. I will remove the heart of stone from your flesh and give you a heart of flesh.
text.DRB: And I will give you a new heart, and will put a new spirit in the midst of you: and will take away the stony heart out of your flesh, and will give you a fleshy heart.

text.NRSV: I will put my spirit within you, and make you follow my statutes and be careful to observe my ordinances.
text.Rotherham: And my spirit, will I put within you, And will cause That in my statutes, ye shall walk, And my regulations, ye shall observe, and do;
text.KJV: And I will put my spirit within you, and cause you to walk in my statutes, and ye shall keep my judgments, and do them.
text.NABRE: I will put my spirit within you so that you walk in my statutes, observe my ordinances, and keep them.
text.DRB: And I will put my spirit in the midst of you: and I will make that you walk in my precepts, and keep my judgements, and do them.

text.NRSV: Then you shall live in the land that I gave to your ancestors; and you shall be my people, and I will be your God.
text.Rotherham: And ye shall dwell in the land which I gave to your fathers,- And ye shall become my people, And I will become your God;
text.KJV: And ye shall dwell in the land that I gave to your fathers; and ye shall be my people, and I will be your God.
text.NABRE: You will live in the land I gave to your ancestors; you will be my people, and I will be your God.
text.DRB: And you shall dwell in the land: which I gave to your fathers, and you shall be my people, and I will be your God.

text.NRSV: I will save you from all your uncleannesses, and I will summon the grain and make it abundant and lay no famine upon you.
text.Rotherham: And I will save you out of all your uncleannesses, —And will call for the corn and will increase it, And will not lay upon you famine; And I will save you out of all your uncleannesses, —And will call for the corn and will increase it, And will not lay upon you famine;
text.KJV: I will also save you from all your uncleannesses: and I will call for the corn, and will increase it, and lay no famine upon you.
text.NABRE: I will deliver you from all your impurities. I will summon the grain and make it plentiful; I will not send famine against you.
text.DRB: And I will save you from all your contaminations: and I will call for corn, and will multiply it, and will not put famine upon you.

text.NRSV: I will make the fruit of the tree and the produce of the field abundant, so that you may never again suffer the disgrace of famine among the nations.
text.Rotherham: And I will multiply The fruit of the tree, and The increase of the field,— To the end that ye may not receive any more the reproach of famine among the nations.
text.KJV: And I will multiply the fruit of the tree, and the increase of the field, that ye shall receive no more reproach of famine among the heathen.
text.NABRE: I will increase the fruit on your trees and the crops in your fields so that you no longer endure reproach from the nations because of famine.
text.DRB: And I will multiply the fruit of the tree, and the offsprings of the field, that you bear no more the reproach of famine among the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: Then you shall remember your evil ways, and your dealings that were not good; and you shall loathe yourselves for your iniquities and your abominable deeds.
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye call to mind Your ways that were wicked, and Your doings that were not good,- And shall become loathsome in your own sight. For your iniquities and For your abominations.
text.KJV: Then shall ye remember your own evil ways, and your doings that were not good, and shall lothe yourselves in your own sight for your iniquities and for your abominations.
text.NABRE: Then you will remember your evil behavior and your deeds that were not good; you will loathe yourselves for your sins and your abominations.
text.DRB: And you shall remember your most wicked ways, & your studies not good: & your iniquities, & your wicked deeds shall displease you.

text.NRSV: It is not for your sake that I will act, says the Lord God; let that be known to you. Be ashamed and dismayed for your ways, O house of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Not for your sakes, am I working, Declareth My Lord Yahweh be it known to you,— Turn ye pale and then blush for your ways. O house of Israel.
text.KJV: Not for your sakes do I this, saith the Lord God, be it known unto you: be ashamed and confounded for your own ways, O house of Israel.
text.NABRE: Not for your sake do I act—oracle of the Lord God. Let this be known to you! Be ashamed and humbled because of your behavior, house of Israel.
text.DRB: Not for you will I do it, saith our Lord God, be it known to you: be ye confounded, & ashamed upon your ways, o house of Israel.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: On the day that I cleanse you from all your iniquities, I will cause the towns to be inhabited, and the waste places shall be rebuilt.
text.Rotherham: Thus saith My Lord, Yahweh, In the day when I cleanse you, from all your iniquities, Then will I cause the cities to be inhabited, And the waste places shall be built;
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord God; In the day that I shall have cleansed you from all your iniquities I will also cause you to dwell in the cities, and the wastes shall be builded.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord God: When I cleanse you of all your guilt, I will resettle the cities and the ruins will be rebuilt.
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord God: In the day that I shall cleanse you from all your iniquities, & shall make the cities to be inhabited, & shall repair the ruinous places.

text.NRSV: The land that was desolate shall be tilled, instead of being the desolation that it was in the sight of all who passed by.
text.Rotherham: And the land that was made desolate shall be tilled,— Whereas it was a desolation, before the eyes of every passer-by;
text.KJV: And the desolate land shall be tilled, whereas it lay desolate in the sight of all that passed by.
text.NABRE: The desolate land will be tilled—once a wasteland in the eyes of every passerby.
text.DRB: & the desert land shall be tilled, which once was desolate in the eyes of every wayfaring man,

text.NRSV: And they will say, “This land that was desolate has become like the garden of Eden; and the waste and desolate and ruined towns are now inhabited and fortified.”
text.Rotherham: And they shall say, This land that was made desolate hath become like the garden of Eden,- And the cities that were waste and deserted and thrown down, now fenced, are inhabited;
text.KJV: And they shall say, This land that was desolate is become like the garden of Eden; and the waste and desolate and ruined cities are become fenced, and are inhabited.
text.NABRE: They will say, “This once-desolate land has become like the garden of Eden. The cities once ruined, laid waste and destroyed, are now resettled and fortified.”
text.DRB: they shall say: This land untilled is become as a garden of pleasure: and the desert cities, and destitute and undermined, have sitten fenced.

text.NRSV: Then the nations that are left all around you shall know that I, the Lord, have rebuilt the ruined places, and replanted that which was desolate; I, the Lord, have spoken, and I will do it.
text.Rotherham: So shall the nations that may be left remaining round about you know that, I Yahweh— Have built that which was thrown down, and Have planted that which was made desolate,— I Yahweh, have spoken and performed.
text.KJV: Then the heathen that are left round about you shall know that I the Lord build the ruined places, and plant that that was desolate: I the Lord have spoken it, and I will do it.
text.NABRE: Then the surrounding nations that remain shall know that I, the Lord, have rebuilt what was destroyed and replanted what was desolate. I, the Lord, have spoken: I will do it.
text.DRB: And the Nations whatsoever shall be left round about you, shall know that I the Lord have builded the destroyed things, & planted the untilled places, that I the Lord have spoken, and done it.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: I will also let the house of Israel ask me to do this for them: to increase their population like a flock.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Even yet—for this, will I be prevailed upon by the house of Israel to work for them,— I will multiply them-Like a flock—with men:
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord God; I will yet for this be enquired of by the house of Israel, to do it for them; I will increase them with men like a flock.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord God: This also I will be persuaded to do for the house of Israel: to multiply them like sheep.
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord God: As yet in this shall the house of Israel find me, that I will do for them: I will multiply them as a flock of men,

text.NRSV: Like the flock for sacrifices, like the flock at Jerusalem during her appointed festivals, so shall the ruined towns be filled with flocks of people. Then they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Like a flock of holy offerings Like the flock of Jerusalem in her appointed feasts, So, shall the waste cities be filled with flocks of men,- And they shall know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: As the holy flock, as the flock of Jerusalem in her solemn feasts; so shall the waste cities be filled with flocks of men: and they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: Like sheep for sacrifice, like the sheep of Jerusalem on its feast days, the ruined cities shall be filled with flocks of people; then they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.DRB: as a holy flock, as the flock of Jerusalem in the solemnities thereof: So shall the desert cities be full of flocks of men: and they shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: The hand of the Lord came upon me, and he brought me out by the spirit of the Lord and set me down in the middle of a valley; it was full of bones.
text.Rotherham: The hand of Yahweh, being upon me, he carried me forth in the spirit of Yahweh, and set me down in the midst of a plain,—and the same was full of bones;
text.KJV: The hand of the Lord was upon me, and carried me out in the spirit of the Lord, and set me down in the midst of the valley which was full of bones,
text.NABRE: The hand of the Lord came upon me, and he led me out in the spirit of the Lord and set me in the center of the broad valley. It was filled with bones.
text.DRB: The hand of our Lord was made upon me, and brought me forth in the spirit of our Lord: and left me in the midst of a field, that was full of bones.

text.NRSV: He led me all around them; there were very many lying in the valley, and they were very dry.
text.Rotherham: and he caused me to pass near them. round about on every side,—and lo! they were very many on the face of the plain, and lo! they were very dry.
text.KJV: And caused me to pass by them round about: and, behold, there were very many in the open valley; and, lo, they were very dry.
text.NABRE: He made me walk among them in every direction. So many lay on the surface of the valley! How dry they were!
text.DRB: And he led me about through them on every side: and there were very many upon the face of the field, and exceeding dry.

text.NRSV: He said to me, “Mortal, can these bones live?” I answered, “O Lord God, you know.”
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me, Son of man. Can these bones, live? And I said My Lord Yahweh, thou, knowest!
text.KJV: And he said unto me, Son of man, can these bones live? And I answered, O Lord God, thou knowest.
text.NABRE: He asked me: Son of man, can these bones come back to life? “Lord God,” I answered, “you alone know that.”
text.DRB: And he said to me: Son of man, thinkest thou these bones shall live? And I said, Lord God, thou knowest.

text.NRSV: Then he said to me, “Prophesy to these bones, and say to them: O dry bones, hear the word of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me, Prophesy, over these bones,—and thou shalt say unto them, Ye bones so dry! hear ye the word of Yahweh!
text.KJV: Again he said unto me, Prophesy upon these bones, and say unto them, O ye dry bones, hear the word of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Then he said to me: Prophesy over these bones, and say to them: Dry bones, hear the word of the Lord!
text.DRB: And he said to me: Prophesy of these bones: and thou shalt say to them: Dry bones hear ye the word of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God to these bones: I will cause breath to enter you, and you shall live.
text.Rotherham: Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, Unto these bones,— Lo! I am about to bring into you— spirit, and ye shall live;
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord God unto these bones; Behold, I will cause breath to enter into you, and ye shall live:
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord God to these bones: Listen! I will make breath enter you so you may come to life.
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord God to these bones: Behold I will put spirit into you, and you shall live.

text.NRSV: I will lay sinews on you, and will cause flesh to come upon you, and cover you with skin, and put breath in you, and you shall live; and you shall know that I am the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: Yea I will lay upon you—sinews, And bring up over you flesh. And cover over you—skin, And put in you—spirit And ye shall live, Then shall ye know that I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: And I will lay sinews upon you, and will bring up flesh upon you, and cover you with skin, and put breath in you, and ye shall live; and ye shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: I will put sinews on you, make flesh grow over you, cover you with skin, and put breath into you so you may come to life. Then you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.DRB: And I will give sinews upon you, and will make flesh to grow up over you, and will stretch a skin on you: and I will give you spirit, and you shall live, and you shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: So I prophesied as I had been commanded; and as I prophesied, suddenly there was a noise, a rattling, and the bones came together, bone to its bone.
text.Rotherham: And when I prophesied as I was commanded, then was there a noise, as I prophesied and lo! a rattling, and the bones came near, each bone unto its own bone.
text.KJV: So I prophesied as I was commanded: and as I prophesied, there was a noise, and behold a shaking, and the bones came together, bone to his bone.
text.NABRE: I prophesied as I had been commanded. A sound started up, as I was prophesying, rattling like thunder. The bones came together, bone joining to bone.
text.DRB: And I prophesied as he had commanded me: and there was made a sound when I prophesied, and behold a commotion: and bones came to bones, every one to his juncture.

text.NRSV: I looked, and there were sinews on them, and flesh had come upon them, and skin had covered them; but there was no breath in them.
text.Rotherham: And when I looked, then lo! upon them were sinews, and flesh had come up, and there had spread over them skin above, but spirit, was there none within them.
text.KJV: And when I beheld, lo, the sinews and the flesh came up upon them, and the skin covered them above: but there was no breath in them.
text.NABRE: As I watched, sinews appeared on them, flesh grew over them, skin covered them on top, but there was no breath in them.
text.DRB: And I saw, and behold upon them sinews, and flesh was grown up: and a skin was stretched out in them above, and they had no spirit.

text.NRSV: Then he said to me, “Prophesy to the breath, prophesy, mortal, and say to the breath: Thus says the Lord God: Come from the four winds, O breath, and breathe upon these slain, that they may live.”
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me, Prophesy unto the spirit, Prophesy, Son of man. and thou shalt say unto the spirit. Thus saith My Lord Yahweh- From the four winds, come thou, O spirit, And breathe into these slain That they may live.
text.KJV: Then said he unto me, Prophesy unto the wind, prophesy, son of man, and say to the wind, Thus saith the Lord God; Come from the four winds, O breath, and breathe upon these slain, that they may live.
text.NABRE: Then he said to me: Prophesy to the breath, prophesy, son of man! Say to the breath: Thus says the Lord God: From the four winds come, O breath, and breathe into these slain that they may come to life.
text.DRB: And he said to me: prophesy to the spirit, prophesy son of man, & thou shalt say to the spirit: Thus saith our Lord God: Come spirit from the four winds, and blow upon these slain, and let them be revived.

text.NRSV: I prophesied as he commanded me, and the breath came into them, and they lived, and stood on their feet, a vast multitude.
text.Rotherham: And when I prophesied as he commanded me, then came into them the spirit, and they lived and stood upon their feet, an exceeding great army.
text.KJV: So I prophesied as he commanded me, and the breath came into them, and they lived, and stood up upon their feet, an exceeding great army.
text.NABRE: I prophesied as he commanded me, and the breath entered them; they came to life and stood on their feet, a vast army.
text.DRB: And I prophesied as he had commanded me: and spirit entered into them, and they lived: and they stood upon their feet, an army passing great.

text.NRSV: Then he said to me, “Mortal, these bones are the whole house of Israel. They say, ‘Our bones are dried up, and our hope is lost; we are cut off completely.’
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me, Son of man. These bones, are all the house of Israel,— Lo! they are saying. Dried are our bones, and lost is our hope We are quite cut off!
text.KJV: Then he said unto me, Son of man, these bones are the whole house of Israel: behold, they say, Our bones are dried, and our hope is lost: we are cut off for our parts.
text.NABRE: He said to me: Son of man, these bones are the whole house of Israel! They are saying, “Our bones are dried up, our hope is lost, and we are cut off.”
text.DRB: And he said to me: Son of man: All these bones, are the house of Israel: They say: our bones are withered, our hope is perished, and we are cut off.

text.NRSV: Therefore prophesy, and say to them, Thus says the Lord God: I am going to open your graves, and bring you up from your graves, O my people; and I will bring you back to the land of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Therefore prophesy and thou shalt say unto them Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, Lo I myself am going to open your graves And will cause you to come up out of your graves. O my people,— And will bring you upon the soil of Israel.
text.KJV: Therefore prophesy and say unto them, Thus saith the Lord God; Behold, O my people, I will open your graves, and cause you to come up out of your graves, and bring you into the land of Israel.
text.NABRE: Therefore, prophesy and say to them: Thus says the Lord God: Look! I am going to open your graves; I will make you come up out of your graves, my people, and bring you back to the land of Israel.
text.DRB: Therefore prophesy, and thou shalt say to them: Thus saith our Lord God: Behold I will open your graves, and will bring you out of your sepulchres o my people: and will bring you into the land of Israel.

text.NRSV: And you shall know that I am the Lord, when I open your graves, and bring you up from your graves, O my people.
text.Rotherham: So shall ye know that I, am Yahweh,— When I open your graves. And cause you to come up out of your graves O my people,
text.KJV: And ye shall know that I am the Lord, when I have opened your graves, O my people, and brought you up out of your graves,
text.NABRE: You shall know that I am the Lord, when I open your graves and make you come up out of them, my people!
text.DRB: And you shall know that I am the Lord, when I shall have opened your sepulchres, & shall have brought you out of your graves o my people:

text.NRSV: I will put my spirit within you, and you shall live, and I will place you on your own soil; then you shall know that I, the Lord, have spoken and will act,” says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And I will put my spirit within you and ye shall live, And I will settle you upon your own soil, So shall ye know that I, Yahweh have spoken and have performed. Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: And shall put my spirit in you, and ye shall live, and I shall place you in your own land: then shall ye know that I the Lord have spoken it, and performed it, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: I will put my spirit in you that you may come to life, and I will settle you in your land. Then you shall know that I am the Lord. I have spoken; I will do it—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: and shall have given my spirit in you, & you shall live, & I shall make you rest upon your ground: & you shall know that I the Lord have spoken & done it, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me saying:
text.KJV: The word of the Lord came again unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: The Two Sticks. Thus the word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, take a stick and write on it, “For Judah, and the Israelites associated with it”; then take another stick and write on it, “For Joseph (the stick of Ephraim) and all the house of Israel associated with it”;
text.Rotherham: Thou therefore Son of man Take thee one stick, and write upon it For Judah, and for the sons of Israel his companions, Then take thou another stick, and write upon it For Joseph the stick of Ephraim, and all the house of Israel his companions.
text.KJV: Moreover, thou son of man, take thee one stick, and write upon it, For Judah, and for the children of Israel his companions: then take another stick, and write upon it, For Joseph, the stick of Ephraim, and for all the house of Israel his companions:
text.NABRE: As for you, son of man, take one stick and write on it, “Judah and those Israelites associated with it.” Then take another stick and write on it: “Joseph, Ephraim’s stick, and the whole house of Israel associated with it.”
text.DRB: And thou son of man, take thee one piece of wood, and write upon it: Of Juda, & of the children of Israel his fellows: & take another piece of wood, & write upon it: Of Joseph the wood of Ephraim, and of all the house of Israel, and of his fellows.

text.NRSV: and join them together into one stick, so that they may become one in your hand.
text.Rotherham: Then bring them near—the one to the other —for thee into one stick,- And they shall become united in thy hand.
text.KJV: And join them one to another into one stick; and they shall become one in thine hand.
text.NABRE: Join the two sticks together so they become one stick in your hand.
text.DRB: And join them, one to the other for thee into one piece of wood, and they shall be into an union in thy hand.

text.NRSV: And when your people say to you, “Will you not show us what you mean by these?”
text.Rotherham: And when the sons of thy people speak unto thee, saying,- Wilt thou not tell us what these things are to thee?
text.KJV: And when the children of thy people shall speak unto thee, saying, Wilt thou not shew us what thou meanest by these?
text.NABRE: When your people ask you, “Will you not tell us what you mean by all this?”
text.DRB: And when the children of thy people shall say to thee speaking: Doest thou not declare unto us what thou meanest by these?

text.NRSV: say to them, Thus says the Lord God: I am about to take the stick of Joseph (which is in the hand of Ephraim) and the tribes of Israel associated with it; and I will put the stick of Judah upon it, and make them one stick, in order that they may be one in my hand.
text.Rotherham: speak unto them Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, Lo! I, myself, am going to take the stick of Joseph. which is in the hand of Ephraim, and the tribes of Israel, his companions,— And I will place them upon it even upon the stick of Judah. And will make of them one stick, And they shall become one in my hand.
text.KJV: Say unto them, Thus saith the Lord God; Behold, I will take the stick of Joseph, which is in the hand of Ephraim, and the tribes of Israel his fellows, and will put them with him, even with the stick of Judah, and make them one stick, and they shall be one in mine hand.
text.NABRE: answer them: Thus says the Lord God: I will take the stick of Joseph, now in Ephraim’s hand, and the tribes of Israel associated with it, and join to it the stick of Judah, making them one stick; they shall become one in my hand.
text.DRB: Thou shalt speak to them: Thus saith our Lord God: Behold I will take the piece of wood of Joseph, which is in the hand of Ephraim, & the tribes of Israel, that are adjoined to him: and I will give them together with the piece of wood of Juda, and will make them into one piece of wood: and they shall be one in his hand.

text.NRSV: When the sticks on which you write are in your hand before their eyes,
text.Rotherham: And the sticks whereon thou shalt write shall remain in thy hand before their eyes.
text.KJV: And the sticks whereon thou writest shall be in thine hand before their eyes.
text.NABRE: The sticks on which you write, you must hold in your hand in their sight.
text.DRB: And the pieces of wood whereupon thou shalt write, in thy hand, shall be before their eyes.

text.NRSV: then say to them, Thus says the Lord God: I will take the people of Israel from the nations among which they have gone, and will gather them from every quarter, and bring them to their own land.
text.Rotherham: Therefore speak thou unto them Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Lo! I myself, am going to take the sons of Israel, from among the nations whither they have gone,— And I will gather them from every side, And will bring them in upon their own soil;
text.KJV: And say unto them, Thus saith the Lord God; Behold, I will take the children of Israel from among the heathen, whither they be gone, and will gather them on every side, and bring them into their own land:
text.NABRE: Say to them: Thus says the Lord God: I will soon take the Israelites from among the nations to which they have gone and gather them from all around to bring them back to their land.
text.DRB: And thou shalt say to them: Thus saith our Lord God: Behold I will take the children of Israel out of the midst of the nations, to which they are gone: and I will gather them together on every side, and will bring them to their ground.

text.NRSV: I will make them one nation in the land, on the mountains of Israel; and one king shall be king over them all. Never again shall they be two nations, and never again shall they be divided into two kingdoms.
text.Rotherham: And will make of them one nation in the land. among the mountains of Israel, And one king, shall they all have for king, —And they shall remain no longer two nations, Nor shall they be divided into two kingdoms any more.
text.KJV: And I will make them one nation in the land upon the mountains of Israel; and one king shall be king to them all: and they shall be no more two nations, neither shall they be divided into two kingdoms any more at all:
text.NABRE: I will make them one nation in the land, upon the mountains of Israel, and there shall be one king for them all. They shall never again be two nations, never again be divided into two kingdoms.
text.DRB: And I will make them into one nation in the land on the mountains of Israel, and there shall be one king ruling over them all: & they shall no more be two nations, neither shall they be divided any more into two kingdoms.

text.NRSV: They shall never again defile themselves with their idols and their detestable things, or with any of their transgressions. I will save them from all the apostasies into which they have fallen, and will cleanse them. Then they shall be my people, and I will be their God.
text.Rotherham: Neither shall they defile themselves any more With their manufactured gods or With their detestable things, or With any of their transgressions,- But I will save them out of all their dwelling- places, wherein they have sinned, And I will cleanse them And they shall become my people, And I, will become their God.
text.KJV: Neither shall they defile themselves any more with their idols, nor with their detestable things, nor with any of their transgressions: but I will save them out of all their dwellingplaces, wherein they have sinned, and will cleanse them: so shall they be my people, and I will be their God.
text.NABRE: No longer shall they defile themselves with their idols, their abominations, and all their transgressions. I will deliver them from all their apostasy through which they sinned. I will cleanse them so that they will be my people, and I will be their God.
text.DRB: Neither shall they be polluted any more in their idols, & their abominations, and all their iniquities, & I will save them out of all the seats, in which they have sinned, & I will cleanse them: & they shall be my people, & I will be their God.

text.NRSV: My servant David shall be king over them; and they shall all have one shepherd. They shall follow my ordinances and be careful to observe my statutes.
text.Rotherham: And my servant David shall be king over them, And one shepherd, shall they all have, And in my regulations, shall they walk, And my statutes, shall they observe and do them.
text.KJV: And David my servant shall be king over them; and they all shall have one shepherd: they shall also walk in my judgments, and observe my statutes, and do them.
text.NABRE: David my servant shall be king over them; they shall all have one shepherd. They shall walk in my ordinances, observe my statutes, and keep them.
text.DRB: And my servant David king over them, & there shall be one Pastor of them all, they shall walk in my judgements, and shall keep my commandments, and shall do them.

text.NRSV: They shall live in the land that I gave to my servant Jacob, in which your ancestors lived; they and their children and their children’s children shall live there forever; and my servant David shall be their prince forever.
text.Rotherham: And they shall dwell upon the land. which I gave to my servant- to Jacob, Wherein your fathers dwelt,— Yea they shall dwell thereupon. They, and their children and their children’s children Unto times age-abiding, And David my servant shall be prince unto them Unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: And they shall dwell in the land that I have given unto Jacob my servant, wherein your fathers have dwelt; and they shall dwell therein, even they, and their children, and their children’s children for ever: and my servant David shall be their prince for ever.
text.NABRE: They shall live on the land I gave to Jacob my servant, the land where their ancestors lived; they shall live on it always, they, their children, and their children’s children, with David my servant as their prince forever.
text.DRB: And they shall dwell upon the land, which I gave to my servant Jacob, wherein your fathers dwelt, and they shall dwell upon it, themselves, & their children, & their children's children, even for ever: & David my servant their Prince for ever.

text.NRSV: I will make a covenant of peace with them; it shall be an everlasting covenant with them; and I will bless them and multiply them, and will set my sanctuary among them forevermore.
text.Rotherham: And I will solemnise to them a covenant of prosperity, A covenant age-abiding, shall it be with them,— And I will place them. And multiply them, And set my sanctuary in the midst of them. Unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: Moreover I will make a covenant of peace with them; it shall be an everlasting covenant with them: and I will place them, and multiply them, and will set my sanctuary in the midst of them for evermore.
text.NABRE: I will make a covenant of peace with them; it shall be an everlasting covenant with them. I will multiply them and put my sanctuary among them forever.
text.DRB: And I will make a league of peace to them, an everlasting covenant shall be to them: and I will found them, & will multiply them, and will give my sanctification in the midst of them for ever.

text.NRSV: My dwelling place shall be with them; and I will be their God, and they shall be my people.
text.Rotherham: And my habitation shall be over them, And I will become their God,- And they, shall become my people,
text.KJV: My tabernacle also shall be with them: yea, I will be their God, and they shall be my people.
text.NABRE: My dwelling shall be with them; I will be their God, and they will be my people.
text.DRB: And my tabernacle shall be in them: & I will be their God, and they shall be my people.

text.NRSV: Then the nations shall know that I the Lord sanctify Israel, when my sanctuary is among them forevermore.
text.Rotherham: So shall the nations know that I, Yahweh, am hallowing Israel,- When my sanctuary is in the midst of them Unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: And the heathen shall know that I the Lord do sanctify Israel, when my sanctuary shall be in the midst of them for evermore.
text.NABRE: Then the nations shall know that I, the Lord, make Israel holy, by putting my sanctuary among them forever.
text.DRB: And the Gentiles shall know that I am the Lord the sanctifier of Israel, when my sanctification shall be in the midst of them for ever.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying:
text.KJV: And the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: Mortal, set your face toward Gog, of the land of Magog, the chief prince of Meshech and Tubal. Prophesy against him
text.Rotherham: Son of man Set thy face against Gog of the land of the Magog, Prince of Rosh Meshech and Tubal,- And prophesy concerning him,
text.KJV: Son of man, set thy face against Gog, the land of Magog, the chief prince of Meshech and Tubal, and prophesy against him,
text.NABRE: Son of man, turn your face against Gog of the land of Magog, the chief prince of Meshech and Tubal, and prophesy against him.
text.DRB: Son of man, set thy face against Gog, and the Land of Magog, the Prince of the head of Mosoch, and Thubal: and prophesy of him and thou shalt say to him: Thus saith our Lord God: Behold I to thee Gog Prince of the head of Mosoch and Thubal.

text.NRSV: and say: Thus says the Lord God: I am against you, O Gog, chief prince of Meshech and Tubal;
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt say, Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh,— Behold me! against thee O Gog, Prince of Rosh, Meshech and Tubal;
text.KJV: And say, Thus saith the Lord God; Behold, I am against thee, O Gog, the chief prince of Meshech and Tubal:
text.NABRE: Say: Thus says the Lord God: See! I am coming against you, Gog, chief prince of Meshech and Tubal.
text.DRB: And I will turn thee about, and will put a bit in thy jaws: and will bring forth thee, and all thine army, the horses and horsemen clothed with brigantines,

text.NRSV: I will turn you around and put hooks into your jaws, and I will lead you out with all your army, horses and horsemen, all of them clothed in full armor, a great company, all of them with shield and buckler, wielding swords.
text.Rotherham: Therefore I will turn thee about, and will put hooks in thy jaws,—and bring thee forth and all thine army. Horses and horsemen Clothed with gorgeous attire, all of them, A mighty gathered host With shield and buckler handling swords, all of them:
